situation: Four located optics claims were machine-verified: critical angle glass(1.5)->air = 41.8 deg, water(1.33)->air = 48.8 deg, optical-fiber numerical aperture (core 1.50 / cladding 1.48) = 0.244, and the photon energy of 500 nm green light = 2.48 eV. The false claim 'glass->air critical angle is 30 degrees' was caught and BROKEN -- the true value is 41.8 deg.

pre_run.summary: 5 located optics claims through /derivation/solve: 4 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. This tick also EXTENDED the prose bridge so optics is reachable from plain language -- the critical-angle template was generalized beyond fiber (air=1.00, water=1.33, glass=1.50), a numerical-aperture and a Snell's-law example were added, and the oracle is told photon energy is always in joules (eV converted). Before the fix all five returned INCOMPLETE; after, four held and the imposter broke.

domain_results.detail: critical angle (Snell TIR), numerical aperture sqrt(n_core^2-n_clad^2), photon energy hc/lambda -- 4 held; 30-deg critical angle rejected (true 41.8)

wisdom: Light bends and reflects by exact law: the critical angle is arcsin(n_rare/n_dense), a fiber's light-gathering is sqrt(n_core^2 - n_clad^2), a photon's energy is hc over its wavelength. Four located claims held to the floor and survived; the one that said 30 degrees where the law says 41.8 broke on contact. The same engine that checks a prime now checks how light is carried -- and refuses the false number whatever the domain.

title: Located and verified — propositional logic laws (formal_logic)
situation: Four located laws of propositional logic were machine-verified by truth-table evaluation: excluded middle (p OR ~p) is a tautology, non-contradiction (p AND ~p) is a contradiction, modus ponens ((p>>q AND p)>>q) is a tautology, and one of De Morgan's laws (~(p AND q) >> (~p OR ~q)) is a tautology. The false claim 'p implies q is a tautology' was caught and BROKEN -- it is false when p is true and q is false.

pre_run.summary: 5 located logic claims through /derivation/solve: 4 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. The verifier evaluates the full truth table; a 'law' that is not true under every assignment does not pass.

domain_results.detail: excluded middle / non-contradiction / modus ponens / De Morgan held; bare p>>q rejected as non-tautology

wisdom: A law of logic must hold under EVERY assignment of true and false, not merely the convenient ones. Excluded middle, non-contradiction, modus ponens, and De Morgan held under every row; 'p implies q' fails the single row where p is true and q is false, so it is no tautology and the engine refused it. The same floor that checks a prime or a bond angle checks an argument's form: truth is what survives every case.

title: Located and verified — matter waves & fiber telecom (optics)
situation: Three located claims spanning quantum matter-waves and fiber-optic telecom were machine-verified: an electron at 1e6 m/s has de Broglie wavelength 7.27e-10 m (lambda = h/p), a single-mode fiber at 0.2 dB/km over 100 km loses 20 dB, and an 80-channel WDM system at 100 Gbps/channel carries 8000 Gbps. The false claim that the same electron's wavelength is 1e-9 m was caught and BROKEN -- the true value is 7.27e-10 m.

pre_run.summary: 4 located claims through /derivation/solve: 3 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. This tick EXTENDED the prose bridge to reach three optics verifiers that had no cheat-sheet example yet -- de_broglie (matter-wave), fiber_attenuation, and wdm_capacity -- so they are now reachable from plain language. de Broglie lambda=h/p, fiber loss = alpha*length, WDM total = channels*bitrate.

domain_results.detail: de Broglie 7.27e-10 m, fiber 20 dB, WDM 8000 Gbps held; 1e-9 m wavelength rejected (true 7.27e-10)

wisdom: The same light-law floor reaches from the quantum to the continental: a moving electron has a wavelength (h over its momentum), a hair-thin fiber loses only a fifth of a decibel per kilometer so a touch of light crosses a hundred kilometers, and one fiber carries eighty colors at once for eight terabits a second. Three located claims held; the electron-wavelength that was off by a third broke on contact. Matter is wave, and the measure is exact.

title: Located and verified — Pythagorean triples & plane geometry (geometry)
situation: Five located geometry claims were machine-verified: the right triangles 3-4-5, 5-12-13, and 8-15-17 (a^2 + b^2 = c^2), the area of a radius-5 circle (78.54 = pi*r^2), and the validity of a 2-3-4 triangle (triangle inequality). The false claim that 3-4-6 is a right triangle was caught and BROKEN -- 9 + 16 = 25, not 36.

pre_run.summary: 6 located geometry claims through /derivation/solve: 5 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. Pythagorean triples checked by a^2+b^2=c^2, circle area by pi*r^2, triangle validity by the triangle inequality. The engine recomputes the relation; it does not accept the label 'right triangle'.

wisdom: A right triangle is not named so by assertion but by a^2 + b^2 = c^2 holding exactly. Three ancient triples held -- 3-4-5, 5-12-13, 8-15-17 -- and the circle gave back pi times its radius squared; but 3-4-6, which looks near enough, breaks because 9 plus 16 is 25 and not 36. The floor that checks a prime and a bond angle measures a triangle the same way: by the relation it must satisfy, in every case.

title: Located and verified — derivatives & integrals (calculus)
situation: Five located calculus rules were machine-verified by SymPy: d/dx sin(x)=cos(x), d/dx ln(x)=1/x, the integral of cos(x)=sin(x), d/dx e^x=e^x, and d/dx x^4=4x^3. The false claim d/dx cos(x)=sin(x) was caught and BROKEN -- the true derivative is -sin(x).

pre_run.summary: 6 located calculus claims through /derivation/solve: 5 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. Each derivative/integral is recomputed symbolically; the engine checks the claimed expression against the true one, not the label.

domain_results.detail: sin->cos, ln->1/x, int cos->sin, e^x->e^x, x^4->4x^3 held; cos->sin rejected (true -sin x)

wisdom: A derivative is not a matter of opinion: the slope of cos(x) is minus sin(x), exactly, and the engine recomputes it symbolically rather than trusting the table. Five rules held -- the circular pair sin/cos, the logarithm, the exponential that is its own derivative, the power rule -- but the sign-flipped 'd/dx cos = sin' broke, because the truth carries a minus. The same floor that checks a prime, a bond angle, and a right triangle checks the calculus: by recomputation, every time.

title: Located and verified — shell capacities & electron configurations (atomic)
situation: Five located atomic-structure claims were machine-verified: the n=3 shell holds 18 and the n=4 shell holds 32 electrons (2n^2), and the electron configurations of neon (1s2 2s2 2p6), argon (1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p6), and chromium (...3d5 4s1, a Madelung exception). The false claim that the n=3 shell holds 8 electrons was caught and BROKEN -- the true capacity is 18.

pre_run.summary: 6 located atomic claims through /derivation/solve: 5 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. Shell capacity checked by 2n^2; configurations checked against the Madelung filling order WITH the known exceptions (chromium's 3d5 4s1 held). The engine computes the shell and the filling; it does not accept the asserted count.

domain_results.detail: n=3->18, n=4->32; Ne/Ar/Cr configs held (Cr exception included); n=3->8 rejected (true 18)

wisdom: An electron shell holds exactly twice the square of its number -- 2, 8, 18, 32 -- and the engine computes it rather than trusting the count; the n=3 shell holds 18, so the common '8' (the period, not the shell) breaks. Even chromium, which defies the naive filling order to sit at 3d5 4s1, was confirmed because the engine carries the real exceptions. Matter is built in discrete, countable steps, and the floor counts them exactly.

title: Located and verified — standard test statistics (statistics)
situation: Three located inferential-statistics claims were machine-verified by recomputation from the test distribution: a two-sided z of 1.96 gives p~0.05, a chi-squared of 3.841 on 1 df gives p~0.05, and a two-sided z of 2.576 gives p~0.01. The false claim that z=1.96 two-sided gives p~0.5 was caught and BROKEN -- the true p is ~0.05.

pre_run.summary: 4 located statistics claims through /derivation/solve: 3 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. This tick made statistics reachable end-to-end: fixed the derivation collector to read the statistics umbrella result shape ({pvalue/confidence_interval/multiple_comparisons: {...}}), and added the statistics cheat-sheet to the prose bridge. p-values are recomputed from the normal/chi-square distribution, not taken from the claim.

wisdom: A p-value is not a number one may assert -- it is recomputed from the test's own distribution, and a two-sided z of 1.96 yields about five in a hundred, not one in two. Three standard critical values held -- the 1.96 and 2.576 of the normal table, the 3.841 of chi-square on one degree of freedom -- and the inflated p broke on contact. The same floor that checks a prime, a bond angle, and a derivative now weighs evidence under uncertainty: by recomputation, refusing the convenient number.

title: Verified cross-domain chain — the tetrahedral 109.47 deg, two independent witnesses
situation: The tetrahedral angle 109.47 deg is arrived at independently by TWO domains and verified as a LINKED chain: step 0 (coordination geometry) confirms a tetrahedral coordination has central angle 109.47 deg; step 1 (VSEPR molecular geometry) confirms methane (4 bonding domains, 0 lone pairs) is tetrahedral at 109.47 deg, building on step 0. Both CONFIRMED -> the chain HOLDS. A false variant claiming 90 deg for methane BREAKS at the molecular-geometry step -- the cross-domain link is machine-checked, not assumed.

pre_run.summary: Two-witness co-confirmation (Deut 19:15) run through /derivation/verify as a linked chain: geometry and molecular_geometry independently yield 109.47 deg for the same tetrahedral structure (HOLDS, 2/2 confirmed); the 90-deg false link is caught BROKEN. This is the symmetry axis (already named) shown for the first time as a MACHINE-VERIFIED CROSS-DOMAIN TRAIL, not two separate single-domain claims.

domain_results.detail: coordination tetrahedral -> 109.47 deg = arccos(-1/3)
VSEPR 4 bonding/0 lone -> tetrahedral 109.47 deg; builds on the geometry step

wisdom: By the mouth of two witnesses a matter is established. The tetrahedral angle -- arccos of minus one third, 109.47 degrees -- is reached one way by pure coordination geometry and another, wholly independent way, by counting a molecule's bonding and lone pairs; the engine confirms both and links them into one trail. When the link is false (90 degrees), the chain breaks exactly where the falsehood enters. A connection trusted is a connection two independent floors have each measured and the chain has held.

title: Located and verified — dimensional analysis (physics_dimensional)
situation: Three located physics equations were machine-verified as dimensionally consistent by reducing both sides to SI base units: Newton's second law F = m*a (newton = kg*m/s^2), the mass-energy relation E = m*c^2 (joule = kg*(m/s)^2), and the kinematic d = v*t (meter = (m/s)*s). The false claim that force equals mass times velocity (F = m*v) was caught and BROKEN -- newton (kg*m/s^2) does not match kg*m/s.

pre_run.summary: 4 located dimensional-analysis claims through /derivation/solve: 3 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. This tick added physics_dimensional to the prose bridge and fixed an oracle mis-structuring (it had borrowed the mathematics 'set-to-zero' form, dropping the '='; the cheat-sheet now requires a literal '=' with both sides). Each equation's two sides are reduced to kg/m/s/A/K/mol/cd and compared.

domain_results.detail: F=ma, E=mc^2, d=vt dimensionally consistent; F=mv rejected (newton != kg*m/s)

wisdom: An equation that means anything must balance in its units before it can balance in its numbers: force is kilogram-meter per second squared, and any law claiming to give a force must reduce to exactly that. F=ma, E=mc^2, and d=vt each balanced both sides to the same SI base units; F=mv broke because mass times velocity is a momentum, not a force. Before truth in magnitude, truth in dimension -- and the floor checks the dimension first.

title: Located and verified — balanced chemical equations (chemistry)
situation: Three located chemical equations were machine-verified as balanced by checking atom and charge conservation across the arrow: the synthesis of water (2 H2 + O2 -> 2 H2O), the combustion of methane (CH4 + 2 O2 -> CO2 + 2 H2O), and the combustion of glucose (C6H12O6 + 6 O2 -> 6 CO2 + 6 H2O). The false claim that 2 H2 + O2 -> 3 H2O is balanced was caught and BROKEN -- it does not conserve atoms (the correct coefficient is 2 H2O).

pre_run.summary: 4 located chemistry claims through /derivation/solve: 3 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. The verifier counts every element on each side of the arrow (and net charge) and confirms they match under the stated coefficients; an unbalanced equation is rejected and the correct balancing reported.

domain_results.detail: water / methane / glucose combustion balanced; 2 H2 + O2 -> 3 H2O rejected (correct: 2 H2O)

wisdom: A chemical equation is true only when nothing is created or destroyed across the arrow -- every atom that enters must leave, and the charge must balance. Water, the burning of methane, and the burning of glucose each conserved every element on both sides; '2 H2 + O2 -> 3 H2O' broke because six hydrogens cannot come from four. The same floor that weighs a force's dimensions weighs a reaction's atoms: conservation first, and the books must close.

title: Located and verified — musical intervals & equal temperament (music_theory)
situation: Four located music claims were machine-verified: 440 Hz to 880 Hz is an octave (2:1), 660 Hz above 440 Hz is a perfect fifth (3:2), 550 Hz above 440 Hz is a major third (5:4), and middle C (MIDI 60) is about 261.63 Hz in equal temperament. The false claim that 440 Hz and 880 Hz form a perfect fifth was caught and BROKEN -- that ratio is 2:1, an octave.

pre_run.summary: 5 located music claims through /derivation/solve: 4 VERIFIED, 1 BROKEN. This tick added music_theory to the bridge AND fixed a critical integrity flaw: the oracle had silently 'corrected' the false fifth (encoding it as octave) and reported HOLDS -- laundering a falsehood. The bridge now enforces FAITHFULNESS: encode the claim exactly as stated, never correct it; the engine decides truth. After the fix the false fifth correctly BREAKS, the true claims still hold, and numeric-false regressions still break.

domain_results.detail: octave 2:1, fifth 3:2, major third 5:4, middle C 261.63 Hz held; octave-mislabeled-as-fifth rejected

wisdom: The intervals are not conventions but ratios: the octave doubles a frequency, the fifth is three to two, the major third five to four -- creation sings in whole numbers. Four such ratios held against 440 Hz; the claim that a doubling is a fifth broke, because two to one is an octave, not three to two. And a deeper guard was set this day: the engine must judge the claim AS SPOKEN, never quietly mend it -- for a tool that corrects a falsehood before testing it has only flattered the speaker, not served the truth.

title: The Rule of 72 - how fast money and debt double
situation: Divide 72 by a yearly interest rate to get the years until an amount doubles. The engine confirmed the shortcut against exact compound interest: $1,000 at 8%/yr is $1,999 after 9 years (72/8=9); at 12%, $1,974 after 6 years (72/12=6); at 6%, $2,012 after 12 years (72/6=12). A false claim that 8% turns $1,000 into $5,000 in 9 years was caught -- it reaches only about $1,999.
use: To estimate doubling time in your head: years-to-double = 72 / (interest rate). Works for savings, investments, inflation, and debt alike.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom drawn from the verified Atlas (finance verifier, compound interest A=P(1+r/n)^(nt)). Three doubling estimates confirmed against exact compounding; a 5x overclaim rejected. The rule is an approximation but a faithful one near everyday rates.

wisdom: Money you save and debt you owe double on the very same clock. At a card rate near 18%, a balance left alone doubles in about four years (72/18=4); savings at 6% take twelve. Holding the doubling time in your head turns an abstract 'rate' into a real calendar -- how fast a sum works FOR you, and how fast it works AGAINST you. The wise plant for the first and flee the second: 'the borrower is slave to the lender' (Proverbs 22:7). Compound interest is patient; let it serve you and never own you.

title: Reading the thermometer - Celsius and Fahrenheit
situation: To go from Celsius to Fahrenheit: multiply by 9/5 and add 32. The engine confirmed the everyday anchor points: water freezes at 0C = 32F and boils at 100C = 212F; body temperature 37C = 98.6F; a moderate oven 180C = 356F. A false claim that 100C = 200F was caught -- it is 212F.
use: Celsius to Fahrenheit: F = C x 9/5 + 32. Fahrenheit to Celsius: C = (F - 32) x 5/9. Quick mental estimate (good for weather and body heat, drifts high for oven temps): double the Celsius and add 30.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (mathematics verifier, equality mode). Four conversion anchor points confirmed exactly; a false boiling-point conversion rejected. The exact formula is reliable everywhere; the double-and-add-30 shortcut is a within-a-few-degrees estimate for ordinary air and body temperatures only.

domain_results.detail: 0C=32F, 100C=212F, 37C=98.6F, 180C=356F confirmed; 100C=200F rejected

wisdom: Two scales, one reality: the same boiling kettle reads 100 in one language and 212 in the other. Carry a few anchor points and you are never lost -- freezing 0/32, boiling 100/212, fever near 38C/100F, a warm room 20/68, a moderate oven 180/356. For the weather or a child's fever, double the Celsius and add thirty in your head; for the oven, use the real formula, because the quick trick runs hot as the numbers climb. A measure honestly converted is a small daily faithfulness: the thing itself never changed, only the words we read it by.

title: Scaling a recipe - halving and doubling awkward amounts
situation: The kitchen ladder: 1 cup = 16 tablespoons = 48 teaspoons, and 1 tablespoon = 3 teaspoons. The engine confirmed the arithmetic behind the everyday tricks: half of 3/4 cup is 6 tablespoons; a third of a cup is 16 teaspoons; double 2/3 cup is 1 1/3 cups. A false claim that half of 3/4 cup is 4 tablespoons was caught -- it is 6.
use: To halve or double an awkward amount, switch to a smaller unit first. Ladder: 1 cup = 16 Tbsp = 48 tsp; 1 Tbsp = 3 tsp. Halve 3/4 cup -> read it as 12 Tbsp -> take 6 Tbsp. Halve 1/3 cup -> read it as 16 tsp -> take 8 tsp (2 Tbsp + 2 tsp). Double 2/3 cup -> 1 1/3 cups.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (mathematics verifier, equality mode). The conversion ladder and three scaling results confirmed exactly; a false halving rejected. Whole-number teaspoons make fractions of a cup easy to split.

wisdom: The fraction that stumps you in cups becomes plain in teaspoons. Half of three-quarters of a cup looks like a riddle until you read it as twelve tablespoons and simply take six; a third of a cup is sixteen teaspoons, easily halved to eight. Keep the ladder in mind -- a cup is sixteen tablespoons, a tablespoon is three teaspoons -- and no recipe is too odd to scale. 'She looks well to the ways of her household' (Proverbs 31:27): the small, exact care of a measure is part of feeding a family well.

title: Reading a 'study found' headline - when 'significant' is just luck
situation: A result called 'statistically significant' usually means p < 0.05 -- about a 1-in-20 chance of arising by luck alone. So if a study quietly measures many things, some will look significant by chance. The engine took 20 measurements (one at p=0.03, which looks significant on its own) and applied the standard correction for testing 20 things: NONE survived. The claim that the p=0.03 finding is real after correction was caught and BROKEN.
use: When you see a single 'significant' result, ask how many things were measured. Rough rule: testing N things, expect about N/20 to look 'significant' by chance even if nothing is real. Trust a finding only if it survives correction for all the tests run -- or, better, if an independent study repeats it.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (statistics verifier, multiple-comparisons correction). With 20 raw p-values (smallest 0.03), the Bonferroni correction rejects none at alpha 0.05; the claim that the 0.03 finding is significant after correction was rejected.

domain_results.detail: Bonferroni over 20 tests -> rejected set empty; claimed [index 0] significant -> rejected (computed [])

wisdom: 'Significant' is not the same as 'true.' One chance in twenty is the usual bar, so measure twenty things and one will cross it by luck alone -- and a headline that shows you only the winner has hidden the nineteen that lost. Before you rearrange your table or your fears around a single study, ask how many questions it really asked, and whether anyone else has seen the same thing. 'The simple believes everything, but the prudent gives thought to his steps' (Proverbs 14:15).

title: Squaring a corner - the 3-4-5 rule
situation: To make a true right angle without a square: measure 3 units along one edge and 4 along the other, and the straight line between those two marks must be exactly 5. The engine confirmed 3-4-5 and its working multiples 6-8-10, 12-16-20, and the separate triple 5-12-13. A corner measured 3-4-5.5 (diagonal too long) was caught -- it is NOT square.
use: Mark 3 units along one edge and 4 along the other (any unit -- feet, hand-spans -- as long as both are the same). Measure the diagonal between the marks: exactly 5 means the corner is square; longer means the corner is open past 90, shorter means it is closed. Adjust until the diagonal reads 5. Scale up for big jobs: 6-8-10, 9-12-15, or 12-16-20 feet for a deck or foundation.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (geometry verifier, Pythagorean check a^2+b^2=c^2). The 3-4-5 rule and its multiples confirmed; an off-by-half corner (3-4-5.5) rejected as not square. Any whole-number multiple of 3-4-5 squares a corner; the diagonal is the test.

wisdom: The oldest builder's trick needs no tool but a tape: three one way, four the other, and the diagonal must come to five. If it does, the corner is square and every wall, bed, and tile laid from it will run true; if the diagonal runs long or short, the error is small now but grows across the whole job. Check the corner before you build on it. 'Everyone who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock' (Matthew 7:24): a true foundation is worth the few minutes it takes to measure.

title: Baking soda and vinegar - what the fizz really is
situation: Baking soda (a base) and vinegar (an acid) react: NaHCO3 + CH3COOH -> CH3COONa + H2O + CO2 -- a harmless salt (sodium acetate), water, and carbon-dioxide gas (the fizz). The engine confirmed this equation is balanced; a version missing the water was caught as unbalanced. Because the acid and base NEUTRALIZE each other, the mixture's cleaning chemistry largely cancels out -- what is left is mostly salty water once the bubbling stops.
use: Use the FIZZ for what it is -- gentle agitation, not extra chemical power. For a slow drain: pour in baking soda, then vinegar, let it foam ~15 minutes, flush with hot water (the bubbling helps loosen gunk). For real cleaning, use each ALONE: baking soda as a mild scouring base for scrubbing; vinegar as an acid for hard-water spots and soap scum. Do not bottle them pre-mixed expecting a stronger cleaner -- once the fizz dies, both are spent. SAFETY: never mix bleach with vinegar (or with ammonia) -- that makes toxic gas.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (chemistry verifier, atom/charge balance). The baking-soda-and-vinegar equation confirmed balanced; an unbalanced variant rejected. Balance shows the products are a salt + water + CO2 -- evidence the acid and base neutralize, so the combined liquid is not a stronger cleaner.

domain_results.detail: NaHCO3 + CH3COOH -> CH3COONa + H2O + CO2 balanced; missing-water variant rejected

wisdom: The dramatic fizz is mostly theater: an acid and a base meeting and cancelling, leaving salt water and a puff of the same gas we breathe out. It is wonderful for a child's lesson and handy for loosening a drain by sheer bubbling, but it is not a secret super-cleaner -- pre-mixed, the two undo each other. Knowing what is really happening saves the pantry and sets expectations honestly; 'the prudent are crowned with knowledge' (Proverbs 14:18). Use the right single thing for the job, and keep bleach far from both.

title: Which years are leap years - the rule and its trap
situation: A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4 -- EXCEPT century years, which must also be divisible by 400. The engine confirmed it: 2024 is a leap year, 2000 was (divisible by 400), but 1900 was NOT and 2100 will NOT be (centuries not divisible by 400). The common belief that 1900 was a leap year was caught and rejected.
use: To know if February has 29 days: (1) not divisible by 4 -> no. (2) divisible by 4 but is a century year (ends in 00) -> only if also divisible by 400. (3) divisible by 4 and not a century -> yes. So 2024 yes, 2000 yes, 1900 no, 2100 no.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (calendar_time verifier, leap-year rule). Four years confirmed (2024, 2000 leap; 1900, 2100 not); the myth that 1900 was a leap year rejected. The 400-year exception keeps the calendar aligned to the true solar year of about 365.2422 days.

domain_results.detail: 2024 leap, 2000 leap, 1900 not, 2100 not; claimed 1900 leap rejected

wisdom: The simple rule -- every fourth year -- is almost right, and 'almost' is why the calendar once drifted into the wrong season. The true year is not 365 and a quarter days but a touch less, so three times in four hundred years we skip the leap day: that is why 2000 kept it but 1900 and 2100 do not. A small, deliberate correction, repeated faithfully, keeps the whole reckoning true across centuries. 'Let them be for signs and for seasons, and for days and years' (Genesis 1:14): the lights were set in order, and our counting only keeps step with them.

title: A dollar later is worth less than a dollar now
situation: To compare money you would get later against money now, you DISCOUNT the future amount: present value = future amount / (1 + rate)^years. The engine confirmed: $1,100 a year from now, at a 10% rate, is worth exactly $1,000 today; $1,000 in five years at 6% is about $747 today; $10,000 in ten years at 5% is about $6,139 today. The claim that $1,100 a year out is still worth $1,100 today was caught and rejected.
use: Compare offers across time by discounting: present value = future $ / (1 + r)^(years), where r is the rate you could earn (or the rate you pay on debt). Offered $1,000 now or $1,100 in a year: if you can earn more than 10%, take the cash now; less than 10%, take the later money. For a lottery or settlement, the 'lump sum' is smaller than the total of payments precisely because near money is worth more.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (finance verifier, present value PV = FV/(1+r)^t). Three discounting results confirmed; the no-discount error rejected. The same compounding that grows savings (rule of 72) runs backward to value a future sum today.

wisdom: Money has a clock on it: a dollar in hand can work for a year before a promised dollar even arrives, so the promise is worth less today -- and the longer the wait or the higher the rate, the less it is worth. This is why a lump sum is smaller than the sum of the payments, and why 'pay later' quietly costs the seller something they hope to charge back to you. Before you trade present cash for a future promise (or the reverse), discount it honestly and compare. 'Which of you, desiring to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost?' (Luke 14:28).

title: The 12th fret is the octave - tuning and intonation
situation: Fretting a string at the 12th fret halves its vibrating length and doubles its pitch -- exactly one octave (a 2:1 frequency ratio). The engine confirmed it: the A string at 110 Hz becomes 220 Hz; the low E at 82.41 Hz becomes 164.81 Hz; and concert A, the tuning reference, is 440 Hz. The common notion that the 12th fret raises the pitch a fifth was caught and rejected -- it is an octave, not a fifth.
use: Tune to concert A = 440 Hz. To CHECK INTONATION: play the open string, then the note at the 12th fret -- they should be the same note exactly one octave apart and equally in tune on a tuner. If the 12th-fret note reads sharp or flat, the string length needs adjusting at the bridge (move the saddle back if sharp, forward if flat). For tuning by ear, lightly touch the string over the 12th fret to sound the natural octave harmonic.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (music_theory verifier, frequency ratios + equal temperament). The 12th-fret octave (2:1) confirmed on two strings, concert A = 440 Hz confirmed; the 'fifth' mislabel rejected. Halving the string length doubles the frequency -- the most consonant interval there is.

domain_results.detail: 110->220 and 82.41->164.81 are octaves; MIDI 69 = 440 Hz; 12th fret = fifth rejected

wisdom: Halve the string and the pitch doubles -- the octave, the simplest and most settled of all intervals, two to one. That single fact tells you where every note repeats and lets you test whether an instrument is honest with itself: open string and 12th fret must agree, or the intonation lies a little more with every fret you climb. A well-tuned string, like a well-ordered life, is checked against a fixed reference and corrected where it drifts. 'Play skillfully on the strings, with loud shouts of joy' (Psalm 33:3).

title: Reading a food label - where the calories really are
situation: Calories come from grams by fixed factors: carbohydrate and protein give about 4 calories per gram, FAT gives 9, and alcohol 7. The engine confirmed it: 100 g of fat is about 900 calories; a tablespoon of olive oil (14 g fat) about 126; 30 g of protein 120; and a mix of 20 g carb + 5 g protein + 10 g fat is 190. The common underestimate that 10 g of fat is only 40 calories (treating fat like carbs) was caught and rejected -- it is 90.
use: Estimate the calories in a serving: 4 x (carb grams) + 4 x (protein grams) + 9 x (fat grams) [+ 7 x (alcohol grams)]. Because fat carries more than double the calories per gram, added oils and fats are where calories hide: a single tablespoon of oil or butter is roughly 120 calories. To sanity-check a label, multiply the macros and see if it lands near the stated calories.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (nutrition verifier, Atwater factors 4/4/9/7). Four calorie totals confirmed from macro grams; the fat-as-4-cal underestimate rejected. Fat is the calorie-dense macro -- the same grams cost more than twice the carbohydrate.

domain_results.detail: 100g fat=900, 14g fat=126, 30g protein=120, 20C/5P/10F=190 confirmed; 10g fat=40 rejected (it is 90)

wisdom: Calories keep a strict ledger: four for a gram of bread or meat, but nine for a gram of fat -- the oil you barely see in the pan outweighs the bread you can. Knowing the three small numbers lets you read any label and judge a meal without a guess, and it quietly explains why a drizzle 'adds up' faster than a slice. None of this is to fear food, only to handle it with open eyes. 'Have you found honey? Eat only as much as you need, lest you be filled with it and vomit' (Proverbs 25:16): enough, and no waste.

title: Watts = volts x amps - why the breaker trips
situation: Electric power follows one rule: watts = volts x amps. The engine confirmed it: a 1500-watt space heater on 120 volts draws 12.5 amps; a standard 15-amp, 120-volt circuit supplies at most 1800 watts; a 1200-watt microwave draws 10 amps; a 240-volt dryer at 30 amps is 7200 watts. The dangerous claim that a 15-amp, 120-volt circuit can supply 3000 watts was caught and rejected -- its true limit is 1800.
use: Watts = volts x amps. US wall circuits are 120 V; a 15-amp circuit tops out at 120 x 15 = 1800 W, and electrical code says hold a continuous load to 80% of that -- about 1440 W. To see if two things can share a circuit, add their wattage: a 1500 W space heater plus a 1200 W microwave is 2700 W, far over the limit, so it trips the breaker. Big appliances (dryer, oven, AC) use 240 V on their own dedicated circuit. Never defeat a breaker or fuse -- it is sized to protect the wire from fire.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (electrical verifier, P = V x I). Four power facts confirmed across 120 V and 240 V; the 3000 W overestimate for a 15 A circuit rejected. The breaker's rating is the real ceiling -- exceeding it is what overheats the wire.

wisdom: One small multiplication keeps a home safe: volts times amps is watts, and every circuit has a ceiling it cannot be argued past. The breaker that trips when a heater and a microwave share a line is not a nuisance but a guard standing between an overloaded wire and a fire -- which is why a coin jammed in a fuse box has burned down houses. Add up what you plug in, respect the limit, give the hungry appliances their own line. 'The prudent sees danger and hides himself' (Proverbs 22:3).

title: How long a planet's year is - Kepler's third law
situation: For every planet, the SQUARE of its orbital period (in Earth-years) equals the CUBE of its distance from the sun (in AU, where Earth = 1). The engine confirmed it for Mercury (0.241 yr at 0.387 AU), Earth (1 and 1), Mars (1.881 yr at 1.524 AU), and Jupiter (11.86 yr at 5.20 AU). A made-up planet '2 AU away orbiting in 2 years' was caught and rejected -- at 2 AU the year is about 2.83 years.
use: To estimate a planet's year from its distance: take the distance in AU (Earth=1), cube it, then take the square root -- that is the period in Earth-years. A world twice as far as Earth (2 AU): 2 cubed is 8, square root about 2.83 years. This is why the outer planets crawl: Jupiter, five times farther than Earth, takes nearly twelve years to circle the sun once. A fine lesson to teach with a backyard look at the planets.

pre_run.summary: Practical/teaching wisdom grounded in the Atlas (astronomy verifier, Kepler's third law T^2 = a^3). Four real planets confirmed; a fabricated period-distance pair rejected. One simple relation ties every planet's distance to the length of its year.

domain_results.detail: Mercury/Earth/Mars/Jupiter consistent with T^2=a^3; 2 AU in 2 yr rejected (true ~2.83)

wisdom: The planets do not wander freely; each keeps a covenant between its distance and its year, the square of one always matching the cube of the other. The same law governs the swift inner worlds and slow far Jupiter -- one rule, written once, holding everywhere we can measure. To show a child that the heavens run on a law they can check with their own arithmetic is to show them a Lawgiver. 'The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky above proclaims his handiwork' (Psalm 19:1).

title: How many equal shares can you make - the greatest common divisor
situation: To divide two different quantities into the SAME number of equal groups, find their greatest common divisor (the largest number that divides both). The engine confirmed it: 12 and 18 share a GCD of 6 (so 6 groups); 24 and 36 share 12; 15 and 25 share 5; and 13, being prime, has no equal split above one. The claim that 12 and 18 could make 9 equal groups was caught and rejected -- 9 does not divide 12.
use: Making gift bags, baskets, or teams from two different stocks? The most equal groups you can make is the greatest common divisor of the two counts. 12 apples + 18 pears -> GCD 6 -> 6 baskets of 2 apples and 3 pears each. To find the GCD by hand: keep subtracting the smaller number from the larger until both match (Euclid's way), or list each number's divisors and take the biggest they share. If a count is prime (13, 17, 23...), it won't divide evenly except one-by-one.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (number_theory verifier, gcd + primality). Three GCDs confirmed (12&18->6, 24&36->12, 15&25->5), 13 confirmed prime; a non-dividing claim (12&18->9) rejected. The GCD is exactly the largest equal-group count two quantities allow.

wisdom: Fair division has an exact answer: two heaps can be shared into equal portions only as many ways as a single number -- their greatest common divisor -- allows, and no wishing makes nine groups out of twelve. Knowing it turns a fussy sorting job into a moment's arithmetic, whether you are filling party bags, splitting a harvest, or making even teams. And a prime count simply cannot be evened out, which is its own quiet lesson in accepting what a thing actually is. 'That there might be equality' (2 Corinthians 8:14).

title: Why a longer password beats a clever one
situation: A password's strength is measured in BITS, and each random character adds only a few: the engine confirmed one digit carries about 3.32 bits, one lowercase letter about 4.70, one full-keyboard character about 6.57, and a single yes/no carries exactly 1. The number of guesses needed to break it is 2 raised to those total bits -- so each added character MULTIPLIES the work. The notion that 26 letters give 26 bits per character was caught and rejected; it is only about 4.7.
use: Strength (bits) = length x bits-per-character (digit ~3.3, lowercase ~4.7, full keyboard ~6.6); guesses to crack = 2^bits. Because of that exponent, LENGTH beats complexity: a 16-character passphrase of a few random words (~75 bits) is far stronger than an 8-character 'P@ssw0rd!' (~52 bits and already on every cracking list). Use a long passphrase of unrelated words, and let a password manager carry the long ones so you needn't memorize them.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (information_theory verifier, Shannon entropy of a uniform choice = log2 of the options). Four per-character bit values confirmed; the '26 letters = 26 bits' error rejected. Since guesses = 2^(total bits) and bits = length x per-char, adding length compounds strength exponentially.

wisdom: A single character hides less than people think -- under five bits for a letter -- so a short password is weak however clever its symbols. But the work to guess one doubles with every bit, which means LENGTH, not punctuation, is what truly hardens it: four honest words you can remember outlast a tortured eight-character cipher you cannot. Make it long, make it yours, and let a tool keep the rest. 'Guard the deposit entrusted to you' (1 Timothy 6:20).

title: Why you hear the neighbor's bass, not their treble
situation: A sound's wavelength is the speed of sound (about 343 m/s in air) divided by its frequency. The engine confirmed it: a deep 100 Hz bass note is 3.43 m long -- longer than most rooms; concert A (440 Hz) is about 0.78 m; a 343 Hz tone is exactly 1 m; a high 10,000 Hz tone is only about 3.4 cm. The claim that a 100 Hz note is 0.34 m was caught and rejected -- it is 3.43 m, ten times longer.
use: Wavelength = 343 / frequency (meters, hertz). LOW notes are long waves (a 100 Hz bass ~ 3.4 m) that bend around corners and pass through walls; HIGH notes are short waves (treble ~ centimeters) that travel in straight lines and are easily absorbed. So to block a neighbor's thumping bass you need MASS -- dense, heavy walls; soft things won't stop it. To tame echo and harsh treble inside a room, soft furnishings (rugs, curtains, cushions) are enough.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (acoustics verifier, wave relation v = f x wavelength). Four sound wavelengths confirmed from 100 Hz to 10 kHz; a ten-fold-too-short bass wavelength rejected. Long bass waves diffract and penetrate; short treble waves are directional and absorbable.

domain_results.detail: 343Hz->1m, 440Hz->0.78m, 100Hz->3.43m, 10kHz->0.0343m confirmed; 100Hz->0.34m rejected

wisdom: What you cannot block is often what you cannot see: the bass that carries through a wall is a wave three meters long, bending around every obstacle, while the crisp treble dies in the first curtain it meets. Knowing this turns a vague annoyance into a plan -- mass for the low, softness for the high -- and saves you from soundproofing the wrong way. The unheard half of a sound is the long, low part that travels farthest. 'He who has ears to hear, let him hear' (Mark 4:9).

title: What a positive test really means - don't panic at the number
situation: A positive result on an accurate test is NOT the same as having the condition -- its real meaning depends on how common the condition is (the base rate). The engine confirmed with Bayes' theorem: for a condition 1% of people have, a 99%-sensitive test with a 5% false-positive rate gives only about a 17% chance you truly have it; if the condition is rarer (0.1%), only about 2%; and a more specific test (1% false positives) raises it to 50%. The fearful assumption that a '99% accurate' test means a 99% chance you are sick was caught and rejected -- it is about 17%.
use: When a screening test comes back positive, ask two questions before you fear: (1) How common is this condition? The rarer it is, the more a lone positive is a false alarm. (2) What is the test's false-positive rate? Lower is far better. The honest probability you actually have it = (sensitivity x prevalence) / (sensitivity x prevalence + false-positive-rate x (1 - prevalence)). The practical move is almost always a confirmatory test -- never a single screening -- and a conversation with your doctor, not a panic.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (probability verifier, Bayes' theorem). Three base-rate scenarios confirmed (17%, ~2%, 50%); the 'accurate test = certain diagnosis' error rejected. A test's accuracy and the result's meaning are different things once the condition is rare.

domain_results.detail: P(disease|+): 1% prev->0.167, 0.1% prev->0.019, 1% false+->0.5; claimed 0.99 rejected

wisdom: A test that is right 99 times in 100 can still be wrong about YOU most of the time, if the thing it looks for is rare -- because there are simply far more healthy people to mislabel than sick ones to catch. The number on the result states its case loudly; the base rate is the quiet witness that examines it. So weigh both, seek the confirming test, and do not let one alarming positive rule your peace. 'The one who states his case first seems right, until the other comes and examines him' (Proverbs 18:17).

title: Why pavement floods and lawn does not - storm runoff
situation: Storm runoff follows Q = C x rainfall-intensity x area, where C is the fraction that runs off instead of soaking in: roof and pavement about 0.95, lawn about 0.30, woods about 0.10. The engine confirmed it for a quarter-acre lot in a 2 in/hr storm: as lawn it sheds about 0.15 cfs (~67 gallons per minute), but PAVED it sheds about 0.475 cfs (~213 gpm) -- more than three times as much; a 4 in/hr downpour on lawn doubles the lawn figure to 0.30 cfs. A claim that the lawn sheds 0.50 cfs was caught and rejected.
use: Estimate runoff with Q = C x intensity x area. C (the run-off share): pavement/roof ~0.95, packed dirt ~0.6, lawn ~0.30, woods ~0.10. To keep water ON your land and out of the basement, LOWER C: gravel or pavers instead of solid concrete, rain gardens and grassed swales to catch flow, keep trees and mulch. Size gutters and yard drains for a hard rain (2 in/hr) on your HARDEST surface, since that is what overwhelms them.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (hydrology verifier, Rational Method Q = C*i*A). Three runoff figures confirmed (lawn 0.15, paved 0.475, downpour 0.30 cfs); a wrong lawn figure rejected. The runoff coefficient C is the lever: hard surfaces shed ~3x what lawn does.

wisdom: The same rain falls on the lawn and the driveway, but the ground keeps what the pavement throws away -- three times as much water rushing off the hard surface to find the low spot, often a neighbor's yard or your own foundation. Every square of concrete is a small decision to shed water instead of receive it; gravel, garden, and grass decide the other way. To tend the ground is partly to let it drink. 'The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to work it and keep it' (Genesis 2:15).

title: How many ways - handshakes, seatings, and the lottery
situation: Counting arrangements has exact answers. The engine confirmed: in a group of 10 where everyone greets everyone once there are 45 handshakes (n times n-minus-1 over 2); a family of 6 can be seated in 720 different orders (6 factorial); choosing 2 toppings from 5 gives 10 options; and a 6-from-49 lottery has 13,983,816 combinations. The claim that the lottery has 'about a million' combinations was caught and rejected -- it is nearly fourteen million.
use: Three everyday counts: (1) Everyone-meets-everyone in a group of n -> n x (n-1) / 2 (handshakes, toasts, the lines between dots): 10 people = 45. (2) Seating n people in a row -> n factorial: a family of 6 = 720 orders, so 'we never sit the same way twice' is nearly true for years. (3) Picking k things from n where order doesn't matter -> 'n choose k'. A 6-from-49 lottery = 13,983,816, so one ticket is about 1 chance in 14 million -- roughly the odds of flipping 24 heads in a row.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (combinatorics verifier). Handshakes C(10,2)=45, seating 6!=720, toppings C(5,2)=10, lottery C(49,6)=13,983,816 all confirmed; the 'about a million' lottery estimate rejected. Combinations grow far faster than intuition expects.

wisdom: The number of ways things can combine outruns the imagination fast: ten friends already make forty-five handshakes, six chairs hide seven hundred and twenty seatings, and a single lottery line is one hope against fourteen million. That last number is worth feeling honestly -- it is why the ticket is a tax on hope, not a plan. Count before you spend, and let daily labor, which compounds, do what a draw almost never will. 'Wealth gained hastily will dwindle, but whoever gathers little by little will increase it' (Proverbs 13:11).

title: Will it frost tonight? Read the dew point
situation: The dew point is the temperature at which the air's moisture begins to condense -- and on a clear, calm night the overnight low falls toward it and tends to settle near it, because forming dew or frost releases heat that slows further cooling. The engine confirmed the dew points: 20C at 50% humidity = 9.3C; 10C at 80% = 6.7C; 25C at 40% = 10.5C; and 5C at 60% = -2.1C (below freezing -> frost risk). A claim that 20C at 50% gives an 18C dew point was caught and rejected -- it is 9.3C.
use: To judge frost tonight, look up the DEW POINT (most weather apps show it), not just the forecast low. On a clear, calm night the temperature drifts down toward the dew point and tends to stall there. If the dew point is at or below freezing (32F / 0C) -> frost is likely; cover tender plants, drain hoses. If the dew point is comfortably above freezing -> frost is unlikely even if the air feels cold. Wind or cloud cover both REDUCE frost risk (they slow the night-time cooling). Bonus: dew point, not humidity percent, is the honest 'mugginess' number -- above ~18C/65F feels sticky.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (meteorology verifier, Magnus dew-point formula). Four dew points confirmed across temperature/humidity pairs; an impossible 18C dew point at 20C/50% rejected. The dew point acts as a soft floor for the overnight low on clear, still nights.

wisdom: The forecast low tells you part of the story; the dew point tells you where the cold will likely stop. When the air can hold no more water it gives some back as dew or frost, and the warmth that releases keeps the night from falling much further -- so a dew point above freezing is a quiet promise your seedlings will live, and one below it is a warning to go cover them. Learn to read the sky's plain signs and act before the cold, not after. 'You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky' (Matthew 16:3).

title: Soil pH - why a plant can starve in rich soil
situation: Every crop has a soil-pH range it can feed in, and outside it even well-fertilized ground 'locks up' nutrients. The engine confirmed: maize is suited to pH 6.2 and potato to 5.5; blueberry (acid-loving) is suited to 4.8 but NOT to 7.5; and a 40/40/20 sand/silt/clay mix is 'loam'. A claim that blueberry is fine at pH 7.5 was caught and rejected.
use: Test your soil pH first (cheap strips or a meter). Most vegetables, grasses, and fruit trees want pH 6.0-7.0; acid-lovers -- blueberry, azalea, rhododendron, potato -- want 4.5-5.5. To RAISE pH (sweeten sour soil): work in garden lime. To LOWER pH (acidify): add elemental sulfur or peat. Change it gradually and retest -- pH moves slowly. Texture matters too: loam (a balance of sand, silt, and clay, e.g. 40/40/20) is ideal; heavy clay holds water but drains poorly, pure sand drains too fast.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (soil_science verifier, per-crop pH suitability + texture class). Four crop/pH judgments confirmed (maize 6.2, blueberry 4.8 yes / 7.5 no, potato 5.5) and loam texture; a false 'blueberry fine at 7.5' rejected. The right pH is what lets roots take up the nutrients already present.

domain_results.detail: maize@6.2 ok, blueberry@4.8 ok & @7.5 not, potato@5.5 ok, 40/40/20=loam; blueberry@7.5 'suitable' rejected

wisdom: A plant can sit in fertile, well-fed ground and still go hungry, because the wrong pH locks the nutrients away where roots cannot reach. So before you add more fertilizer, test the soil and match the bed to the plant -- lime to sweeten, sulfur to sour -- and the same earth that starved one crop will feed another. The ground decides what a seed becomes. 'Other seeds fell on good soil and produced grain' (Matthew 13:8): prepare the soil, and the harvest follows.

title: How far is that, really - reading distance off a map
situation: The straight-line ('as the crow flies') distance between two points has an exact answer. The engine confirmed: New York to Los Angeles is about 3,936 km, London to Paris about 344 km, New York to Chicago about 1,145 km, and one degree of latitude is about 111 km anywhere on Earth. A claim that New York to LA is only 1,000 km was caught and rejected.
use: The handy ruler: 1 degree of latitude ~= 111 km (69 miles) ANYWHERE, and 1 minute of latitude = 1 nautical mile (1.85 km). So count degrees up the SIDE of any map to scale a distance. Longitude lines crowd together toward the poles -- a degree of longitude is 111 km at the equator but only about 79 km at 45 degrees latitude (multiply by the cosine of the latitude). 'As the crow flies' is the shortest possible; real road travel is usually 20-40% farther. Map scale like 1:24000 means one unit on paper = 24,000 on the ground (1 inch = 2,000 feet).

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (geography verifier, Haversine great-circle distance). Three city-pair distances confirmed plus the 111-km-per-degree-of-latitude rule; a grossly short NYC-LA claim rejected. Latitude is a constant ruler; longitude shrinks toward the poles.

domain_results.detail: NYC-LA 3935.7km, London-Paris 343.6km, NYC-Chicago 1144.3km, 1deg lat=111.19km; claimed 1000km rejected

wisdom: The earth keeps one honest ruler everywhere: a degree of latitude is the same hundred-and-eleven kilometers in Kansas or Kenya, so anyone can read real distance off the side of a map. The straight way between two places is always the shortest, and always shorter than the road that actually gets you there -- a quiet reminder that the direct path and the traveled path are rarely the same length. Know the true distance before you set out, and plan for the road, not the crow. 'In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths' (Proverbs 3:6).

title: Why water holds heat - and your cast-iron pan doesn't
situation: Heat needed = mass x specific heat x temperature change (Q = m c dT). Water's specific heat (4186 J per kg per degree C) is among the highest of any common substance. The engine confirmed: warming 1 litre of water 10C takes about 41,860 J; boiling a litre from room temperature (20->100C) takes about 335,000 J; a 1 kg hot-water bottle cooling 30C gives back about 126,000 J. Cast iron (450 J/kg/C) takes only 4,500 J for the same 10C -- about one-ninth of water. A claim that 1 litre of water needs only 10,000 J to rise 10C was caught and rejected.
use: Water stores roughly NINE times the heat of iron per kilogram per degree, and gives it back slowly. Practical consequences: a hot-water bottle stays warm for hours (and a jug of hot water in a cooler or greenhouse buffers a cold night); radiators and heat stores use water for the same reason; a dry pan scorches in seconds but the water in it takes minutes; and a lake or coastline keeps its town milder than inland because the water resists changing temperature. To estimate energy: Q (joules) = litres x 4186 x degrees-C of change.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (thermodynamics verifier, Q = m c dT). Four specific-heat results confirmed (water +10C, boil, hot-water-bottle, and cast iron for contrast); a too-low water figure rejected. This page carries its own citable, tamper-evident proof receipt.

domain_results.detail: water 1kg/10C=41860J, boil 80C=334880J, iron 1kg/10C=4500J, bottle 30C=125580J; 10000J claim rejected

wisdom: Water is the patient element: it refuses to heat or cool quickly, and so it keeps -- a warmth poured in at night is given back slowly till morning, a coastline tempered against the day's extremes. That same stubborn steadiness is why we lean on it, in a kettle, a radiator, a hot-water bottle, a sea. The things that hold the most are rarely the quickest to change. 'He is like a tree planted by streams of water' (Psalm 1:3). And the number is checkable -- this page links to its own sealed proof.

title: How many animals can the land carry?
situation: Pasture has a carrying capacity, and overstocking strips it faster than it regrows. The engine confirmed sustainable densities per acre of decent pasture: about 1 beef cow-calf pair (range 0.25-2), 4 sheep (2-8), 6 goats (2-10), and 200 pastured hens (50-500). A claim that 10 beef cattle per acre is sustainable was caught and rejected -- that is five times the upper limit, a recipe for bare ground.
use: Match animals to land, per acre of good pasture: beef 0.25-2 cow-calf pairs (start near 1), dairy cow 0.5-1.5, sheep 2-8, goats 2-10, horse 0.25-1, pastured pigs 5-25, pastured chickens 50-500, ducks 50-200, rabbits 100-500. Thin or poor ground holds far fewer; start at the LOW end and adjust. The tell of overstocking is grass eaten faster than it grows -- bare patches, mud, rising feed bills, and worm load. The fix is fewer animals OR rotational grazing: split the land into paddocks, move the herd through, and let each rest weeks to recover.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (agriculture verifier, stocking-density reference ranges). Four sustainable densities confirmed (beef, sheep, goat, pastured hens); a 10-cattle-per-acre overstock rejected. Carrying capacity is a hard limit -- exceed it and the pasture, not just the budget, pays.

domain_results.detail: beef 1/ac (0.25-2), sheep 4 (2-8), goat 6 (2-10), hens 200 (50-500) within range; 10 beef/ac rejected

wisdom: Land has a limit, and the kind thing and the wise thing are the same: keep fewer animals than the grass can feed, and both the beasts and the ground stay well. Crowd them past what the pasture grows and you trade green for mud, and a feed bill for what the rain gave free. Move them, rest the land, count honestly. 'Whoever is righteous has regard for the life of his beast' (Proverbs 12:10) -- and good husbandry begins with not asking the land for more than it can give. The numbers here link to their own sealed proof.

title: What inflation quietly does to idle cash
situation: Money kept still loses buying power: its real value = nominal / (1 + inflation)^years. The engine confirmed: $1,000 at 3% inflation is worth about $744 in ten years; at 7% it nearly halves to $508 in ten years; at 2% over thirty years it falls to $552; at 5% over five years, $784. The claim that an idle $1,000 is still worth $1,000 after ten years was caught and rejected -- inflation took roughly a quarter of it.
use: Real value = nominal / (1 + inflation rate)^years. A fast gauge -- inflation has its OWN rule of 72: prices double (your money halves) in about 72 / inflation% years (3% -> ~24 yrs, 7% -> ~10 yrs). The number that actually matters is the REAL return = what your savings earn MINUS inflation: if a bank pays 1% while prices rise 3%, you are losing about 2% a year even as the balance ticks up. To merely hold ground, savings must earn AT LEAST the inflation rate; to grow, beat it.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (economics verifier, inflation adjustment real = nominal/(1+i)^t). Four erosion figures confirmed (3%/10y, 7%/10y, 2%/30y, 5%/5y); the 'no erosion' claim rejected. Idle cash is not stable -- it shrinks at the inflation rate.

wisdom: Cash in a drawer feels safe, but it is quietly bleeding -- a tenth, a quarter, half of its worth, depending on the years and the rate -- because the prices it must someday pay keep climbing while it sits. This is the mirror of the doubling clock: the same compounding that grows savings forward erodes idle money backward. The point is not anxiety but stewardship -- put what you keep where it at least keeps pace, and don't bury it. 'You ought to have deposited my money with the bankers, and at my coming I should have received what was my own with interest' (Matthew 25:27). The figures here link to their own sealed proof.

title: Verified connection — counting is one structure (number theory + combinatorics)
situation: Two independent domains reach the SAME count by different definitions: number theory computes 5! = 120; combinatorics computes the permutations of 5 distinct things, P(5,5) = 120. Both confirmed; the arrangements of five items are one structure seen two ways. This is the DISCRETENESS axis, established by two witnesses (Deut 19:15) and now sealed with a citable proof.
use: When a count can be derived two independent ways and both agree, the agreement is itself evidence the structure is real, not an artifact of one method. Here: factorial (multiply 5x4x3x2x1) and permutation-counting (arrange 5 in 5 slots) are different roads to 120.

pre_run.summary: Two-witness co-confirmation via /derivation/verify (sealed). number_theory 5!=120 and combinatorics P(5,5)=120 both CONFIRMED -> the discreteness connection holds, with a tamper-evident proof.

wisdom: By the mouth of two witnesses a matter is established (Deuteronomy 19:15). Two fields that never consulted each other arrive at the same one hundred and twenty, because they are counting the same reality from different sides. A connection trusted is a connection two independent floors have each measured -- and this one is sealed, so anyone can check it.

title: Verified connection — the tetrahedral angle, two independent witnesses (geometry + molecular geometry)
situation: Pure coordination geometry says a tetrahedral arrangement has a central angle of 109.47 degrees (arccos of -1/3); VSEPR molecular geometry, counting four bonding pairs and no lone pairs, independently gives the same 109.47 degrees. Both confirmed. The SYMMETRY axis, established by two witnesses (Deut 19:15) and now sealed with a citable proof.
use: The tetrahedral 109.47-degree angle governs methane, water's parent geometry, diamond, and silicate minerals. That a pure geometric argument and a chemical electron-counting argument land on the identical angle is why you can trust it across both fields.

pre_run.summary: Two-witness co-confirmation via /derivation/verify (sealed). geometry (coordination tetrahedral -> 109.47) and molecular_geometry (VSEPR 4 bonding/0 lone -> 109.47) both CONFIRMED -> the symmetry connection holds, with a tamper-evident proof.

domain_results.detail: tetrahedral central angle = 109.47 = arccos(-1/3)
VSEPR 4 bonding/0 lone -> tetrahedral 109.47

wisdom: One angle, reached by a geometer's arccosine and a chemist's count of electron pairs -- the same 109.47 degrees, because the shape is one and the disciplines only see it from their own doors. Where two independent witnesses agree on the measure, the measure stands (Deuteronomy 19:15); and being sealed, it can be cited and re-checked by anyone.

title: Errors the engine refused - and what is actually true
situation: This engine VERIFIES a provided claim deterministically; it never generates an answer, so a false claim comes back BROKEN, not dressed up as plausible. Below are real claims it has refused while building the Atlas and Almanac -- each with the true value. The refusal is the point: an engine you can trust is one that tells you NO and shows its work. Three of these refusals carry a sealed, tamper-evident proof you can open and re-check.
caught: '2047 is prime' -> FALSE: 2047 = 23 x 89 (the classic Mersenne imposter, 2^11-1)
'd/dx cos(x) = sin(x)' -> FALSE: it is MINUS sin(x)
'10 beef cattle per acre is sustainable' -> FALSE: the range is 0.25-2/acre; 10 is overgrazing
'copper is [Ar] 3d9 4s2' -> FALSE: copper is an exception, [Ar] 3d10 4s1
'the n=3 electron shell holds 8' -> FALSE: it holds 18 (2n^2); 8 is the period, not the shell
'a 3-4-6 triangle is a right triangle' -> FALSE: 9 + 16 = 25, not 36
'a two-sided z of 1.96 gives p = 0.5' -> FALSE: p is about 0.05
'F = m*v is dimensionally consistent (a force)' -> FALSE: that is momentum, not force
'2 H2 + O2 -> 3 H2O is balanced' -> FALSE: it is 2 H2O (atoms must conserve)
'idle cash keeps its value' -> FALSE: at 3% inflation $1000 is worth ~$744 in 10 years
'440 Hz and 880 Hz form a perfect fifth' -> FALSE: that 2:1 ratio is an OCTAVE
use: Want to test it yourself? POST a claim to /derivation/solve (plain language) or /derivation/verify (structured) with seal:true. A true claim returns HOLDS + a citable proof; a false one returns BROKEN at the failing step. The engine maps a claim only to a real verifier -- what it cannot check, it OMITS rather than guesses.

pre_run.summary: A showcase of refusals, the engine's defining behavior. Each listed claim was machine-checked and rejected against the deterministic verifiers; three are sealed as citable BROKEN proofs (2047 prime, d/dx cos=sin, 10 cattle/acre).

domain_results.detail: 13 representative false claims caught; truth shown for each

wisdom: The strength of a witness is not only what it affirms but what it refuses. Anyone can produce a fluent answer; the rare and trustworthy thing is a firm, reasoned NO -- 2047 is not prime, that is not a force, the cattle will starve the field. 'Let your yes be yes and your no be no; anything more comes from the evil one' (Matthew 5:37). Here the no is exact, and it is sealed, so you need not take it on our word.

title: How much to buy - concrete, tile, flooring
situation: Material math has exact answers. The engine confirmed: a 3x3 m pad 10 cm (0.1 m) deep needs 0.9 m3 of concrete; a 10x5 m drive at 15 cm needs 7.5 m3; a 4x5 m room is 20 m2 of floor; and 50 m2 of 0.25 m2 tiles with 10% waste comes to 221 tiles (rounded UP). A claim that the 3x3x0.1 m pad needs 9 m3 -- a ten-fold decimal slip -- was caught and rejected; it is 0.9.
use: Concrete VOLUME = length x width x depth, ALL in the same unit (metres -> m3; or feet -> cubic feet, divide by 27 for cubic yards). The #1 mistake is depth in the wrong unit: 10 cm is 0.1 m, not 10 -- that slip orders ten times too much. TILES/FLOORING: area divided by one tile's area, then add a WASTE factor (about 10% for straight lay, 15% for diagonal or odd-shaped rooms), and ROUND UP -- you cannot buy a fraction of a tile and you want spares for breakage and future repairs. In every case order a little extra: cheaper than a second trip or a mismatched dye lot.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (construction verifier: volume, area, floor-tile count with waste). Four buy-quantities confirmed (0.9 m3, 7.5 m3, 20 m2, 221 tiles); the ten-fold decimal-slip concrete figure rejected. Quantities round UP; depth must be in consistent units.

domain_results.detail: pad 0.9 m3, drive 7.5 m3, room 20 m2, tiles 221 (50/0.25 +10%, rounded up); 9 m3 slip rejected

wisdom: Before the first bag is opened, the job is half-won or half-lost in the arithmetic: a misplaced decimal buys ten times the concrete, a forgotten waste factor leaves you a tile short with the store sold out of your color. Measure in one honest unit, add a margin, round up, and the work goes smoothly. 'By wisdom a house is built, and by understanding it is established' (Proverbs 24:3) -- and the counting here links to its own sealed proof.

title: Verified connection — molecular shape IS geometry (chemistry + geometry)
situation: A molecule's bond angles are not chemistry's invention; they are the pure coordination-geometry angles. The engine confirmed all four by TWO independent routes -- coordination geometry (a points-on-a-sphere argument) and VSEPR (counting bonding and lone electron pairs): linear 180 deg (2 bonds), trigonal planar 120 deg (3 bonds), tetrahedral 109.47 deg (4 bonds, = arccos(-1/3)), octahedral 90 deg (6 bonds). Eight steps, all HOLD. The shapes of matter obey geometry.
use: This is why you can predict a molecule's shape from a dot diagram: count the bonding groups and lone pairs around the central atom, and the bond angle is fixed by geometry alone -- 2 -> 180, 3 -> 120, 4 -> 109.5, 6 -> 90. CO2 is linear, BF3 trigonal, methane tetrahedral, SF6 octahedral, every time, because the chemistry is only obeying the math of points spread evenly on a sphere.

pre_run.summary: Two-witness co-confirmation via /derivation/verify (sealed). Four bond angles each confirmed by pure coordination geometry AND by VSEPR electron-pair counting -- 8/8 HOLD. The symmetry axis, extended from one angle (tetrahedral) to the full family of common geometries.

domain_results.detail: linear 180, trigonal 120, tetrahedral 109.47, octahedral 90 (coordination angles)
VSEPR 2/3/4/6 bonding -> same four angles

wisdom: The chemist counts electron pairs and the geometer spaces points on a sphere, and they arrive at the very same angles -- one hundred and eighty, one hundred twenty, one hundred nine and a half, ninety -- because there is one order underneath both. Matter is shaped by mathematics it never had to choose. 'In him all things hold together' (Colossians 1:17): the consistency that lets shape be predicted from number is not luck but the signature of a single Author. Two independent witnesses agree, and the proof is sealed for any to check.

title: Located and verified — Fermat numbers, and Fermat's famous error
situation: Pierre de Fermat conjectured (c.1640) that every number of the form F_n = 2^(2^n) + 1 is prime. The engine confirmed the five he could check by hand are indeed prime: F_0=3, F_1=5, F_2=17, F_3=257, F_4=65537. But F_5 = 4,294,967,297 was caught as NOT prime -- exactly the result Leonhard Euler proved in 1732 (F_5 = 641 x 6,700,417), refuting the conjecture. The engine reproduced Euler's refutation from first principles.
use: A worked case of the engine's defining move: it confirms what is true (five Fermat primes, sealed below) and refuses what is false (F_5 is composite), even when the false claim comes from one of history's greatest mathematicians. No number is taken on authority; primality is recomputed. To this day only those five Fermat primes are known -- every larger F_n tested has been composite.

pre_run.summary: Located from the Fermat-number framework; F_0-F_4 verified prime via /derivation/verify (sealed, HOLDS 5/5); F_5 = 4294967297 returned BROKEN (composite), reproducing Euler 1732. Primality computed, not looked up.

wisdom: Even Fermat, reasoning from a beautiful pattern, guessed wrong at the sixth step -- and the error stood until someone simply checked. That is the whole discipline in one story: a pattern, however elegant, is not a proof, and greatness is no guard against a false conclusion. The floor does not care who made the claim; it recomputes. 'Test everything; hold fast what is good' (1 Thessalonians 5:21). The five true primes here are sealed, so you can check them yourself -- as Euler checked Fermat.

title: Verified connection — Pythagoras joins shape and number (geometry + arithmetic)
situation: A right triangle and an arithmetic identity are the same truth seen two ways. The engine confirmed, by two INDEPENDENT routes, three Pythagorean triples: geometry tested 3-4-5, 5-12-13, and 8-15-17 as right triangles, and arithmetic independently confirmed 3^2+4^2=5^2, 5^2+12^2=13^2, 8^2+15^2=17^2. Six steps, all HOLD. The shape and the numbers are one relation.
use: This is why a tape measure can find a square corner (3-4-5, recorded elsewhere in the Almanac) and why a builder, a surveyor, and an algebra student are doing the same thing in different languages. A triangle is right EXACTLY when its sides satisfy a^2+b^2=c^2 -- geometry and number are not two subjects here but one, joined at the right angle.

pre_run.summary: Two-witness co-confirmation via /derivation/verify (sealed). For three triples, geometry confirmed a right triangle AND arithmetic confirmed a^2+b^2=c^2 -- 6/6 HOLD. The Pythagorean theorem is the bridge between shape and number, machine-checked from both sides.

domain_results.detail: 3-4-5, 5-12-13, 8-15-17 are right triangles

wisdom: The geometer draws a corner and the arithmetician adds two squares, and they meet at the same place -- the right angle is the equation, the equation is the right angle. That number and shape answer to one another, that a thing you can SEE is governed by a thing you can COUNT, is among the oldest hints that creation is written in a single, consistent hand. 'When he drew a circle on the face of the deep... I was there' (Proverbs 8:27,30, wisdom at the founding). Two witnesses agree, and the proof is sealed for any to check.

title: Heart-rate zones and the truth about calories burned
situation: Two useful workout numbers, checked. MAX heart rate: a better estimate than the old '220 minus age' is 208 minus 0.7 x age -- about 180 bpm at age 40, 187 at 30. TARGET zone (Karvonen, which uses your resting pulse): for age 40 with a resting heart rate of 60, the 70-80% zone is 144-156 bpm. CALORIES: roughly MET x weight(kg) x hours -- an hour of running (MET 8) at 70 kg burns about 560 kcal. The claim that the same hour burns 1,000 kcal was caught and rejected.
use: MAX HR ~ 208 - 0.7 x age (yours can differ +/- 10-12 bpm; these are averages). TARGET ZONE (Karvonen): zone bpm = (maxHR - restingHR) x intensity + restingHR. Rough intensities: 60-70% easy/fat-burn, 70-80% moderate cardio, 80-90% hard. CALORIES: kcal ~ MET x bodyweight-in-kg x hours (walking ~3 MET, brisk walk ~5, running ~8-12). Treat cardio-machine calorie readouts as optimistic -- and do NOT 'eat back' inflated numbers, which is how exercise quietly fails to move the scale.

pre_run.summary: Practical wisdom grounded in the Atlas (exercise_science verifier: Tanaka max HR, Karvonen zone, MET energy). Four results confirmed (max HR 180/187, zone 144-156, 560 kcal); a 1000-kcal overestimate rejected. Estimates are population averages, not personal certainties.

domain_results.detail: maxHR age40=180, age30=187; Karvonen 70-80% (rest60)=144-156; MET8*70kg*1h=560 kcal; 1000 kcal rejected

wisdom: Know the real numbers and you train smarter and eat honester: a target zone you can feel at your own pulse, and a calorie burn smaller than the machine flatters you with. Bodily exercise is worth tending -- but in proportion, not as the whole of life. 'Bodily training is of some value, but godliness is of value in every way, holding promise for the present life and also for the life to come' (1 Timothy 4:8). Steward the body; do not worship it. The figures here link to their own sealed proof.

title: Located and verified — Euler's prime polynomial, and where it breaks
situation: Euler's famous formula n^2 + n + 41 produces a prime for every n from 0 to 39 -- a startling run of 40 primes in a row. The engine confirmed a sample: n=0 gives 41, n=1 gives 43, n=2 gives 47, n=5 gives 71, n=10 gives 151, and n=39 gives 1601 -- all prime. But at n=40 the formula gives 1681, which the engine caught as NOT prime: it is exactly 41 squared. A pattern that holds forty times, and then fails.
use: A clean lesson in why a streak is not a proof. Forty confirmations did not make the rule true -- and you could not know it failed without CHECKING n=40 (note the formula must fail at n=40, since 40^2+40+41 = 40(40+1)+41 = 41x40+41 = 41x41). The engine confirms the run and refuses the false conclusion. The five Fermat primes told the same story from the other side: see located_fermat_numbers_verified.

pre_run.summary: Located from Euler's prime-generating polynomial. Six sampled values (n=0,1,2,5,10,39 -> 41,43,47,71,151,1601) verified prime via /derivation/verify (sealed, HOLDS 6/6); n=40 -> 1681 returned BROKEN (= 41^2). Primality computed, not assumed.

wisdom: Forty times the pattern held, and the forty-first broke it -- a warning that the mind loves a streak and mistakes it for a law. What seems certain because it has 'always' worked may simply not have been tested far enough; the honest move is to check the next case, not to trust the run. 'There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way to death' (Proverbs 16:25). The primes here are sealed, so the streak -- and its limit -- can be checked by anyone.

title: Verified connection — the bell curve joins probability and statistics
situation: A p-value and a cumulative probability are the same standard normal curve, read two ways. The engine confirmed it by two INDEPENDENT routes: statistics computed that a two-sided z of 1.96 gives p = 0.05 (and z = 2.576 gives p = 0.01), while probability independently computed the normal CDF Phi(1.96) = 0.975 and Phi(2.576) = 0.995. Four steps, all HOLD. Significance testing and probability are not two subjects here -- they are one bell curve.
use: This is why the same number, 1.96, marks both the 95% confidence cutoff (statistics) and the 97.5th percentile of the bell curve (probability): a two-sided p-value of 0.05 is exactly twice the area in one tail beyond z=1.96, and that tail area is 1 minus the CDF. Understand the normal curve once and you hold both -- the chance of a value, and the significance of a result.

pre_run.summary: Two-witness co-confirmation via /derivation/verify (sealed). Statistics (z-test p-values) and probability (normal CDF) confirmed the same standard-normal tail at z=1.96 and 2.576 -- 4/4 HOLD. The Gaussian is the bridge between probability theory and inferential statistics.

wisdom: Beneath the gambler's odds and the scientist's significance lies one curve, the same bell rung from two towers. That the spread of a thousand small chances settles into a shape so regular you can read a percentile off it is a kind of order hidden inside randomness -- chance, but not chaos. 'The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from the LORD' (Proverbs 16:33). Two independent witnesses agree on the curve, and the proof is sealed for any to check.

title: Goldbach's conjecture — verified where we can reach, open where we cannot
situation: Goldbach conjectured in 1742 that every even number greater than 2 is the sum of two primes. It is still UNPROVEN. The engine verified it for a ladder of even numbers, each as a genuine prime-pair decomposition: 100 = 3 + 97, 1,000 = 3 + 997, 10,000 = 59 + 9,941, 100,000 = 11 + 99,989, and 1,000,000 = 17 + 999,983 -- 15 steps, all HOLD. The engine cannot PROVE the conjecture (it verifies, it does not generate insight), but it would CATCH a counterexample: the day some even number had no prime pair, every claimed decomposition would BREAK.
use: This is what it means to honestly ATTEMPT an open problem with a verifier: confirm it case by case where you can reach (each here is sealed and checkable), extend the frontier, and stand ready to refuse the first failure -- without ever claiming the proof you do not have. A conjecture verified to a million is not a conjecture proved; it is a conjecture that has not yet broken. The engine holds that line exactly.

pre_run.summary: An open problem attempted honestly. Five even numbers verified as sums of two primes via /derivation/verify (each: prime a, prime b, a+b=n), sealed, HOLDS 15/15. Decompositions found by search; primality and the sum machine-checked. Verification, not proof -- but a real counterexample would have BROKEN it.

wisdom: There is a discipline in knowing the edge of your knowing. The engine will verify what it can reach -- a million even numbers, each splitting cleanly into two primes -- and will not pretend the next case, or the proof of all cases, is in hand. To confirm faithfully and to confess the limit honestly are the same virtue. 'The secret things belong to the LORD our God, but the things that are revealed belong to us and to our children forever' (Deuteronomy 29:29). What is checkable, we check and seal; what is hidden, we do not claim.

title: Narrow it to almost nothing, then any tool can finish
situation: A problem with 90,000 candidates: which 5-digit number is a perfect square, a palindrome, and has digits summing to 25? The engine did not search for the answer. It DELETED what could not be the answer. is_perfect_square removed 89,783 (90,000 -> 217). is_palindrome removed 210 more (217 -> 7). digit_sum=25 removed 6 (7 -> 1). One candidate survived: 44944. A deliberately dumb tool then 'finished' by returning the single survivor, and the engine verified it -- 44944 satisfies every constraint (HOLDS), and independently 212^2 = 44944 (the derivation chain, SymPy). A hallucinated answer (12321) was REFUSED as BROKEN. Both the elimination trail and the math fact are sealed and citable.
use: This is the engine's whole strategy made executable. Three layers, and we own only the hard one: NARROW -- eliminate to almost nothing (un-copyable: it rides our verified substrate; it produces the trail, the reasoning); FINISH -- return the tiny residual (a commodity: any tool, even a cheap or unreliable one, does this); VERIFY -- re-confirm the finish (ours again). Because we verify, the finish is safe to hand to ANYTHING -- a wrong answer fails, a right one seals. We do not race to generate; we narrow to almost nothing and judge what comes back. POST /narrow/eliminate to narrow, POST /narrow/verify to check a finish, GET /narrow/predicates for the eliminators.

pre_run.summary: The narrow-to-nothing handoff, proven end-to-end on prod. NARROW: 90,000 -> 217 -> 7 -> 1 (44944), elimination trail sealed. FINISH: a dumb tool returns the one survivor. VERIFY: 44944 HOLDS on all three constraints; the wrong answer 12321 is BROKEN; independently 212^2 = 44944 HOLDS via the derivation chain. The engine eliminated what cannot be the answer and verified the finish -- it never generated the answer (Principle B).
