pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_kraft_inequality_bounds_codes. The sealed proof shows the Kraft budget: short codewords cost exponentially (length-1 uses half), and three length-1 codewords are impossible.

title: The best use of a fixed measure spends it whole -- no waste, no overreach
verification: Given a fixed budget for naming things, there are three ways to come out. Spend less than you have, and the leftover slack is waste -- your words are longer than they needed to be. Try to spend more, and it simply cannot be built. But spend the budget EXACTLY, balancing perfectly at the full measure, and nothing is squandered and nothing overreached -- which is precisely what the best codes do. The engine confirmed all three cases. So with any allotment entrusted to you: the mark of good stewardship is to use the whole of it well, neither hoarding nor grasping past your means.
wisdom: What is entrusted to you is meant to be used, fully and faithfully -- not buried in the ground out of fear, nor stretched after more than was given; spend the measure well and hear the welcome. "Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things" (Matthew 25:21).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_kraft_inequality_bounds_codes. The sealed proof shows a complete code spends the Kraft budget exactly (sum=1, no slack); under spends waste, over spends are impossible.

title: Thirteen people, twelve months -- two of them must share a birthday month
verification: Gather thirteen people and ask their birth months. With only twelve months to go around, at least two of them are certain to share one -- not likely, certain, however you arrange it. It is the homely truth that if you have more pigeons than pigeonholes, some hole must hold two. Crowd three hundred sixty-six people into a room and a shared birthday is unavoidable. The engine confirmed the counting exactly. So simple it sounds like nothing, yet it proves a great many things that are not nothing at all.
wisdom: When the gathered are more than the places prepared, some must double up; abundance presses past its bounds, and what cannot all be kept apart is brought together. "And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake" (Luke 5:6).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas pigeonhole_principle. The sealed proof confirms n+1 items in n boxes force a collision (13 in 12, 366 in 365).

title: You can be certain a match exists without ever finding it
verification: Here is a strange and certain thing. In a great city there are surely two strangers with the very same number of hairs on their heads -- because no head holds more than a few hundred thousand hairs, while the people number in the millions, so the counts must repeat. You will never meet the pair, never point them out, yet you KNOW beyond doubt they exist. The engine confirmed the counting that guarantees it. Pure reason can hand you a certainty that no searching could -- a thing proven to be, though unseen.
wisdom: Not everything sure must first be seen; reason and faith alike can hold what the eye has not found, resting on what must be true rather than on what happens to be in view. "Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen" (Hebrews 11:1).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas pigeonhole_principle. The sealed pigeonhole counting guarantees a collision exists (more people than possible hair-counts) without exhibiting it.

title: To deny that two things are both true, you need break only one
verification: Suppose someone claims a deal is BOTH cheap and safe. To prove them wrong you do not have to show it is neither -- you need only show it fails on ONE count: not cheap, or not safe. That is the whole of it: to deny that everything holds at once, find a single thing that does not. A chain said to be sound is undone by one weak link. The engine confirmed the rule exactly. Knowing this keeps an argument honest -- and keeps you from over-claiming, since to overturn an everything-must-hold boast, one counterexample is enough.
wisdom: Where many things must all stand together, a single failure brings the whole down; guard every part, for one fault can undo much patient good. "Wisdom is better than weapons of war: but one sinner destroyeth much good" (Ecclesiastes 9:18).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas de_morgan_laws. The sealed proof confirms De Morgan: not(A and B) = (not A) or (not B).

title: To say neither of two things is so, you must rule out both
verification: The mirror rule is just as useful. To claim that NEITHER of two things happened -- he was not at the market and not at home -- it is not enough to clear one place; you must rule out both. Denial has an honest asymmetry: to deny that everything held, one failure suffices; but to deny that anything at all held, you must check every case and find each one false. The engine confirmed both laws. So weigh which kind of denial you are making, and do not declare a clean sweep until you have actually swept every corner.
wisdom: Do not pronounce a thing wholly absent until you have searched each place it might be; the careful examine all the evidence before they conclude, and so are not made liars by the one corner they skipped. "These were more noble... in that they... searched the scriptures daily, whether those things were so" (Acts 17:11).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas de_morgan_laws. The sealed proof confirms De Morgan: not(A or B) = (not A) and (not B).

title: You cannot make every note perfectly in tune at once -- something must yield
verification: Build a chord two honest ways -- by pure thirds or by stacking pure fifths -- and they land a hairsbreadth apart, off by a tiny, exact amount the old musicians named. So no instrument can have all its intervals perfectly pure at the same time; tune the fifths sweet and the thirds turn harsh, tune the thirds sweet and the fifths must give. The tuner cannot serve both, and must yield a little somewhere, by a known and measurable amount. The engine confirmed the exact gap, and refused the claim that the two roads meet. Perfection in everything at once is simply not on offer.
wisdom: You cannot give every good thing its full and pure due at the very same moment; some honest yielding is required, and wisdom is knowing where to give. No servant can answer perfectly to two masters at once. "No man can serve two masters... Ye cannot serve God and mammon" (Matthew 6:24).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_syntonic_comma. The sealed proof shows the pure third (5:4) and the stacked-fifths third (81:64) disagree by 81:80 -- the syntonic comma.

title: Better a small flaw spread evenly than perfection in a few and ruin in the rest
verification: Faced with a gap that cannot be closed, the old tuners had a choice. One way made a handful of keys gorgeously pure -- but left a few others so sour they could not be played at all. The other way, the one our pianos use, spreads the tiny error evenly across every key, so none is perfect yet every one is usable and sweet enough. The engine confirmed both schemes exactly. When an imperfection cannot be removed, it is often wiser to share it out thinly among all than to heap it onto a few.
wisdom: When a burden cannot be done away, let it be borne in common and evenly, that none be crushed while others go free; fairness spreads the load so that all may stand. "Not that other men be eased, and ye burdened: But by an equality... that there may be equality" (2 Corinthians 8:13-14).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_syntonic_comma. The sealed proof underlies temperament: meantone makes some keys pure and others unusable; equal temperament spreads the error so every key works.

title: From one agreed note and one simple rule, the whole keyboard follows
verification: The entire singing range of a piano -- all eighty-eight keys, some seven octaves -- is built from just two things: one reference note (the A that orchestras tune to) and one rule (each step up multiplies the pitch by the same fixed sliver, twelve of which double it). Give any key its number and the exact frequency falls out; climb twelve steps and the pitch doubles, descend twelve and it halves. The engine confirmed the formula to the hair and refused a wrong value. Order this complete asks only for a fixed starting point and a faithful ratio.
wisdom: Vast and intricate things rest on a single sure foundation and a rule held faithfully; lay the one cornerstone rightly, keep the one rule, and all the rest stands in proportion. "For other foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ" (1 Corinthians 3:11).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_midi_to_frequency. The sealed proof shows every key = 440 * 2^((n-69)/12): one reference and one ratio generate the whole keyboard.

title: Tune to a shared note, and many can play together; tune each to himself, and none can
verification: The rule that turns a key into a pitch needs one thing it cannot supply itself: a starting note everyone agrees on. That A had to be fixed by international agreement, for without a common reference each player could be perfectly in tune with himself and still clash with his neighbor. The engine confirmed the map is exact ONCE the shared A is granted -- the math is sure, but the reference is a covenant. A people can only make harmony together when they have first agreed on a common pitch.
wisdom: Harmony among many is not each doing what is right in his own ears, but all consenting to one standard above them; agree on the shared note, and the music becomes possible. "There is one body, and one Spirit... One Lord, one faith, one baptism" (Ephesians 4:4-5).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_midi_to_frequency. The sealed proof is exact given A440, which is an agreed convention -- the shared reference the formula requires.

title: A fair coin holds the most doubt; a sure thing holds none
verification: How much we do not know can be measured, and it follows the shape of the odds. A perfectly fair coin -- fifty-fifty -- carries the most uncertainty there is for two outcomes: one full unit of doubt, because either face is equally live. A coin already certain to come up heads carries none; its outcome tells you nothing new. And a lopsided ninety-to-ten holds barely a scrap of doubt, since you can all but call it in advance. The engine measured these exactly. The evener the chances, the less you can foresee; the more settled, the less the result surprises.
wisdom: Be humble about the balanced future, for where the odds are even no one can call the day; hold your plans loosely and your trust firmly, since tomorrow is not yet read. "Boast not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth" (Proverbs 27:1).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas uncertainty_entropy_coconfirm. The sealed proof shows entropy = average surprise: a fair coin is 1 bit (max), a certain outcome 0 bits, a 90/10 split only 0.47 bits.

title: When two questioned apart give the same answer, the matter is established
verification: There is a way to be sure of a thing that no single voice can give. Take two witnesses who cannot consult each other -- here, two utterly separate ways of measuring uncertainty, one from the world of messages and one from the world of the very small -- and put the same question to each. When they return the identical answer, again and again, the matter is settled: not because anyone forced the agreement, but because the truth was one before either spoke. The engine did exactly this and sealed the accord. We do not weld two truths together; we let each speak, and listen for the harmony already there.
wisdom: A thing testified to twice, by witnesses who did not confer, stands established and sure; so God confirms His purposes, doubling the sign that we might trust it. "And for that the dream was doubled unto Pharaoh twice; it is because the thing is established by God" (Genesis 41:32).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas uncertainty_entropy_coconfirm. The sealed proof is a two-witness co-confirmation: quantum (von Neumann) and information (Shannon) entropy return identical bits.

title: Change one plus to a minus, and distance becomes duration
verification: Ordinary geometry measures the distance across a field by ADDING the squares of the sides -- the old carpenter triangle, three and four make five. Spacetime measures the gap between two events almost the same way, but with one plus turned to a minus: it SUBTRACTS instead. That single flipped sign is the whole secret engine of relativity -- the slowing of moving clocks, the shrinking of speeding rulers, the unpassable speed of light all pour out of it. The engine confirmed both the adding and the subtracting on the very same three-four-five numbers. One small change in the rule, and the world is remade.
wisdom: Do not despise the small turn of the helm; a single sign, a single word, a single choice can swing the whole course of a life from one direction to its opposite. "Behold also the ships... yet are they turned about with a very small helm, whithersoever the governor listeth" (James 3:4).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_spacetime_is_pythagoras_sign_flipped. The sealed proof shows space adds squares (3-4-5) while spacetime subtracts (5^2-4^2=3^2) -- relativity from one flipped sign.

title: Beneath all that moving observers disagree on, there is one thing they all share
verification: Two travelers rushing past each other will not agree on how far apart two events were, nor on how much time passed between them -- their rulers and clocks each tell a different tale. And yet, when each combines space and time by the spacetime rule, they arrive at the very same number, the one quantity no motion can alter. The engine confirmed this shared, invariant measure on the same figures. So under all the disagreement there is something fixed; the clocks shift, the rulers shift, but one thing beneath them does not.
wisdom: When everything around you seems in flux and no two accounts agree, look for the thing that does not move; there is a constancy beneath the change, and at the bottom of it One who never alters. "For I am the LORD, I change not" (Malachi 3:6).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_spacetime_is_pythagoras_sign_flipped. The sealed proof shows the spacetime interval is the one quantity all observers compute the same, though they disagree on space and time alone.

title: Step twice as far from the fire, and you feel only a quarter of its warmth
verification: Move back from a fire, a lamp, or a loudspeaker to twice the distance, and you do not get half -- you get a QUARTER. Anything that pours out from a single point spreads over the surface of a widening ball, and that surface grows with the square of the distance, so the strength thins by the same square. The engine confirmed the very same one-quarter in the pull of gravity and in the loudness of sound, two witnesses to one geometry. Double the distance, quarter the force; step back once more, a quarter again. Distance tells far more than it first appears.
wisdom: A generous source pours itself out into all the world by an even measure, sparing none who will draw near; so the Father spends His common mercies freely, outward, upon all alike. "He maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust" (Matthew 5:45).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_inverse_square_two_witnesses. The sealed proof confirms the inverse-square law in gravity AND sound: double the distance, quarter the strength.

title: Warmth and help fade fast with distance -- so tend those nearest first
verification: Because anything radiating from a source weakens with the square of the distance, nearness counts for far more than we reckon. The one beside the fire is warmed fourfold over the one twice as far; the voice reaches the close listener with a strength the distant one will never feel. The engine confirmed this steep falling-off in both gravity and sound. The plain consequence for a life: your warmth, your help, your example land hardest on those right beside you, and thin quickly with every step away. Tend first, then, to the near.
wisdom: Do not neglect the ones close at hand while reaching after the far; the neighbor at your door receives more of your light than the stranger over the hill, and is the first charge of your love. "Better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off" (Proverbs 27:10).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_inverse_square_two_witnesses. The sealed inverse-square law (strength ~ 1/r^2) means influence falls off steeply with distance -- nearness counts most.

title: What truly fades, fades by halves -- and so is never quite gone
verification: Many things do not dwindle by losing the same amount each day, but the same FRACTION -- half, then half of that, then half again. After one such span (a half-life) half remains; after two, a quarter; after three, an eighth. It is the one rule behind a fading echo, a draining battery, a medicine clearing the blood, and the dust of old stars. The engine confirmed the very same quarter-remaining in a decaying nucleus and in an emptying circuit, and refused the claim that half would still be left. Because each step takes only a fraction, the thing thins forever yet never strictly reaches nothing.
wisdom: All earthly things pass, and pass by measure; what is fading does not vanish in a day but thins like grass before the wind -- so hold the passing loosely, and the abiding fast. "As for man, his days are as grass... the wind passeth over it, and it is gone" (Psalm 103:15-16).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_exponential_decay_two_witnesses. The sealed proof shows exponential decay loses a fixed fraction per interval: a quarter remains after two half-lives.

title: The steepest relief comes first; the last of it lingers longest
verification: A thing that fades by a fraction fades FASTEST when there is most of it to lose -- a scalding cup cools quickest while it is hottest, a debt at interest shrinks fastest when largest, a fever or a fresh grief eases most in the early going. Then, as little remains, the easing slows, and the last traces linger far longer than you would guess. The engine confirmed this falling curve across two unrelated systems. Take heart in the steep early relief; be patient with the long, slow tail.
wisdom: The sharpest of a sorrow is often the soonest to pass, and though a trace may linger long, the morning does come; bear the slow remainder in hope, for the worst is already behind. "Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning" (Psalm 30:5).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_exponential_decay_two_witnesses. The sealed proof shows exponential relaxation is fastest when the quantity is greatest, then slows toward a long tail.

title: Whatever grows by a fixed percent doubles on a fixed clock -- savings and debt alike
verification: Anything that grows by the same PERCENT each year has a fixed doubling time, no matter how small it starts. A handy rule: divide seventy-two by the yearly percent and you have the years to double -- money at seven percent doubles in about ten years, and so does a debt at seven percent. The engine confirmed this same runaway law in a decaying nucleus and a growing bank balance. That is the blessing and the danger together: a little saved keeps doubling into much, and a little owed keeps doubling into ruin. Compounding works tirelessly -- for you, or against you.
wisdom: Beware the debt that quietly doubles, for what compounds against you will master you in time; far better to be the one to whom increase comes than the one it devours. "The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender" (Proverbs 22:7).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_growth_is_decay_two_witnesses. The sealed proof shows a fixed percent gives a fixed doubling/halving time (7%/yr doubles in ~10 yr; Rule of 72).

title: Growth and decay are one motion, run two ways
verification: The slow doubling that fills an account and the slow halving that empties a fading sample are not two different things -- they are one law with its direction reversed: a fixed fraction gained or lost in every fixed season. The same patient clock lifts the saver and empties the spendthrift, builds the diligent and wears down the neglectful. The engine confirmed the single engine working in a nucleus and in a bank. To see this is to be sober about both: the gain that blesses and the loss that warns tick by the very same rate.
wisdom: There is one law over the field and the heart alike: a little tended grows by seasons into much, and a little neglected falls away by the same measure. What you put in, in time you take out. "Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap" (Galatians 6:7).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_growth_is_decay_two_witnesses. The sealed proof shows growth and decay are the same exponential law with the rate sign flipped (e^(-ln2)=1/2; doubling is its inverse).

title: When the stream speeds up, it presses less
verification: It seems backwards, but fast-moving water or air pushes SOFTER, not harder. Narrow a pipe and the water inside must hurry through -- and as it speeds up, its sideways push (its pressure) drops. The same hidden rule lifts a roof in a gale (wind racing over the top presses down less than the still air beneath, so the roof is pushed UP) and sucks a shower curtain inward. It is no special magic of fluids, only energy keeping its books: speed is bought with pressure. The engine confirmed the identical ledger in flowing water and in a falling weight.
wisdom: The unseen workings of moving air and water are real though hidden, and the wise respect what they cannot see at work; you mark the effect even when the cause slips past the eye. "The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh" (John 3:8).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_bernoulli_is_energy_conservation. The sealed proof shows Bernoulli is energy conservation: when flow speeds up, pressure must drop so the total head stays fixed.

title: What is given up in one form is taken back in another -- nothing is lost on the way
verification: Watch water fall down a pipe and you might think it loses something on the way -- but it loses nothing. Its height turns into pressure, its pressure into speed, and the full sum is kept faithfully from top to bottom, the same total only spent in a different coin. The engine confirmed this exact bookkeeping in a fluid and in a falling stone: ten units at rest as height, ten in motion as speed, never nine, never eleven. So it often is with what we seem to surrender; it is not gone, only changed in form.
wisdom: Take heart that what is laid down in one shape is not destroyed but transformed; the Keeper of all loses nothing entrusted to Him, but renders it back in its season, whole. "This is the Father's will... that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing" (John 6:39).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_bernoulli_is_energy_conservation. The sealed proof shows the total head is conserved (10 m as height = 10 m as pressure = the same energy) -- form changes, the sum is kept.

title: Most gather near the middle; the far extremes are few
verification: Measure the heights of a crowd, the scores on a test, the errors in a hundred careful measurements, and the same humped shape appears: a great mass piled near the average, thinning out to a few at either far end. About nineteen in twenty fall within two steps of the center, and only a rare one in a hundred lies beyond two and a half. The engine confirmed these exact cutoffs, the famous bell curve, agreed upon by two separate fields at once. So remember when you meet a startling extreme that it is, by its nature, uncommon -- most of life clusters near the middle.
wisdom: There is quiet wisdom in the middle portion -- neither the want of the lowest nor the surfeit of the highest -- and most are given to walk there; do not despise the ordinary lot, for it is where grace most often keeps us. "Give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me" (Proverbs 30:8).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_normal_distribution_prob_stats. The sealed proof confirms the normal curve: ~95% within ~2 steps (z=1.96) of the mean, ~99% within ~2.6 (z=2.576).

title: A thousand small chances settle into a shape you can read -- chance, but not chaos
verification: Toss enough coins, gather enough random little outcomes, and something astonishing happens: the jumble does not stay a jumble. It settles into one smooth, regular curve so dependable that you can read a precise percentage straight off it. The engine confirmed this same curve from two directions at once -- the gambler reckoning odds and the scientist judging a result both land on it. Underneath what looks like pure randomness lies a hidden order; the world is uncertain in its parts, yet lawful in the whole. Chance, it turns out, is not chaos.
wisdom: What seems to us a scatter of accidents is, to the One who numbers all things, an order kept; He counts the uncountable and loses track of none, so that beneath the wildest randomness His government holds. "He telleth the number of the stars; he calleth them all by their names" (Psalm 147:4).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_normal_distribution_prob_stats. The sealed two-witness proof shows the normal curve bridges probability and statistics -- regular order emerging from many small chances.

title: Two who share a task finish faster than either alone -- and equals halve the time
verification: One pipe fills a tank in three hours, another in six; set them flowing together and the tank is full in just TWO -- faster than even the quicker pipe alone. That is the law of shared loads: their rates add, so the combined time is always less than the smallest single time, and two equal helpers cut the job to exactly half. The same arithmetic governs a lens bending light to a point and two wires sharing a current. The engine confirmed it on the very numbers (a third plus a sixth makes a half) and refused a false answer. Joined effort does not merely add -- it lightens.
wisdom: A burden divided is more than halved when willing hands take hold together; what would wear out one is borne lightly by two, and the load on each grows less. "Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ" (Galatians 6:2).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_thin_lens_is_reciprocal_addition. The sealed proof shows reciprocal-addition (1/3+1/6=1/2): shared rates combine so the whole is faster than the fastest part.

title: Learn one shape and you read many problems at a glance
verification: The same hidden form turns up again and again: an eyeglass focusing light, two wires sharing a current, springs linked end to end, pumps sharing a load -- all of them combine by the very same rule, where it is the RECIPROCALS that add. Learn that one shape and a lens problem and a circuit problem stop being two problems and become one. The engine confirmed the lens as physics and the plain fraction-adding as arithmetic, one identity beneath both. To recognize the form beneath many faces is to see much with little.
wisdom: Blessed is the trained eye that spots the one pattern running through many things, for it understands quickly what others puzzle over long; such seeing is a gift to be prized. "But blessed are your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear" (Matthew 13:16).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_thin_lens_is_reciprocal_addition. The sealed proof shows lenses, parallel resistors, and the harmonic mean share one reciprocal-addition form.

title: To move more of anything, push harder or clear the path
verification: Whatever flows -- the current in a wire, water through gravel, heat through a wall -- obeys one plain rule: the amount that gets through equals how hard it is pushed, divided by how much the path resists. So there are only ever two ways to move more: raise the push, or lower the resistance. The engine confirmed this same shape in electricity (current is voltage over resistance) and in groundwater (flow is the slope times how freely the soil lets water pass), two unrelated things keeping one law. Know the rule and you know which lever to pull.
wisdom: Where you would see more good move through a life or a people, you may either press harder or smooth the way; both increase the stream, but the second tires no one out. "But let judgment run down as waters, and righteousness as a mighty stream" (Amos 5:24).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_transport_law_ohm_darcy. The sealed proof shows flow = drive / resistance for both electric current (Ohm) and groundwater (Darcy).

title: When little gets through, clear the channel before you push harder
verification: Because flow is the push divided by the resistance, a clogged path starves the stream no matter how hard you shove. Often the wiser remedy is not more force but less hindrance -- widen the pipe, clear the silt, mend the broken wire -- and the same effort suddenly carries far more. The engine confirmed that the path matters as much as the push, in current and in water alike. Before straining harder against a thing that barely moves, ask what is in the way.
wisdom: Do not merely strive harder against a stopped-up way; take up the stone that blocks it, for half of helping is the clearing of obstacles. "Cast ye up, cast ye up, prepare the way, take up the stumblingblock out of the way of my people" (Isaiah 57:14).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_transport_law_ohm_darcy. The sealed transport law (flow = drive / resistance) shows lowering resistance raises flow as surely as raising the drive.

title: Add a few more people and the bonds between them multiply far faster
verification: In a room of ten, how many handshakes pass between them? Each of the ten greets nine others, but every handshake is shared by two, so it is ten times nine over two -- forty-five. Add just one more person and you add not one bond but ten. The number of relationships grows almost as the SQUARE of the number of people: double the company and you nearly quadruple the ties to keep. The engine confirmed the exact count and refused a wrong one. This is why a small fellowship stays close while a large one strains -- the bonds outrun the bodies.
wisdom: One heart cannot hold an endless multitude of close bonds; as a people grows, the wise divide it into smaller circles where each can still be known, lest the burden of caring for all crush the few. "Provide out of all the people able men... and place such over them, to be rulers of thousands, and rulers of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens" (Exodus 18:21).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_pairs_are_triangular_numbers. The sealed proof shows pairs among n people = n(n-1)/2 (45 for ten) -- growing nearly as n squared.

title: To count the pairs, just add the whole numbers in order
verification: Here is a tidy gift for mental arithmetic. To count the handshakes among ten people you need only add one plus two plus three on up to nine -- and there is a schoolboy trick for that: fold the list and pair the ends, one with nine, two with eight, and so on, giving five nines, which is forty-five. The plain act of counting who greets whom turns out to be the oldest running sum there is. The engine confirmed the two are one and the same. Learn to number a little cleverly, and much becomes easy in the head.
wisdom: It was a child who first saw the quick way to add the long row of numbers, while his elders labored; God delights to hide a plain truth from the lofty and show it to the lowly. "I thank thee, O Father... because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes" (Matthew 11:25).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_pairs_are_triangular_numbers. The sealed proof shows counting pairs equals summing 1..(n-1) (Gauss): 1+...+9 = 45.

title: The speedometer tells your speed this instant -- not the average of the whole trip
verification: There is a difference between how fast you have gone on average and how fast you are going RIGHT NOW. A four-hour drive may average fifty miles an hour, yet at this very second the needle may read zero at a light or seventy on the open road. Mathematics has an exact tool for that instantaneous rate -- the slope of the curve at a single point -- and the engine recomputes such slopes precisely rather than trusting a textbook. The lesson carries over: judge a thing not only by its long average but by which way, and how fast, it is moving at this moment.
wisdom: Do not weigh a soul only by the slow average of its years; ask which way it is turning today, for the present moment is where repentance and grace meet. "Behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation" (2 Corinthians 6:2).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_calculus_derivatives_verified. The sealed proof recomputes exact instantaneous rates (derivatives) symbolically rather than trusting a table.

title: Getting the direction right matters as much as getting the size right
verification: When the engine checked the rules of slopes, it accepted the true ones and rejected one that looked almost identical -- because the real answer carried a MINUS sign the claim had dropped. A right size pointed the wrong way is still wrong; an answer off by only a sign can be the exact opposite of the truth. The engine confirmed five rules and refused the sign-flipped pretender. So in any reckoning, do not be content that the magnitude is near -- make sure it points the right way, for direction is half of truth.
wisdom: A path may look correct in every measure and still lead the wrong way; check not only how far but whither, for the plausible road can run opposite to life. "There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death" (Proverbs 14:12).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_calculus_derivatives_verified. The sealed proof accepted five derivative rules and refused d/dx cos = sin (true value is -sin) -- a sign error inverts the truth.

title: Before you trust the numbers, check that the units match
verification: Here is a free sanity check that catches mistakes before any arithmetic. In any honest formula, the KIND of thing on the left must be the same kind as on the right -- a length must equal a length, a force a force. You can spot a wrong law by its units alone: a claim that force equals mass times speed falls apart at once, because mass times speed is a momentum, not a force, and the two cannot be equal whatever the figures. The engine confirmed the sound laws (force is mass times acceleration; energy is mass times the speed of light squared) and rejected the unit-mismatched pretender. Truth in dimension comes before truth in amount.
wisdom: Look well to what a thing IS before you reckon how much; the diligent know the nature of their goods before they count them, and so are not fooled by a tidy-looking sum. "Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds" (Proverbs 27:23).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_dimensional_analysis_verified. The sealed proof checks units first: F=ma and E=mc^2 balance; F=mv is rejected (mass*velocity is momentum, not force).

title: Weigh like against like -- you cannot set apples against gallons
verification: Much foolish reckoning comes from setting unlike things against each other -- as if you could equate a distance with a weight, or hours with dollars, and tally them up. Nature does not allow it: a force can never equal a momentum, no matter the numbers, because they are different kinds of thing. The engine confirmed that an equation must first match in the KIND it measures, and refused one that did not. So before you argue that one thing outweighs another, make sure the two are even the same sort of thing -- else the whole comparison is empty.
wisdom: Use the right measure for each kind of thing, and do not confound them; a just length, a just weight, a just measure -- each in its own kind -- is the beginning of honest dealing. "Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have" (Leviticus 19:36).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_dimensional_analysis_verified. The sealed proof shows two sides of an equation must be the same dimension/kind; mismatched kinds (force vs momentum) are rejected.

title: Every even number seems to be two primes added -- one hundred is three plus ninety-seven
verification: Pick any even number above two and you can, it seems, always split it into two prime numbers added together: one hundred is three plus ninety-seven, a thousand is three plus nine hundred ninety-seven, a million is seventeen plus nine hundred ninety-nine thousand nine hundred eighty-three. People have checked this for even numbers past the billions and it has NEVER once failed. The engine sealed several of these splits, each checkable by hand. And yet -- here is the honest part -- no one has ever PROVEN it must hold for every even number forever. It is a thing that has not broken, which is not the same as a thing that cannot.
wisdom: We may trace a pattern far up the mountain and still not see its summit; the deep things run past our finding out, and wisdom marvels at the order without pretending to have measured it all. "O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God!... his ways past finding out!" (Romans 11:33).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_goldbach_conjecture_verified. The sealed proof verifies several even numbers as sums of two primes (100=3+97, a million=17+999983); it is a CONJECTURE, verified vastly but never proven.

title: A thing tested a thousand times and never broken is still not a thing proven
verification: There is a real difference between what has held so far and what must hold always. A rule may pass every test you can reach -- a million trials, a billion -- and still, the very next case might be the one that breaks it; until you have shown WHY it can never fail, you have not proved it, only failed to disprove it. The engine keeps this line exactly: it confirms and seals each case it can check, and refuses to claim the proof it does not have. To verify faithfully AND to confess the limit honestly are one and the same virtue.
wisdom: Know the edge of your own knowing, and do not dress a strong hope in the clothes of certainty; the humble confess what they have not searched out, and are nearer the truth for it. "Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?" (Job 11:7).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_goldbach_conjecture_verified. The sealed proof embodies the engine discipline: verify each reachable case, never claim the unproven whole (Goldbach remains open).

title: Three along, four across, five on the slant -- and the corner is square
verification: Here is a builder trick older than any level or square. To make a true right-angle corner -- for a garden bed, a foundation, a quilt -- measure three units along one edge and four along the other, then check the straight line between those marks: if it is exactly five, the corner is perfectly square. The engine confirmed this and its cousins (five-twelve-thirteen, eight-fifteen-seventeen) from both sides at once. A corner you can SEE is told true by numbers you can COUNT. No fancy tool needed -- just a tape and three numbers.
wisdom: Build true from the corner out, for everything raised upon a crooked beginning leans; lay the cornerstone square and the whole house stands. "And are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the chief corner stone" (Ephesians 2:20).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_pythagoras_shape_and_number. The sealed proof confirms 3-4-5 (and 5-12-13, 8-15-17) make right angles -- a corner is square exactly when a^2+b^2=c^2.

title: What you can see is ruled by what you can count
verification: A carpenter draws a corner; a child adds two squares; and they arrive at the very same truth -- the right angle IS the equation, and the equation IS the right angle. Shape and number are not two separate subjects here but one thing spoken in two tongues. The engine confirmed it from both sides, the drawn and the reckoned, on the same triangles. That a thing you can SEE should answer so exactly to a thing you can COUNT is among the oldest hints that the world was written in a single, consistent hand.
wisdom: The visible world is no random scatter; it is governed by an order you cannot see but can reason out, and that order points beyond itself to its Author. "For the invisible things of him... are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made" (Romans 1:20).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_pythagoras_shape_and_number. The sealed two-witness proof shows a right angle (shape) and a^2+b^2=c^2 (number) are one truth, confirmed from both sides.

title: In any change, nothing is made or lost -- only rearranged
verification: Burn a log to ash and smoke, and it looks as though most of it has vanished -- but not one atom is gone. Every speck of the wood has merely flown off as gas or settled as ash; counted up, the matter before and after is exactly equal. This is the bedrock of chemistry: across any real change, every atom that goes in must come out, only recombined into new things. The engine confirmed it for water, for burning fuel, for the very sugar your body burns -- and rejected an equation that tried to conjure atoms from nowhere. Nothing is created, nothing destroyed; it is all only turned into something else.
wisdom: Even what seems to perish is not annihilated but transformed and returned; the body goes back to the ground it came from, kept whole in the hand of its Maker until He calls for it again. "In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground... for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return" (Genesis 3:19).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_balanced_equations_verified. The sealed proof shows every atom is conserved across a reaction (water, combustion balanced); an atom-creating equation is rejected.

title: The books must close: you cannot get more out than went in
verification: When the engine checks a chemical recipe, it counts every atom on each side, and the two tallies MUST match -- a claim that six units of hydrogen come out where only four went in is refused outright, no matter how tidy it looks. Nature keeps honest books: nothing comes from nowhere, and what you draw out had to be put in. The engine confirmed the balanced recipes and rejected the one that did not add up. So mistrust any promise -- of profit, of yield, of effort spared -- that quietly produces more than its ingredients can hold.
wisdom: You cannot draw out of a thing what was never in it; a pure stream rises no higher than its spring, and an honest accounting brings forth only what was truly there. "Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one" (Job 14:4).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_balanced_equations_verified. The sealed proof rejects an unbalanced equation (6 H cannot come from 4) -- the atom counts must match exactly.

title: Crowd things evenly around a center and they settle at fixed angles, every time
verification: The shape of a molecule is not a chemistry secret -- it is plain geometry. Count how many groups crowd around the central atom and the angle between them is fixed: two spread to a straight line, three to a flat triangle, four to the gentle tetrahedral splay of about a hundred-ten degrees, six to right angles all around. The engine confirmed these same four angles two ways at once -- by counting electron pairs and by spacing points evenly on a sphere. Matter takes its form from a mathematics it never had to choose; push things apart fairly and they find their appointed places.
wisdom: Everything finds the place that was set for it; the stars keep their courses and the least particle its angle, by a decree that does not pass away. "He hath also stablished them for ever and ever: he hath made a decree which shall not pass" (Psalm 148:6).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_molecular_shape_is_geometry. The sealed two-witness proof shows bond angles are fixed by geometry alone: 2->180, 3->120, 4->109.5, 6->90.

title: A tiny bend in the water molecule is part of why anything lives
verification: A water molecule is not straight -- it is bent, by the same fixed geometry that sets every molecule its shape. That little crook is no small thing: it is why ice floats instead of sinking (so ponds freeze on top and life endures beneath), why water clings and climbs and dissolves the salts a body needs. The engine confirmed the family of fixed angles that govern such shapes. So much of what keeps us alive hangs on a bend of a few degrees in a thing too small to see -- a reminder that the great rests on the carefully-made small.
wisdom: The Maker who upholds the vast also tends the unseen and the slight, and on His care for the smallest detail the whole of life is hung; not a sparrow falls without Him. "Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing?... the very hairs of your head are all numbered" (Matthew 10:29-30).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_molecular_shape_is_geometry. The sealed proof confirms the fixed bond angles behind molecular shapes; water bends rather than lying straight.

title: Past a certain slant, light cannot leave the water -- it turns back in
verification: A diver looking up sees something strange: straight overhead, a bright round window to the sky -- but tilt your gaze toward the side and the surface turns to a mirror, throwing back the bottom. The reason is exact: once light inside the water tries to escape at a slant steeper than about forty-two degrees, it cannot get out at all and reflects entirely back inward. The engine confirmed this critical angle and refused a wrong one. The very same trapping of light, bounced along inside a hair-thin glass thread, is how the world carries the internet -- mile after mile on light that is never allowed to leave.
wisdom: Light keeps to laws and ways we did not set and can scarcely trace, dwelling and travelling by appointment; how little of even the common we truly comprehend. "Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness, where is the place thereof?" (Job 38:19).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_optics_tir_photon_verified. The sealed proof confirms the critical angle for total internal reflection (41.8 deg water-to-air); a 30-degree claim broke.

title: What is kept faithfully within its channel travels farthest
verification: A single thread of glass can carry a beam of light across a city -- even under an ocean -- because the light is trapped: it strikes the walls too steeply to leak out, and so bounces onward, mile upon mile, scarcely dimming. Let it scatter loosely and it would die in a few feet; kept faithfully inside its narrow way, it reaches the far shore intact. The engine confirmed the law that governs this gathering and holding of light. So with anything precious entrusted to you: what is guarded and kept to its course arrives; what is leaked and scattered is lost.
wisdom: Guard well what is committed to your keeping and let it not leak away in idle scattering; a trust held faithfully reaches the one it was meant for. "O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust" (1 Timothy 6:20).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_optics_tir_photon_verified. The sealed proof confirms total internal reflection and fiber light-gathering (numerical aperture) -- trapped light is carried far.

title: There are far more ways to choose a few from many than you would guess
verification: Out of six people you can pick a pair in fifteen different ways; out of ten you can pick five in two hundred fifty-two. The number of ways to choose climbs steeply as the group grows -- which is exactly why a lottery is so nearly hopeless: the winning few must be drawn from so very many possible choices. The engine counts these from first principles, getting the one exact answer and refusing a wrong one. When you marvel that a particular hand or draw came up, remember how many other hands could have.
wisdom: Out of the great multitude, a few are chosen -- and the choosing is no small or careless thing, but precise and purposed; walk worthy, then, of having been picked from among so many. "For many are called, but few are chosen" (Matthew 22:14).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_binomial_factorial_verified. The sealed proof confirms binomial counts (6 choose 2 = 15, 10 choose 5 = 252) exactly, recomputed not trusted.

title: Arrange only a handful of things and the orderings run past counting
verification: Set four books on a shelf and there are twenty-four ways to order them; seven books, five thousand and forty ways; eight, over forty thousand. The count of arrangements multiplies so fiercely that a single shuffled deck of cards has more possible orders than there are stars we can see -- so the deck in your hands has almost certainly never, in all of history, been arranged in just that way before. The engine computes these counts exactly and refused one that was off by a few hundred. From a mere handful springs a near-infinity of order.
wisdom: The possibilities that open from even small things are beyond our reckoning, and the thoughts and works of God outrun every counting; we stand before a vastness we did not make and cannot number. "How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God!... if I should count them, they are more in number than the sand" (Psalm 139:17-18).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_binomial_factorial_verified. The sealed proof confirms factorial growth (4!=24, 7!=5040, 8!=40320) exactly; an off-by-a-bit factorial was rejected.

title: A one-in-twenty result happens by luck about one time in twenty
verification: When scientists call a finding significant at the common bar, here is what it actually means: IF nothing real were going on, a result this striking would still turn up by sheer chance about one time in twenty. That is a useful guard -- but it is not proof. One in twenty such findings can be a fluke, dressed up to look like a discovery. The engine recomputes these thresholds from the math itself (a certain standard score really does give five-in-a-hundred, not one-in-two) and refuses an inflated claim. Before you trust a surprising result, ask how often plain luck would have produced it.
wisdom: Do not believe every striking sign at once, for some wonders are only chance in fine clothing; weigh the spirit of a thing, and test whether it is truly of God or merely of the moment. "Beloved, believe not every spirit, but try the spirits whether they are of God" (1 John 4:1).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_statistics_pvalues_verified. The sealed proof recomputes p-values (z=1.96 -> 0.05) and rejects an inflated claim -- a 0.05 result occurs ~1-in-20 by chance.

title: Try enough things and one will pass by luck -- so count all your tries
verification: If a fluke turns up about one time in twenty, then test twenty unrelated things and you should EXPECT one of them to look impressive by luck alone. This is the oldest way people fool themselves: try many remedies, many hunches, many predictions, then trumpet the one that seemed to work and quietly forget the rest. The honest course is to count every attempt, not just the winner -- the more you tried, the less a single hit means. The engine weighs evidence by recomputation, refusing the convenient number. Beware the lucky success pulled from a heap of forgotten failures.
wisdom: Guard against deceiving your own heart, which is quick to keep the flattering result and bury the rest; honest reckoning counts the misses as faithfully as the hits. "For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself" (Galatians 6:3).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_statistics_pvalues_verified. The sealed proof underlies the multiple-comparisons guard: at a 1-in-20 bar, ~1 of 20 tests passes by chance; honest analysis counts all tries.

title: Matter is built level by level, each holding an exact and countable number
verification: The whole vast variety of substances -- iron, oxygen, gold, salt -- comes from one tidy rule: the tiny charges inside an atom fill up in shells, and each shell holds an exact count, two then eight then eighteen then thirty-two, never a fraction and never one more. Matter is assembled in discrete, countable steps, like courses of brick, and the orderly filling of those shells is the very reason the elements line up as they do in the chemist table. The engine computed these capacities rather than trusting the textbook, and rejected a too-small count. There is a quiet, exact arithmetic underneath the stuff of the world.
wisdom: Nothing in creation is heaped up at random; each thing is given its measure and its place, weighed out by a hand that does not err. "To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure" (Job 28:25).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_atomic_shells_configs_verified. The sealed proof confirms shell capacities 2n^2 (2,8,18,32) exactly; a too-small count was rejected.

title: A true account keeps the exceptions; it does not force them to fit
verification: The neat rule for how an atom fills its shells works almost everywhere -- but a few elements, like chromium, quietly break it, arranging themselves against the tidy pattern. The honest thing is not to pretend they obey, but to record the real exception as it stands. The engine did exactly that: it confirmed chromium in its odd, true arrangement, because it carries the genuine exceptions rather than smoothing them over. A map that hides what defies it is a false map. Love the rule, but love the truth more -- and keep what will not fit.
wisdom: Speak and record what is actually so, not the tidier tale you wish were so; the one who deals truly, exceptions and all, is trusted, while the smoother of facts is found out. "Lying lips are abomination to the LORD: but they that deal truly are his delight" (Proverbs 12:22).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_atomic_shells_configs_verified. The sealed proof confirms chromium 3d5 4s1 -- the engine carries the real exceptions instead of forcing the naive filling order.

title: Four marvels of the heavens turn out to be one small triangle, seen four ways
verification: Four of the strangest truths ever discovered about space and time -- that fast clocks run slow, that fast rulers run short, that all observers agree on one deep measure though they agree on nothing else, and that a thing energy and its motion are two sides of one quantity -- look like four separate wonders. They are not. They are a single right triangle, the plain three-four-five of a carpenter, read from four sides. The engine confirmed that one figure generates them all, and refused a false one. What seemed four mysteries is one shape; the deep laws are fewer than they first appear.
wisdom: Many members, one body; many marvels, one truth beneath them -- so do not be dazzled by the multitude of wonders into missing the single hand that orders them all. "For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body" (1 Corinthians 12:12).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_relativity_is_geometry. The sealed capstone shows time dilation, length contraction, the spacetime interval, and E=mc^2 are one 3-4-5 triangle read four ways.

title: See the shape beneath a mystery, and the strange begins to feel inevitable
verification: Relativity sounds like a string of arbitrary oddities -- until you find the plain triangle hiding underneath, and then it all turns necessary: of COURSE the clock slows and the ruler shrinks, for that is simply the geometry of a right triangle carried into time. Nothing was invented to make it strange; the strangeness was only the shape, unrecognized. The engine traced every part back to that one figure. So with many a hard thing: it bewilders while its form is hidden, and steadies the moment the order beneath it is seen.
wisdom: What confounds us in the dark grows plain when the light is given; the deep things are not random but ordered, and to be shown their pattern is to fear them less and trust their Maker more. "He revealeth the deep and secret things: he knoweth what is in the darkness, and the light dwelleth with him" (Daniel 2:22).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_relativity_is_geometry. The sealed capstone shows relativity is inevitable, not arbitrary -- the geometry of one right triangle carried into space and time.

title: Every labor of the machine is paid in one coin: the plain yes-or-no
verification: All the busy work of a computer -- to squeeze a file small, to guard a message against garbling, to put a list in order, to find one name among many -- comes down to a single humble coin: the bit, one yes-or-no answer. And the price of any such task is simply how many honest yes-or-no answers the truth of it requires; to pin down one thing out of eight equally likely takes exactly three. The engine confirmed this single accounting beneath five different tasks and refused a wrong count. Nothing can be had for fewer answers than the matter genuinely holds. (When the choices are not equally likely, the count is gentler, but the principle stands.)
wisdom: The smallest coin, faithfully counted, is the true measure of a gift; God reckons not by show but by the plain units actually given, and the least yes or no, rightly spent, is seen and weighed. "And there came a certain poor widow, and she threw in two mites... this poor widow hath cast more in, than all" (Mark 12:42-43).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_information_has_a_price. The sealed capstone shows compress/sort/search/error-correct are all bit-accounting: the price of pinning 1 of N equally-likely is log2(N).

title: Order, certainty, and safety are never free -- each is bought at its true price
verification: To bring order out of jumble, to make a thing sure, to guard it against loss -- none of these comes free, and none can be bargained below what the matter actually costs. The engine showed that every such gain is paid in plain yes-or-no answers, exactly as many as the truth of the thing contains, and not one fewer. No cleverness cheats the price; the books always balance. So when something promises certainty or order for nothing, suspect it -- the real cost has only been hidden, not escaped.
wisdom: What is truly worth having must be bought and held fast, not had for nothing and let slip; pay the price of wisdom gladly, and do not sell it cheap. "Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding" (Proverbs 23:23).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_information_has_a_price. The sealed capstone shows order/certainty/safety each cost a measurable number of bits; nothing is had for fewer.

title: The oldest writing in the world is not on stone or scroll, but in the cell
verification: Long before any tablet was carved, there was a writing inside every living thing: an alphabet of just four letters, set in words of three, spelling out the whole instruction for a body. And it is written with remarkable care -- many words have spare spellings, so a slip can be forgiven; the message rides on two matching threads, so if one is damaged the other restores it. The engine confirmed this digital, error-guarded code, the very same craft a computer uses for reliable messages, done first and best in the living cell. Life, at bottom, is a message -- written to be read, and re-read, and not lost.
wisdom: You are yourself a kind of living letter, inscribed not in ink but in something deeper, meant to be read by all who know you; take care, then, what is written there. "Ye are... the epistle of Christ... written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tables of stone, but in fleshy tables of the heart" (2 Corinthians 3:3).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_life_is_a_digital_code. The sealed capstone shows DNA is a digital, error-managing code (4^3=64 alphabet, triplet frame, redundant, two-strand error-correctable).

title: What is meant to last is built to survive being copied a thousand times
verification: The code of life is not merely written; it is written to ENDURE the wear of endless copying. It carries spare spellings so that a small mistake changes nothing, and it keeps a second matching copy alongside the first so that damage to one can be mended from the other. The engine confirmed this two-fold safeguard -- forgiveness built in, and a witness kept in reserve. The deepest lesson of the cell is for anything you wish to pass on: do not merely record it, but make it resilient -- tolerant of small errors, and held by more than one witness -- so it survives the generations.
wisdom: What is truly to be handed down must be kept faithfully and guarded against the corruption of years; the words that endure are those settled firmly and not left to one frail copy. "For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven" (Psalm 119:89).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_life_is_a_digital_code. The sealed capstone shows life is error-managed: redundant spellings (tolerance) plus two complementary strands (correction).

title: The same shape holds up a star, a lock, a song, and a seed
verification: Look closely and the same few truths keep turning up everywhere. The carpenter triangle behind the bending of starlight is the same triangle behind a particle energy; the hidden number that two and twelve share -- nothing -- both guards the lock on your bank and closes the circle of music; the reversible flip inside a computer is the same flip that lets one strand of life restore the other. Four subjects as far apart as physics, machines, music, and living cells, and one order runs beneath them all. The engine read these same structures in every field and refused a false one in each alike. (It READS this recurring order; it does not invent it, nor claim to have charted the whole of reality.)
wisdom: There is one hand behind the many works, one Father through and in them all, so that the deep agreement of distant things is no accident but a signature. "One God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all" (Ephesians 4:6).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of the GRAND capstone. The sealed proof shows the SAME verified structures (triangle, coprimality, involution, digital 4^3=64) recur across physics, CS, music, and genetics.

title: We are mapmakers, not authors -- we trace the order, we did not write it
verification: When the same deep pattern turns up in a star and a song, we have not cleverly forced the likeness -- we have stumbled on an order that was already there, waiting in every place we thought to look. That is the whole humility of honest work: to draw the map faithfully, and never to sketch in a road that is not really there. The map is not the land, and the land was not made by the mapmaker. The engine keeps to this exactly -- confirming only what genuinely recurs, refusing to claim the rest, and never dressing a guess as a proof. To map the world rightly is to confess that Someone else laid the country out.
wisdom: Remember your place before the works of God: you arrived in a world already framed, whose measures you did not set and whose foundations you did not lay; trace them in awe, and do not pretend they are yours. "Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth?... or who hath stretched the line upon it?" (Job 38:4-5).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of the GRAND capstone honest boundary: the engine READS the recurring order, it does not author it; we map, never invent.

title: That the world can be read at all is the deepest wonder of it
verification: Stranger than any single law of nature is that there ARE laws -- that one small handful of truths governs the farthest star and the smallest seed, that the world is consistent enough to be mapped in the first place. It did not have to be so; a true chaos could never be read. Everything the engine does leans on this quiet miracle: it finds one order across every field, and a falsehood breaks against every verifier alike. (THAT the same structures recur, it has checked and sealed; WHY the world is so faithfully consistent, it cannot prove -- it only reads the consistency, it did not make it.) The world is legible because it was spoken into being by reason itself.
wisdom: The universe is no accident of chaos but a cosmos held in coherence by the One who is before it and upholds it; that we can understand anything at all is His gift and His signature. "And he is before all things, and by him all things consist" (Colossians 1:17).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of the GRAND capstone central claim: the world is mappable/consistent (verified for the recurring structures); WHY it is so is not a theorem, only read -- pointing to one Author.

title: Located and verified -- everyday weather: dew point, heat index, wind chill (meteorology)
situation: The almanac core, made checkable. Dew point (Magnus-Tetens), saturation vapor pressure (Magnus), heat index (NWS Rothfusz), and wind chill (NWS 2001) are standard public formulas. Each is recomputed by the engine and confirmed against the claim, not taken on the page's word: T=25C/RH=60% gives a 16.7C dew point; cool moist air at 2C/90% dews at 0.6C (frost risk); 90F at 70% humidity feels like 106F; 20F with a 15 mph wind feels like 6F. Real numbers a farmer or gardener can use, each sealed.

pre_run.summary: 5 located meteorology claims through /derivation/verify: 5 CONFIRMED, sealed (HOLDS 5/5). Dew point and saturation vapor pressure by Magnus; heat index by Rothfusz (NWS); wind chill by NWS 2001. Each recomputed from the formula, not the claim; a false dew point (25C claimed where 16.7C is true) MISMATCHES.

domain_results.detail: dew point 25C/60%=16.7C and 2C/90%=0.6C; es(25C)=31.6 hPa; heat index 90F/70%=105.9F; wind chill 20F/15mph=6.2F

wisdom: The sky has always been readable to those who learn its signs, and the old weather-wisdom is not superstition but measurement: the dew, the mugginess, the biting wind each follow a known law. The Lord faulted not the reading of the sky but the failure to read the deeper signs. "When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red... O ye hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the signs of the times?" (Matthew 16:2-3).

title: The grass is wet at dawn because the night air cooled to its dew point
verification: Air can hold only so much moisture, and the warmer it is the more it holds. Let it cool, and at a certain temperature -- the dew point -- it can hold no more, and the surplus comes out as water: dew on the grass, fog in the hollow, beads on a cold glass. The engine computed it: air at seventy-seven degrees and sixty percent humidity reaches its dew point near sixty-two (twenty-five and sixteen-point-seven in Celsius). And if that dew point is below freezing, the dew comes as frost. The wet lawn is not magic; it is the night air wringing itself out.
wisdom: The gentlest things fall quietly in the cool and the dark, unseen until morning shows them; so grace and good teaching settle on a soul in its still hours. "My doctrine shall drop as the rain, my speech shall distil as the dew, as the small rain upon the tender herb" (Deuteronomy 32:2).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_meteorology_verified. The sealed proof computes the dew point (25C/60% -> 16.7C; frost when below freezing).

title: The wind makes the cold bite deeper than the thermometer says
verification: Your body keeps a thin film of warmed air against the skin like an invisible coat; the wind strips it away, and the cold rushes in faster, so a calm chill becomes a cruel one. It is measurable: twenty degrees with a fifteen-mile wind feels like six. The engine confirmed the standard reckoning. So dress not for the number on the thermometer but for the wind on top of it -- the air does not lie, but it does not tell the whole story either.
wisdom: There are trials whose bare measure looks bearable until the wind of circumstance drives them home; have compassion, then, on the one who shivers where you would not, for you do not feel the wind that finds him. "He casteth forth his ice like morsels: who can stand before his cold?" (Psalm 147:17).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_meteorology_verified. The sealed proof confirms wind chill (20F + 15mph wind feels like 6F).

title: It is not only the heat but the humidity -- damp air will not let you cool
verification: You cool yourself by sweating, for as the sweat dries it carries the heat away -- but only if the air is dry enough to take it. On a muggy day the air is already near full of water and cannot accept much more, so the sweat lingers and you bake in your own heat. That is why ninety degrees in damp air can feel like a hundred and six. The engine confirmed the standard heat-index figure. The danger of a hot day is hidden in how wet the air is.
wisdom: Be a shade to the weary in the scorching season, for the heat that one bears lightly may overcome another; the merciful are a covering when the sun is cruel. "For thou hast been... a refuge from the storm, a shadow from the heat" (Isaiah 25:4).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_meteorology_verified. The sealed proof confirms the heat index (90F/70% humidity feels like ~106F).

title: Located and verified -- the gardener's rules: soil pH, hardiness, crop rotation (agriculture)
situation: Practical growing wisdom, made checkable against USDA / Cooperative-Extension reference data. The engine confirmed that a tomato is hardy in zone 7b and likes a near-neutral pH 6.5; that a blueberry craves acid soil (pH 4.8, in its 4.5-5.5 band) and FAILS the check at a sweet 6.8; that a potato tolerates sour ground (pH 5.5); and that following tomato with corn is a sound rotation while tomato-after-potato BREAKS it (both are nightshades and share pests and disease). Reference-range checks, not universal law -- local advice varies -- but each claim here is recomputed against the documented range and sealed.

pre_run.summary: 5 located agriculture claims through /derivation/verify: 5 CONFIRMED, sealed (HOLDS 5/5). Hardiness-zone, soil-pH, and crop-rotation checks against USDA/Extension public data. False controls MISMATCH: blueberry at pH 6.8 (too alkaline for its 4.5-5.5 range) and a tomato->potato rotation (both Solanaceae, adjacent same-family).

domain_results.detail: tomato hardy zone 7b (range 4a-11b); tomato pH 6.5 (6.0-6.8); blueberry pH 4.8 (4.5-5.5, acid-loving); potato pH 5.5 (5.0-6.5); rotation tomato->corn sound (different families)

wisdom: Good husbandry is taught wisdom, not luck: the one who knows his ground and his seasons is being instructed by the same God who set the laws he plants by. "Doth the plowman plow all day to sow?... For his God doth instruct him to discretion, and doth teach him" (Isaiah 28:24-26).

title: Match the crop to the ground -- blueberries crave sour soil, most greens a near-neutral one
verification: A seed cannot thrive in soil its roots cannot drink from. Each plant wants its own sourness or sweetness of earth, measured as pH: a blueberry pines for acid ground (about 4.5 to 5.5) and sickens in sweet soil, while a tomato or lettuce wants it near the middle, around six and a half. The engine checked these against the growers' tables and confirmed them -- and flagged a blueberry set in sweet soil as a plant that will fail. So test your ground and feed it to the crop, for the same patch is good earth for one seed and barren for another.
wisdom: The same word falls on every kind of ground, but only the rightly-prepared heart bears the harvest; tend the soil of the soul as a wise gardener tends his beds. "But other fell on good ground, and sprang up, and bare fruit an hundredfold" (Luke 8:8).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_agriculture_verified. The sealed proof confirms crop soil-pH ranges (blueberry 4.5-5.5 acid; tomato ~6.5).

title: Do not plant a crop where its cousin just stood
verification: Plants of one family share the same enemies. Set a tomato where a potato grew last year and you spread the table afresh for the very pests and blights that family invites, for the tomato and the potato are kin -- both nightshades. The engine confirmed the rule: tomato followed by corn (a stranger family) passes, while tomato after potato breaks it. Rotate your beds so that each year a different family stands there, and the troubles that built up under the last crop find no welcome under the next.
wisdom: Even the land is to be given its change and its rest, not worked the same way without ceasing; there is wisdom and mercy in letting a thing lie fallow and come round again. "But in the seventh year shall be a sabbath of rest unto the land, a sabbath for the LORD" (Leviticus 25:4).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_agriculture_verified. The sealed proof confirms a sound rotation (tomato->corn) and breaks tomato->potato (both Solanaceae).

title: Plant what your winter will allow, not only what your heart desires
verification: Every plant has a cold it can survive and a cold it cannot. Gardeners reckon this by hardiness zones -- a map of how bitter the winters run -- and a crop's zone range tells you plainly whether it will live where you are. The engine confirmed, for instance, that a tomato is at home in zone 7b. Before you fall in love with a seed, learn the worst your winter does and whether the plant can bear it; longing does not soften the frost. Plant in season and within your bounds, and your labor is not wasted on the cold.
wisdom: There is a season set for everything, and the wise work with the times appointed rather than against them; do in its season what the season is for. "To every thing there is a season... a time to plant, and a time to pluck up that which is planted" (Ecclesiastes 3:1-2).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_agriculture_verified. The sealed proof confirms a crop's hardiness-zone tolerance (tomato hardy in 7b).

title: Located and verified -- the sea's rules: pressure with depth, wave speed, salinity (oceanography)
situation: Sea-wisdom made checkable against standard physical-oceanography references (NOAA / textbook). The engine confirmed that pressure climbs by about one atmosphere every ten metres of depth (about 2 atm at 10 m, near 11 at 100 m); that a deep-water wave's speed grows with its length (a 100-metre swell runs at 12.5 m/s, a 25-metre wave at only 6.2); that water of 35 parts-per-thousand salt is marine while 0.3 is fresh; and that a 3-metre tidal range is mesotidal. Standard formulas with standard seawater values -- real seas vary -- but each claim is recomputed and sealed; false claims (100 m at 2 atm, 35 ppt called fresh) break.

Each recomputed; false controls MISMATCH (100 m claimed at 2 atm; 35 ppt claimed fresh).
