"Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?" (Job 38:16).

title: Every ten metres down, the sea lays on another atmosphere of weight
verification: Slip beneath the waves and the water presses on you harder the deeper you go -- about one whole atmosphere more for every thirty-odd feet (ten metres). At the surface you bear one; ten metres down, two; a hundred metres down, eleven, the weight of a great hand closing. The engine confirmed the reckoning. This is why a diver must rise slowly and why the creatures of the deep are built so strangely: the depths are not gentle, and what is borne lightly at the top would crush at the bottom.
wisdom: Have a tender care for the one who has gone down into deep trouble, for a weight grows heavier the lower one sinks, and grief at its depths presses as the shallows never did. "Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?" (Job 38:16).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_oceanography_verified. The sealed proof shows pressure rises ~1 atm per 10 m of depth.

title: The long swell outruns the storm that made it, and reaches the shore first
verification: On the open sea the longest waves travel the fastest: a swell with crests a hundred metres apart runs at twelve and a half metres a second, while a short twenty-five-metre wave makes barely six. So when a great storm churns the far ocean, its long, slow swells race out ahead of it and roll onto distant beaches while the sky is still calm and blue -- an old warning to sailors that weather is coming. The engine confirmed the speeds. Heed the long swell; the storm that sent it is on its way.
wisdom: The wise read the quiet signs that run ahead of trouble and make ready, while the heedless wait for the storm itself; let the far swell teach you to prepare before the wind arrives. "A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished" (Proverbs 22:3).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_oceanography_verified. The sealed proof shows deep-water wave speed grows with wavelength (100 m at 12.5 m/s vs 25 m at 6.2).

title: Water is fresh, or salt, or the troubled brackish in between -- and one spring cannot give both
verification: The sea is salt -- about thirty-five parts of salt in a thousand of water -- and the river is fresh, under half a part; where they meet, in the river-mouth, the water turns brackish, neither one thing nor the other. The engine confirmed these lines plainly: thirty-five parts is marine, a third of a part is fresh. A single source yields its own kind of water and not another; you know a spring by what flows from it, sweet or salt, not by what it claims.
wisdom: As no fountain pours out both sweet and bitter, so a heart cannot be at once true and false; let your one spring run clean. "Doth a fountain send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?... so can no fountain both yield salt water and fresh" (James 3:11-12).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_oceanography_verified. The sealed proof confirms salinity classes: 35 ppt marine, 0.3 fresh.

title: Located and verified -- the calendar's rules: leap years, the day of the week (calendar_time)
situation: The almanac's own backbone, made checkable against the Gregorian civil calendar. The engine confirmed the leap-year rule with its famous century twist: 2024 is a leap year, 2023 is not, the year 2000 was (divisible by 400), but 2100 will NOT be (divisible by 100, not by 400) -- a correction upon a correction that keeps the calendar in step with the sun. It also confirmed that a given date falls on a knowable weekday (2 May 2026 is a Saturday) and that a day's worth of seconds added to a timestamp lands exactly one day later. Civil-calendar rules, each recomputed and sealed; a false leap-year claim (2100 called a leap year) breaks.

pre_run.summary: 6 located calendar_time claims through /derivation/verify: 6 CONFIRMED, sealed (HOLDS 6/6). Gregorian leap-year rule (2024 T, 2023 F, 2100 F, 2000 T), day-of-week derivation (2026-05-02 = Saturday), duration addition (+86400 s = +1 day). Each recomputed from the rule; a false claim (2100 leap=True) MISMATCHES.

domain_results.detail: leap: 2024 yes, 2023 no, 2000 yes (div 400), 2100 no (div 100 not 400); 2026-05-02 is Saturday; +86400 s = +1 day

wisdom: The seasons and the years were appointed by their Maker, and to keep a true calendar is to walk in step with His ordering of the lights; the careful reckoning of time is a kind of reverence. "He appointed the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down" (Psalm 104:19).

title: A leap day every four years -- but three century-years out of four are skipped
verification: The year is not a tidy number of days; the earth takes about three hundred sixty-five and a quarter to round the sun, so we add a day every fourth year to catch up. But a plain quarter is a hair too much, so the rule adds a second, finer correction: a century-year is NOT a leap year -- UNLESS it divides by four hundred. Thus 1900 was no leap year and 2100 will be none, yet 2000 was. The engine confirmed each case and refused a false one. A surprising rule, but it is what keeps the calendar from sliding out of step with the seasons over the long centuries.
wisdom: The works of God are exact to a degree we are tempted to round off; honor the small remainder you would gladly ignore, for it is often the thing that keeps the whole from drifting. "He appointed the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down" (Psalm 104:19).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_calendar_time_verified. The sealed proof confirms the Gregorian leap rule: 2024 yes, 2100 no (century not div by 400), 2000 yes.

title: A small correction, made faithfully, keeps a great thing from drifting astray
verification: Without the leap day, the calendar would creep a quarter-day off the sun every year, and in a few centuries spring would arrive in what the page called winter. A tiny correction each fourth year -- and a finer one each century -- holds it true for ages. The engine confirmed the rule works exactly. The lesson reaches past the calendar: small errors left alone do not stay small; they accumulate into a wide drift. The faithful make their little corrections in season, and so keep the long course straight.
wisdom: A small slip ignored becomes a great straying in time; give the more earnest heed to the steady things, and mend the little drift before it carries you far. "Therefore we ought to give the more earnest heed to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let them slip" (Hebrews 2:1).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_calendar_time_verified. The sealed leap-year rule is a periodic small correction that keeps the civil year aligned with the solar year.

situation: Household nutrition math, made checkable against USDA / National Academies (DRI) public references. The engine confirmed the calorie rule -- 4 calories in a gram of carbohydrate or protein, 9 in a gram of fat (so 50 g carb + 30 g protein + 20 g fat = 500 calories); the body-mass index (75 kg at 1.75 m gives BMI 24.5, in the normal band); the energy-balance arithmetic (1500 eaten against 2000 burned is a 500-calorie daily deficit); and an RDA check (90 mg of vitamin C meets the adult-male requirement). Reference math, not personal medical advice -- a body is more than a number -- but each figure here is recomputed and sealed; a wrong calorie total breaks.

"What? know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you... therefore glorify God in your body" (1 Corinthians 6:19-20).

title: Fat carries more than twice the fuel of bread or meat, gram for gram
verification: Not all food weighs the same in fuel. A gram of fat holds nine calories, while a gram of carbohydrate or of protein holds only four -- so a small drizzle of oil or pat of butter quietly carries more than twice the energy of the same weight of bread or lean meat. The engine confirmed the reckoning: fifty grams of carb, thirty of protein, and twenty of fat add up to five hundred calories, and a wrong total breaks. This is why the richest-seeming little additions are the ones that add up fastest on the plate.
wisdom: The richest fare is to be taken in measure, for a little of it goes a long way and too much turns sweet to sickness; know when enough is enough. "Hast thou found honey? eat so much as is sufficient for thee, lest thou be filled therewith, and vomit it" (Proverbs 25:16).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_nutrition_verified. The sealed proof confirms Atwater 4-4-9 calorie math (fat 9 kcal/g vs 4 for carb/protein).

title: Eat a little less than you spend, day after day, and the difference tells in time
verification: Weight is, at bottom, a ledger: take in fewer calories than you burn and the body draws on its stores; take in more and it lays them up. The engine confirmed the plain arithmetic -- fifteen hundred eaten against two thousand spent is a five-hundred-calorie daily deficit. No single day shows much, but a small gap kept steadily, by the common reckoning, sheds about a pound a week. The work is not in any one heroic day; it is in the small honest difference, repeated.
wisdom: The patient and steady reach plenty, while the hasty who would have it all at once come to want; let small, diligent restraint do its slow and certain work. "The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want" (Proverbs 21:5).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas located_nutrition_verified. The sealed proof confirms energy-balance arithmetic (1500 - 2000 = -500 kcal/day deficit).

title: The average rate of change over an interval is one idea, read in many sciences
situation: The rate of change of a function over an interval -- the slope of the straight line joining its endpoints, (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a) -- is a single arithmetic that wears different names in different fields. In pure mathematics it is the secant slope; the engine confirmed it for f(x)=x^2 on [1,3] (slope 4) and proved the general identity (b^2-a^2)/(b-a) = a+b. In PHYSICS the very same quotient is average velocity: a falling body whose position is 4.9t^2 has average speed 19.6 m/s over [1,3] s. In CHEMISTRY (and so in organic reaction kinetics) it is the average reaction rate: a reactant falling from 8 to 2 units over 3 seconds reacts at 2 units per second. One formula, three readings -- distance over time, change of concentration over time, rise over run.
use: This is the everyday bridge between calculus and the lab. Wherever you ask 'how fast, on average, did this change?' you are taking a secant slope: miles per hour, pounds per week, molarity per second, degrees per minute. The instantaneous rate (the derivative, sealed separately) is the limit of this as the interval shrinks to a point; the average rate is what you can measure directly from two snapshots. HONEST BOUNDARY: the average over a span is not the value at any single instant -- it smooths the journey; only in the limit does it become the rate 'right now'.

pre_run.summary: Cross-domain co-confirmation on prod (HOLDS 4/4, sealed). mathematics confirmed the secant slope (3^2-1^2)/(3-1)=4 and the symbolic identity (b^2-a^2)/(b-a)=a+b; the same quotient read as physics average velocity (4.9*3^2-4.9*1^2)/(2)=19.6 m/s and as chemistry average reaction rate -(2-8)/(3-0)=2 units/s. A false secant slope (claimed 5) BROKE.

domain_results.detail: secant slope of x^2 on [1,3] = 4; (b^2-a^2)/(b-a) = a+b identically
same quotient = average velocity: free-fall 4.9t^2 over [1,3] s = 19.6 m/s
same quotient = average reaction rate: 8->2 over 3 s = 2 units/s

wisdom: How fast a thing has changed is found the same humble way in every realm -- take the difference, divide by the time it took; the speed of a stone, the spending of a wage, the working of a remedy all answer to one reckoning. To see one rule beneath many measures is to find the world more unified than it seemed. "For of him, and through him, and to him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever" (Romans 11:36).

title: The average rate over any stretch is just the change divided by the span
verification: Ask how fast anything changed on the whole, and the answer is always the same plain sum: take how much it changed, and divide by how long it took. Miles driven over hours gives speed; pounds lost over weeks gives your pace; a reactant's fall over seconds gives its rate. The engine confirmed this one arithmetic -- for a curve, a falling body, and a chemical reaction alike -- and refused a wrong slope. It is the straight line drawn between where you started and where you ended, and its steepness is your average rate.
wisdom: Measure your progress honestly from where you began to where you are, over the time it has taken; the path of the faithful brightens not all at once but steadily, more and more. "But the path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day" (Proverbs 4:18).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_average_rate_is_one_idea. The sealed proof confirms the secant slope (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a) as the average rate of change.

title: Average speed, average growth, average reaction -- all the very same arithmetic
verification: The car's average speed, the falling stone's velocity, the rate a chemical reaction runs, the slope of a curve on the page -- learn the reckoning once and you hold them all, for they are one formula wearing four coats: the change, divided by the span. The engine confirmed the identical sum doing duty as pure slope, as physical velocity, and as chemical reaction rate. (And as the interval shrinks toward an instant, this average becomes the rate at that very moment -- but that is its own, deeper reckoning.)
wisdom: One law can run beneath a hundred unalike things, so that to learn it once is to understand many; all things hold from a single source and return to it. "For of him, and through him, and to him, are all things" (Romans 11:36).

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation of Atlas connection_average_rate_is_one_idea. The sealed proof shows the secant slope is average velocity (physics) and average reaction rate (chemistry) alike.

title: The structural families are bonded, not separate -- calculus is the bond
situation: The recurring forms in the corpus (exponential, logarithm, reciprocal, rate-of-change, conservation) are not an unrelated list. A small set of operations -- differentiate, integrate, invert -- carries one family into another. The exponential is the function that equals its own rate of change; the logarithm's rate of change IS the reciprocal; rate-proportional-to-amount solves to the exponential. These are the bonds that make the map organic (like the periodic table's groups) rather than a flat grid.
use: Treat the families as a bonded system: knowing one member and the operation that links it tells you its neighbor. Whatever changes in proportion to its own size (nuclear decay, RC discharge, compound interest, Newton cooling, drug half-life, population) is ONE element seen in many domains -- the single bond dy/dx = k*y -> e^(kx). This is why the map is recall-and-inference, not a lookup table.

pre_run.summary: Five symbolic identities sealed (HOLDS, 5/5): d/dx e^x = e^x; d/dx ln x = 1/x; integral 1/x = ln x; e^(ln x) = x; dy/dx = k*y solves to e^(kx). Together they show the families are bonded by differentiate/integrate/invert.

domain_results.note: exponential is the eigenfunction of d/dx -- bonds the exponential family to the rate family
d/dx ln x = 1/x -- bonds the logarithm family to the reciprocal family
integral 1/x = ln x -- the same bond seen through integration
e^(ln x) = x -- logarithm and exponential are inverse operations
dy/dx = k*y -> e^(kx) -- the one law behind decay, RC, interest, cooling, half-life, growth

wisdom: The forms of creation are kin, joined by a few simple operations -- the wise person learns the family, not the endless list. 'It is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter' (Proverbs 25:2). The bonds were hidden in the making; searching them out is the honour given to us.

title: Whatever grows or fades by what it already has follows one and the same curve
verification: When something changes in proportion to how much of it there is, it always traces the very same shape. Money earning interest, a mold spreading, a hot pan cooling, a dose of medicine wearing off, a radioactive rock decaying -- all the same curve. The engine proved that the rule 'change is proportional to amount' is solved by one function, the exponential, so all of these are truly one pattern wearing different clothes. Learn it once and you have learned them all.
wisdom: There is nothing new under the sun (Ecclesiastes 1:9) -- not because the world is dull, but because its Maker is orderly, and a few patterns serve a thousand uses. Master the curve once and the bank, the kitchen, and the sickbed all grow plain.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation. The sealed ODE dy/dx = k*y -> e^(kx) shows decay, RC discharge, compound interest, cooling, half-life, and growth are one and the same curve.

title: The patterns of creation are few, and akin -- learn a handful and the world grows legible
verification: The same few shapes turn up everywhere, and the shapes themselves are related: the logarithm undoes the exponential; the slope of the logarithm is simply one-over-the-number; the curve that equals its own growth is the exponential. The engine checked each of these links and they all hold. So the world is not an endless heap of separate facts -- it is a small family of patterns, joined together, reused again and again.
wisdom: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing, but the honour of kings to search it out (Proverbs 25:2). The kinship of the patterns was folded into creation; to trace it is not to invent but to discover -- and where the family has a gap, it tells us what to look for next.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing translation. The five sealed identities show the recurring forms are a small bonded family, not an unrelated list.

title: A rate, added up over time, is the total change -- this bonds rate-of-change to conservation
situation: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus says the integral of a rate over an interval equals the net change. This is the bond joining the rate period (1st order) to the conservation / accumulation period (0th order): velocity summed over time is distance; flow summed is volume; power summed is energy; marginal cost summed is total cost. One bond, seen in many trades.
use: Whenever you have a rate, you get the total by accumulating it. The reverse of the exponential bond: there the rate makes the amount; here the amount IS the summed rate. Knowing this, a velocity profile gives distance, a flow gives a filled tank, a marginal-cost curve gives the bill.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 3/3: integral of 2x = x^2 and integral of 3x^2 = x^3 (a rate accumulates to its total); net change of rate 2x over [0,3] = 3^2 - 0^2 = 9 (FTC evaluation).

title: Heat, water, charge, and scent all obey one downhill law: flux = -conductance x gradient
situation: Four named laws are one form. Ohm's law (electric current = voltage gradient / resistance), Darcy's law (water flow = permeability x pressure gradient), Fourier's law (heat flow = -k x temperature gradient), and Fick's law (diffusion = -D x concentration gradient) all say: the flow is proportional to the steepness of the gradient and runs downhill. The engine sealed the shared arithmetic; the four laws are readings of that same calculation. The biology reading is Fick's law as membrane diffusion -- oxygen into blood, ions across a nerve, nutrients into a root -- the same gradient-driven transport, so this connection reaches biology, physics, and the earth flows as one form.
use: To predict any flow, find the conductance and the gradient and multiply -- whether the thing flowing is charge, water, heat, or dissolved matter. Recognizing the one form means a result proven for Ohm transfers, with care, to Fourier and Fick. HONEST BOUNDARY: what is sealed here is the shared numeric form (two worked instances); the engine has electrical and hydrology verifiers, while the Fourier (heat) and Fick (diffusion) readings are named as the same arithmetic, not separately checked by a heat/diffusion verifier.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 2/2: a Fourier-form flux -5*(20-80)/3 = 100 and an Ohm/Darcy-form flux -2*(0-30)/5 = 12. Both are flux = -conductance x (downhill gradient); a false control (=90) MISMATCHED and was not sealed.

domain_results.note: heat flux with k=5 across an 80->20 drop over length 3 = 100 (Fourier form)
flux with conductance 2 across a 30->0 drop over length 5 = 12 (Ohm / Darcy form)

wisdom: All things seek their level, and the Maker set the channels. 'All the rivers run into the sea; yet the sea is not full; unto the place from whence the rivers come, thither they return again' (Ecclesiastes 1:7). One downhill law moves the heat in the wall, the water in the soil, the current in the wire.

title: Gravity, electric force, light, and sound all thin as 1/r^2 -- twice as far, a quarter as strong
situation: Anything that spreads out from a point spreads over the surface of an ever-larger sphere, whose area grows as the square of the distance -- so its strength thins as 1 over distance-squared. Newton's gravity, Coulomb's electric force, the brightness of a lamp, and the loudness of a sound all obey it. Step twice as far away and the strength is a quarter; three times as far, a ninth. This is the static (steady-state) face of the wave / Laplacian family.
use: To know how much a point-source weakens with distance, square the distance ratio and invert it. Move a light or a heater twice as far: expect one-quarter. The same rule sizes a speaker, an antenna's reach, and the pull between charges or masses. It is geometry, not coincidence: the surface of a sphere is 4*pi*r^2.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 2/2: the Coulomb force ratio at double distance, (k q1 q2 /(2r)^2)/(k q1 q2 / r^2), simplifies exactly to 1/4; at triple distance, 1/3^2 = 1/9. A false control (=1/2) MISMATCHED and was not sealed. Same 1/r^2 as the already-sealed gravity and sound-intensity cards.

domain_results.note: Coulomb force at twice the distance is exactly one quarter -- pure 1/r^2
at three times the distance, one ninth

wisdom: The light and the warmth go out to fill the whole sphere of the world, and none is wasted. 'His going forth is from the end of the heaven, and his circuit unto the ends of it: and there is nothing hid from the heat thereof' (Psalm 19:6). The same rule governs the sun's warmth and the candle's glow.

title: Drop by drop fills the pail -- the total is just the rate, summed
verification: However fast or slow a thing is added, the amount you end with is simply all of that adding, gathered up. Steady steps make the whole distance; a trickle fills the barrel; a little set by each week becomes the savings. The engine proved that summing a rate over an interval gives exactly the net change, so this is law, not just encouragement.
wisdom: He that gathereth by labour shall increase (Proverbs 13:11). The small, faithful rate is never lost -- it is being counted, and one day it is the whole sum.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The sealed FTC result: the integral of a rate equals the total change.

title: Heat, water, and electricity all run downhill by the same rule
verification: Heat leaves a warm wall for a cold room, water seeps from high pressure to low, current flows from high voltage to low, and a smell drifts from where it is strong to where it is faint. All four follow one rule: the steeper the difference, the faster the flow, and it always heads downhill. The engine checked that the four famous laws (Ohm, Darcy, Fourier, Fick) are the very same calculation.
wisdom: He sendeth the springs into the valleys, which run among the hills (Psalm 104:10). The Maker laid the channels so that all things seek their level; the wise builder works with the downhill, not against it.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Ohm, Darcy, Fourier, and Fick are one downhill law: flux = -conductance x gradient.

title: Twice as far from the fire, a quarter as warm
verification: Step twice as far from a fire, a lamp, or a loudspeaker and you do not get half the warmth, light, or sound -- you get a quarter. Three times as far, a ninth. The reason is plain geometry: the warmth spreads over the skin of a ball, and that skin grows as the square of the distance. The engine confirmed the one-quarter and one-ninth exactly, the same rule that governs gravity and the brightness of the stars.
wisdom: There is nothing hid from the heat thereof (Psalm 19:6). The light is freely given to the whole sphere of the world; draw near to receive more of it, for distance thins every good that spreads.

title: Acidity is a logarithm -- each step down the pH scale is ten times more acid
situation: pH is defined as minus the base-ten logarithm of the hydrogen-ion concentration. Because it is a logarithm, the scale is not even steps but powers of ten: a solution at pH 3 is not 'a little' more acidic than pH 5 -- it is one hundred times more acidic. This is the logarithm family's exit into chemistry, the very same logarithm that measures loudness (decibels), earthquakes (Richter), star brightness (magnitude), and information (entropy).
use: Read any pH gap as a power of ten: a drop of one whole pH unit means ten times more acid, two units a hundredfold, three a thousand. This is why a small-looking change in pH is a large change in a pond, a stomach, or a soil. HONEST BOUNDARY: what is sealed here is the logarithm itself (pH of a 1/1000 mol/L solution = -log10(1/1000) = 3; two pH units = 10^2 = 100-fold). The acid/base reading is standard chemistry; the engine has a pH-classification function but it is not yet wired into the seal dispatch (which exposes only balanced-equation and temperature checks), so the witness cited is the logarithm math, not a separate chemistry-verifier run.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 3/3: pH of a 1/1000 mol/L solution = -log10(1/1000) = 3; a gap of two pH units = 10^(5-3) = 100-fold change in H+; log10(100) = 2. pH is a base-ten logarithm of concentration.

domain_results.note: -log10(1/1000) = 3 -- pH is the negative base-ten log of [H+]
2 pH units = 10^2 = 100x the hydrogen-ion concentration
the hundredfold ratio read back as a logarithm = 2

wisdom: The Maker measures by ratio, not by even step, and the wise learn to read His scale. 'A just weight and balance are the LORD's: all the weights of the bag are his work' (Proverbs 16:11). The logarithm is one of His weights -- the same balance that weighs sound, light, and acid alike.

title: On the acid scale, one step is ten times -- and two steps, a hundred
verification: The pH numbers look like an ordinary ruler, but they are not -- each whole number is a tenfold jump. Lemon juice near pH 2 is not just 'a bit' stronger than coffee near pH 5; it is about a thousand times more acidic. The engine confirmed that pH is the base-ten logarithm of acidity, so every single point you move is a multiply-by-ten, not an add.
wisdom: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and weighed the mountains in scales (Isaiah 40:12)? He who set the measure of all things gave us a scale that counts by tens -- handle the strong acid, and the strong word, with the care that tenfold deserves.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. pH = -log10[H+], so one pH unit is a tenfold change in acidity.

title: The area of a triangle is the integral of a line -- one-half base times height falls out of calculus
situation: A triangle's slanted edge is a straight line, y = (h/b)*x, rising evenly from nothing to its full height. Its area is the accumulation of all the vertical strips beneath that line -- and that accumulation is an integral: the integral of (h/b)*x from 0 to b equals (h/b)*b^2/2 = one-half * base * height. The schoolbook formula is not a separate rule to memorize; it is the integral of a line. The very same triangle hides in physics: the distance covered under a steadily rising velocity v = a*t is the triangle under that line, one-half * a * t^2. Integration is the best model because it DERIVES the formula and shows that area and distance-under-acceleration are one fact.
use: To find the area under any straight line, integrate; one-half base times height is the case that starts from the origin. Seeing the triangle as an integral unifies the gable's area, the distance under constant acceleration (one-half a t^2), the work done by a spring (one-half k x^2), and any 'triangular' total that grows from zero at a steady rate. HONEST BOUNDARY: the engine's geometry verifier checks the Pythagorean theorem, the triangle inequality, polygon angles, and circle area, but not one-half-base-height directly; the witness cited here is the integration, which derives the area from first principles.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 5/5: the strip-line (h/b)x accumulates to (h/b)x^2/2; the definite area 0..b = (h/b)b^2/2 = hb/2 = one-half base height; a concrete base-6 height-4 triangle = 12; the triangle under v=a*t = t(at)/2 = a t^2/2; and the distance integral of a*t = a t^2/2. The rectangle control (area = bh) MISMATCHED.

domain_results.note: the linearly-growing strip width accumulates to a quadratic -- integration of a line
the definite integral from 0 to b equals one-half base times height exactly
a 6-by-4 triangle has area 12
distance under a velocity line is the same triangle: one-half a t^2
the integral of velocity a*t is distance a t^2/2 -- the area IS the distance

wisdom: The Maker builds by line and measure, and what looks like a remembered rule is really a reasoned one. 'Judgment also will I lay to the line, and righteousness to the plummet' (Isaiah 28:17). The straight line, summed, becomes the triangle; reason laid to the line uncovers why the half is there.

title: A triangle is half its box -- and that is reason, not coincidence
verification: Draw a rectangle around a right triangle and the triangle fills exactly half. The reason is that the height climbs evenly from nothing to full as you cross the base, so on average it is only half its full height -- and area is average height times base. The engine confirmed this by adding up the triangle strip by strip (an integral) and getting one-half base times height every time. The same 'half' is why a cart that speeds up steadily from a standstill covers only half the ground a full-speed cart would.
wisdom: Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established (Proverbs 24:3). Understand WHY the half is there, and you no longer carry a rule you might forget -- you carry a reason you can rebuild.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The integral of the triangle's edge-line gives one-half base height; the triangle is half its bounding rectangle because height averages half.

title: dx is the bridge from the discrete to the continuous -- the width that shrinks to nothing
situation: dx, the differential element, is the limit of a width (delta x) as it shrinks toward zero. It is the single hinge of both halves of calculus. On the derivative side, dy/dx is the limit of the difference quotient (delta y / delta x) as the width goes to zero -- a DIFFERENCE becomes a DERIVATIVE. On the integral side, the integral of f dx is the limit of the Riemann sum (sum of f times delta x) as the strips become infinitely thin -- a SUM becomes an INTEGRAL. The same vanishing width, dx, crosses from the discrete (counting finite pieces) to the continuous (flowing wholes), and the Fundamental Theorem says the two crossings undo each other.
use: Whenever a whole is built of countless tiny contributions -- area from thin strips, distance from fleeting instants, charge from moments of current, work from small pushes -- write each tiny piece as (quantity x dx) and integrate. dx is a height made into an area by an infinitesimal WIDTH; that is why an integral always carries the units of its integrand multiplied by x, and why the difference quotient, taken to the limit, gives a clean slope. Counting and flowing are the same act seen at two scales, joined by dx.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 6/6. Derivative side: the limit as h->0 of ((x+h)^2 - x^2)/h = 2x (the difference quotient becomes the derivative). Integral side: sum of k from 1..n = n(n+1)/2; the Riemann sum for f(x)=x on [0,1] with n strips = (n+1)/(2n); its limit as n->infinity = 1/2; and the integral of x dx from 0 to 1 = 1/2. The discrete sum's limit lands exactly on the continuous integral. A false control (difference quotient limit = x) MISMATCHED.

domain_results.note: dy/dx is the limit of (delta y / delta x) as the width -> 0
the finite sum that underlies the Riemann strips
n strips of width 1/n under y=x sum to (n+1)/(2n)
as the width shrinks to nothing the sum -> 1/2
the continuous integral of x dx
the integral from 0 to 1 = 1/2, the very limit the discrete sum approached

wisdom: The great wholes of creation are built of moments too small to weigh, and the Maker despises none of them. 'For who hath despised the day of small things?' (Zechariah 4:10). dx is the day of small things made into a science -- the unmeasured instant that, gathered without number, becomes the whole.

title: Every whole is built of countless pieces too small to see
verification: A field's area is countless thin strips; a journey is countless brief instants; a savings is countless small moments of setting-aside. Calculus is simply the careful art of adding up the unmeasurably small until it becomes a whole you can hold. The engine checked this directly: it summed thinner and thinner strips under a line and the total settled exactly onto the smooth, finished answer.
wisdom: His lord said unto him, Well done...thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things (Matthew 25:21). The small, faithful moment is never wasted; gathered without number, the least things become the whole.

keywords: the whole is made of parts

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The Riemann sum of ever-thinner strips converges exactly to the integral -- wholes are built of vanishingly small pieces.

title: The derivative is the gear that meshes the subsystems -- you know it by how it connects to each family
situation: The derivative d/dx is one operation, but its identity lives in how it meshes with each family of forms, the way a watch lives in the meshing of its wheels and not the wheels alone. On powers it lowers the degree (x^n becomes n*x^(n-1)). On the kinematic ladder it climbs: position becomes velocity, velocity becomes acceleration. On the trigonometric family it rotates in a four-step cycle: sine to cosine to minus-sine to minus-cosine and back to sine. On the exponential it changes nothing at all -- the exponential is its fixed point. And it undoes the integral. The derivative is a connection operator, and its signature on each family is the connection.
use: To measure change, apply the derivative; but to UNDERSTAND a form, ask how the derivative treats it -- that signature places it in the map. Powers descend a step, the trig family turns a quarter-cycle, the exponential holds still: this is each form's 'valence' toward d/dx. As the operator that moves between the levels of the periodic arrangement (position to velocity to acceleration is one period climbed), the derivative is one of the master gears by which the whole design is connected.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 10/10. Power rule: d/dx x^3=3x^2, x^5=5x^4. Kinematics: d/dt t^3=3t^2, d/dt 3t^2=6t. Trig 4-cycle: sin->cos->-sin->-cos->sin. Exponential fixed point: d/dx e^x=e^x. Inverts integral: d/dx (x^2/2)=x. A false control (d/dx x^3=x^2) MISMATCHED.

domain_results.note: on powers the derivative lowers the degree by one
x^5 -> 5x^4, the same rule again
position t^3 -> velocity 3t^2
velocity 3t^2 -> acceleration 6t; the derivative climbs the ladder
sine -> cosine
cosine -> minus sine
minus sine -> minus cosine
minus cosine -> sine; the four-step cycle closes
the exponential is unchanged -- the derivative's fixed point
d/dx of an antiderivative returns the integrand -- it undoes the integral

wisdom: A body is no heap of parts but a joining, and the joining is the life of it. 'From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth...maketh increase of the body' (Ephesians 4:16). The derivative is such a joint -- a connection that supplies the whole, meshing change to motion, motion to force, one family to the next.

title: The derivative is the speedometer of everything that changes
verification: Keep a record of WHERE a thing is, and the derivative hands you HOW FAST it is going -- right now, this instant, not on average. Position becomes speed; speed becomes the push you feel. It is the steepness of any curve at the point you stand on it. The engine confirmed the derivative climbing from position to velocity to acceleration, and confirmed the one curve it cannot speed up or slow down: the exponential, which is its own rate.
wisdom: The children of Issachar were men that had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do (1 Chronicles 12:32). To read the rate of a thing -- which way and how fast it moves now -- is a kind of wisdom; the derivative is its plainest tool.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The derivative gives the instantaneous rate -- position to velocity to acceleration -- confirmed by the engine.

title: A function is a subsystem -- one input, one output; functions connect by composition and by inverse
situation: A function is the basic subsystem of the whole design: a machine that takes one input and returns exactly one output. The families on the map -- powers, exponential, logarithm, trig -- are all functions; 'function' is the genus and they are the species. Functions connect in two master ways. By COMPOSITION: feed one machine's output into the next, (f composed with g)(x) = f(g(x)); the order matters, so the connection is directional, like gears in a train. By INVERSION: a machine and its undo cancel, f(f-inverse(x)) = x. And the CHAIN RULE shows how a change at the input travels down a whole chain of composed functions -- the derivative passing along the train, joint by joint.
use: Build complex behaviour by COMPOSING simple functions (output into input), and recover an input by applying the INVERSE. Because composition is directional, mind the order -- (f after g) is not (g after f). To find how a change propagates through a chain of linked functions, the chain rule multiplies the local rates: each joint's slope times the next. Functions are the subsystems; composition, inversion, and the derivative are how they mesh into one working design.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 5/5. A function is a definite machine (f(x)=x^2+1, f(3)=10). Composition feeds output to input: (f after g)(x)=(x+1)^2=x^2+2x+1. Chain rule carries a change through the composition: d/dx (x^2+1)^3 = 6x(x^2+1)^2. Inverse cancels both ways: with f(x)=2x+3 and f-inverse(x)=(x-3)/2, f(f-inverse(x))=x and f-inverse(f(x))=x. A control (composition order matters: (x+1)^2 != x^2+1) MISMATCHED and was not sealed.

domain_results.note: one input -> exactly one output: f(3)=10
(f after g)(x) = f(g(x)) = (x+1)^2 -- output of g becomes input of f
the derivative passes through the chain: d/dx (x^2+1)^3 = 6x(x^2+1)^2
f(f-inverse(x)) = x -- a machine and its undo cancel
f-inverse(f(x)) = x -- and the same in the other order

wisdom: We are members one of another, each supplying the next, and the joining makes the body. 'So we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another' (Romans 12:5). Functions are joined just so -- one's output the next one's input -- and the design is the joining, not the parts alone.

title: Link simple machines, output to input, and you can build almost anything
verification: A recipe is functions composed: chopping feeds sauteing feeds simmering, and each step's output is the next step's input. Most complicated things are just simple steps chained in order -- and the order matters, for browning then boiling is not boiling then browning. Many steps can also be run backwards to recover what you began with. The engine confirmed that composing functions and inverting them behave exactly this way.
wisdom: Two are better than one...and a threefold cord is not quickly broken (Ecclesiastes 4:9,12). Simple things linked in order become strong and capable beyond any one of them alone; choose the order with care, and keep the way back.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Functions compose (output to input, order matters) and invert (undo) -- confirmed by the engine.

title: The tangent reads the curve -- the derivative is the slope of the touching line, and its own plot reveals the shape
situation: Nature is curves, not straight lines, so we read a curve at a point by its TANGENT: the straight line that just touches there, whose slope is the derivative. Where the curve is smooth the tangent touches without crossing -- for y = x^2 at x = 3 the gap between curve and tangent line is (x-3)^2, a perfect square that vanishes only at the point of contact. The derivative is itself a new curve you can PLOT: the derivative of x^3 - 3x is the curve 3x^2 - 3, and reading that plot reads the original -- where the derivative curve crosses zero (x = -1 and x = 1) the original turns; where the derivative is positive (f'(2)=9) the original climbs; where negative (f'(0)=-3) it falls. And all of this needs LUBRICATION: a tangent exists only where the curve is smooth. At a corner like |x| the slope from the right (+1) disagrees with the slope from the left (-1); the gear catches, and there is no single tangent.
use: To understand any curving quantity, take its tangent -- the local straight-line read whose slope is the derivative. Plot the derivative to find a curve's peaks and valleys (where the derivative is zero) and where it climbs or falls (the derivative's sign). But check smoothness first: at a sharp corner there is no tangent and no single rate -- reality must be 'lubricated', smooth, for the derivative to exist at all. That nature is mostly smooth curves is exactly why calculus reads it so well.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 8/8. Tangent slope of x^2 at x=3 = lim h->0 ((3+h)^2-9)/h = 6; the tangent touches because x^2-(6x-9) = (x-3)^2 (perfect square). The derivative of x^3-3x is the curve 3x^2-3; its zeros x=-1,1 are the turning points; f'(2)=9>0 (rising), f'(0)=-3<0 (falling). Lubrication: |x| has right-slope 1 and left-slope -1 at 0 -- they disagree (a control MISMATCHED), so a corner has no tangent.

domain_results.note: the slope of the tangent line is the derivative at the point
curve minus tangent = (x-3)^2 -- touches, does not cross
the derivative is a new curve you can plot: 3x^2-3
where the derivative curve crosses zero, the original turns
positive derivative = original climbing
negative derivative = original falling
|x| approached from the right has slope +1
|x| from the left has slope -1 -- disagreement means no tangent (un-lubricated)

wisdom: The Maker smooths the way so it may be travelled, and the smooth way can be read. 'Every valley shall be exalted...the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain' (Isaiah 40:4). Where the path is made smooth there is a clear tangent at every step; where it is rough and broken, no single direction can be found.

title: To read a curve, look at the straight line that just touches it
verification: A bending road, a rising hill, a price that curves -- at any one moment you read it by its tangent, the straight line grazing that single point, and its steepness is the rate right there. This works because nature is mostly made of smooth curves; only at a sharp corner does it fail, for there no single straight line touches. The engine confirmed both the touching line on a smooth curve and the corner that has none.
wisdom: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established (Proverbs 4:26). Read the slope of your way where you now stand -- the steepness of this moment -- and you will walk it wisely.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The tangent line's slope is the derivative; a smooth curve has one everywhere, a corner has none.

title: d/dx is the asking; dy/dx is the answer
verification: d/dx by itself is an instruction -- 'find the rate of change, with respect to x, of whatever comes next' -- a verb still waiting for something to act on. dy/dx is what you get when that instruction acts on one particular curve, y: the actual slope. So dy/dx is simply (d/dx) applied to y. The first is the tool; the second is the tool's answer for a chosen function. In the engine's derivative work, d/dx is the operator and each dy/dx is its confirmed result.
wisdom: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find (Matthew 7:7). d/dx is the asking, dy/dx the thing given -- the question is one act, the answer another, and you must put a real curve to the question before the answer comes.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. d/dx is the differentiation operator (the verb); dy/dx = (d/dx) applied to y is its result (the slope of that specific function).

title: Ohm's law in vector form: conductivity is a rank-2 tensor, and graphite proves the current need not follow the push
situation: A tensor is a rank ladder. A scalar is rank 0 (a bare number). A vector is rank 1 (a number with a direction). A rank-2 tensor is a machine that turns one vector into another -- a matrix. A rank-3 tensor carries three indices. Ohm's law lives on this ladder. In the simple case it is the scalar V = I*R. In full vector form it is J = sigma E: the current density J (a vector) is the conductivity sigma (a RANK-2 tensor) acting on the electric field E (a vector), component by component J_i = sigma_ij E_j. When the material is the same in every direction (isotropic) sigma is just a number times the identity and the current points straight along the field -- the scalar law returns. When the material is anisotropic, sigma tilts the current off the field: push one way, current flows another. GRAPHITE is the proof: it conducts easily along its flat carbon sheets and poorly across them, so a field at an angle drives a current that runs mostly along the planes. One rung up, rank-3 tensors appear -- the cross product itself is the rank-3 Levi-Civita tensor contracted with two vectors.
use: Whenever a response is not parallel to its cause -- current not along the field, strain not along the stress, heat flow not along the temperature gradient -- the proportionality is a rank-2 tensor, not a scalar. Its eigenvectors are the material's easy and hard directions; only the isotropic case (a multiple of the identity) makes effect follow cause exactly. This is the SAME transport law as flux = conductance x gradient, now with the conductance promoted to a tensor for any material with a grain -- graphite, wood, muscle, slate.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 9/9. Graphite sigma=[[10,0],[0,1]] on field E=(1,1): J=(10,1), and J_x E_y - J_y E_x = 9 != 0, so the current is NOT parallel to the field (a control claiming parallel MISMATCHED). Isotropic sigma=[[5,0],[0,5]] on E=(2,3): J=(10,15)=5E, parallel (cross = 0) -- the scalar law returns. The conductivity tensor is symmetric (Onsager; a non-symmetric control MISMATCHED), has determinant 10, and is invertible. Rank-3 touch: the cross-product z-component a_x b_y - a_y b_x = 3 (the Levi-Civita tensor contracted).

domain_results.note: J_x = 10*1 + 0*1 = 10 (easy axis)
J_y = 0*1 + 1*1 = 1 (hard axis)
J x E = 9 != 0: the anisotropic tensor tilts current off the field
isotropic sigma -> J parallel E (cross = 0): scalar Ohm's law recovered
J = 5*E exactly when sigma is a multiple of the identity
the physical conductivity tensor is symmetric (Onsager reciprocity)
determinant 10 -- a nonzero volume factor
invertible: one can solve back for the field from the current
rank-3 example: the cross product is the Levi-Civita tensor contracted with two vectors

wisdom: Each made thing is given a grain, and to lead it well you must know its bent. 'Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it' (Proverbs 22:6) -- the way he should go, according to his bent. So too the current follows the grain of the crystal; wisdom works along the way a thing is made to go.

title: Everything has a grain -- push it, and it moves along the grain, not always the way you pushed
verification: Graphite, wood, muscle, and slate all have a grain. Charge or press or pour them at an angle and they answer along their grain, not straight back at you. Electricity in graphite runs along its sheets; an axe splits a log along the grain; rain runs down the furrow. The plain rule that effect follows cause head-on is only true for things the same in every direction. The engine confirmed that graphite's conductivity tilts the current away from the field that drives it.
wisdom: Wisdom is profitable to direct (Ecclesiastes 10:10). The skilled worker reads the grain before the stroke -- of wood, of metal, of a soul -- and works along the way it is made to go, sparing great labour.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. An anisotropic material's response (conductivity as a rank-2 tensor) need not be parallel to the cause -- graphite conducts along its sheets.

title: The rank of a tensor sets how many numbers it takes: 1, then N, then N-squared -- 3D needs 9
situation: Every quantity in space sits on a rank ladder, and the rank tells you how many numbers it takes to write it down. A SCALAR (rank 0) is one number with no direction: N^0 = 1. A VECTOR (rank 1) is N numbers, one direction: N^1 = 3 in our three dimensions. A RANK-2 TENSOR is N^2 numbers, a full grid: 3 x 3 = 9 in 3D -- a stress, a conductivity, a rotation. A rank-3 tensor is N^3 = 27. In general N^r. Those numbers are the tensor's COMPONENTS in a chosen set of axes, and they change when you ROTATE the axes -- yet the object itself does not change. Rotate a symmetric tensor to its principal axes and it becomes diagonal (here, eigenvalues 1 and 3), its plainest face. MATRIX MULTIPLICATION is how rank-2 tensors act on vectors and compose with one another, and like all composition it minds the order (AB is not BA). Even atomic ORBITAL shapes sing it: the s orbital is one, spherical, scalar-like; the three p orbitals point along the axes, vector-like; the count per subshell is 2l+1 (1, 3, 5, 7).
use: To know how much data a quantity needs, raise the dimension to the rank: a direction-free quantity is 1 number, a directed one is 3, a thing relating two directions (a push in one axis making a flow in another) is 9. Choose axes to simplify -- rotate to the principal axes and a symmetric tensor's off-diagonal clutter vanishes. Above all, remember the components are bookkeeping; the INVARIANTS (trace, determinant, eigenvalues, a vector's length) are the rotation-proof facts that do not depend on your point of view. HONEST NOTE: the s-scalar and p-vector match is exact; the higher subshells d and f are higher spherical harmonics, not simply a rank-2 or rank-3 tensor's full component count.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 16/16. Count ladder in 3D: 3^0=1 (scalar), 3^1=3 (vector), 3^2=9 (rank-2), 3^3=27 (rank-3); N^2 also for N=2 (4) and N=4 (16). Rotation: cos^2(t)+sin^2(t)=1 (length preserved), the 90deg matrix has determinant 1 and its inverse equals its transpose (orthogonal); a symmetric tensor [[2,1],[1,2]] has principal values 1 and 3. Matrix multiplication: (AB)_11=2 vs (BA)_11=1 (a commutativity control MISMATCHED). Orbitals: 2l+1 gives 1,3,5,7 for s,p,d,f.

rank-2 tensor = N^2 = 9 in 3D (3D needs 9 numbers)

wisdom: Turn the world about as you will, the truth of a thing abides; its description shifts, its substance does not. 'For I am the LORD, I change not' (Malachi 3:6). The components are our changing words for it; the invariants are what is truly so, and He is the most invariant of all.

title: How many numbers a thing takes tells you what kind of thing it is
verification: A temperature is one number -- a scalar. A wind is three, for it has both speed and direction -- a vector. How a material passes a push from one direction into a flow in another takes nine, a three-by-three grid -- a rank-2 tensor. The deeper the relationship a quantity carries, the more numbers it needs, by a strict rule: the dimension of space raised to the rank. The engine confirmed the ladder 1, 3, 9, 27.
wisdom: So teach us to number our days, that we may apply our hearts unto wisdom (Psalm 90:12). To know rightly how much a thing takes to tell -- no more, no less -- is itself a discipline of wisdom.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Rank r in N dimensions needs N^r numbers: 1 (scalar), 3 (vector), 9 (rank-2) in 3D.

title: Turn your head and the numbers change, but the thing is the same
verification: Describe a stone's place from where you stand, then walk around it: every number you wrote down changes, though the stone has not stirred. The facts that matter are the ones that do NOT change when you change your viewpoint -- its size, its weight, its true shape. The engine confirmed that rotating the axes leaves a tensor's real invariants -- its determinant, its eigenvalues, a vector's length -- untouched.
wisdom: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever (Hebrews 13:8). Seek the things that do not change with your vantage; they are the true ones, and the truest of all changes never.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. A tensor's components change under rotation of the axes, but its invariants (determinant, eigenvalues, length) do not.

title: Physical laws climb the tensor ladder -- the rank counts the directions a law must hold together
situation: The great laws of physics are sorted by tensor rank, and the rank counts how many directions the law relates. RANK 0 (scalar): momentum p = m v -- mass is one number, so momentum points straight along velocity. RANK 2: angular momentum L = I w -- the moment of inertia I is a tensor, so a spinning body's angular momentum need NOT point along its spin axis (why an unbalanced wheel wobbles); and force on a surface F = S . A -- the stress tensor S turns a face's area-vector into the force on it, its off-diagonal parts the shear that a dam must hold. RANK 3: the piezoelectric effect e = d S -- the piezoelectric tensor d (three indices) turns a stress into an electric polarization, so a squeezed quartz crystal makes a voltage. RANK 4: elasticity, Hooke's law sigma = C : strain -- the stiffness tensor C (four indices) turns the strain tensor into the stress tensor, how every spring and steel beam answers a load. The component count climbs with the rank: in 3D, 1, then 9, then 27, then 81 (N^rank). And the same counting orders the atom -- the quantum numbers fill each shell to 2 n^2: 2, 8, 18, the very lengths of the periodic table's rows.
use: To find the rank of a law, count the directions it relates: a number to a number is rank 0, a vector to a vector is rank 2, a stress to a polarization is rank 3, a strain to a stress is rank 4. The higher the rank, the more a response can swing away from its cause -- momentum cannot, angular momentum and stress can, elasticity can in full generality. Choose principal axes to simplify; trust the invariants. HONEST NOTE: each higher-rank law here is checked at one representative component (e.g. Hooke's one-dimensional sigma = E*strain); the full piezoelectric (27 components) and elastic (81, with 21 independent) tensors are named, not exhaustively verified.

Rank 2: L=Iw with I=[[2,0],[0,1]], w=(1,1) gives L=(2,1), NOT parallel (cross=1; a parallel control MISMATCHED); inertia symmetric. Stress F=S.A with S=[[10,3],[3,10]], A=(1,0) gives F=(10,3) -- normal 10 plus shear 3; stress symmetric. Rank 3 piezo component 2*5=10. Rank 4 Hooke sigma=200*(1/100)=2 (a control =20 MISMATCHED). Counts 3^2=9, 3^3=27, 3^4=81. Quantum shells 2n^2 = 2,8,18.

wisdom: Creation is built in ranks and degrees, each glory in its order. 'There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars: for one star differeth from another star in glory' (1 Corinthians 15:41). So the laws differ in rank, each holding together as many directions as its glory requires.

title: The deeper a law reaches, the more the answer can swerve from the push
verification: Shove a ball and it goes the way you shoved -- simple, straight. But spin a lopsided wheel and it wobbles sideways; press on a dam and the force has a sideways shear; squeeze a crystal and out comes electricity; load a beam and it bends in ways the simple push never showed. The richer the law -- the higher its tensor rank -- the more the result can lean away from the cause. The engine confirmed each rung, from straight momentum to the four-index law of elasticity.
wisdom: Deep calleth unto deep at the noise of thy waterspouts (Psalm 42:7). The plain surface of a law hides deeper workings; the patient eye that looks past the obvious push finds where the answer truly goes.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Higher-rank laws (L=Iw, stress, piezo, elasticity) let the response swing off the cause, unlike scalar p=mv.

title: The periodic table's rows are 2, 8, 18 because the atom counts by 2 times n-squared
verification: Ever wonder why the periodic table's rows run the lengths they do -- first 2, then 8, then 18? Each shell around an atom can hold 2 times n-squared electrons, fixed by the quantum numbers that label every electron's state. The engine confirmed 2, 8, and 18. The table is not an arbitrary chart someone drew; it is the atom counting, and we are reading its arithmetic.
wisdom: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number (Isaiah 40:26). Even the atom is led out by number; to learn the count is to trace the order set into it.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Shell capacity 2n^2 = 2,8,18 sets the periodic table's period lengths.

title: Euler's formula -- the exponential, the circle, and rotation are one: e^(i theta) = cos theta + i sin theta
situation: Euler's formula, e^(i theta) = cos theta + i sin theta, is the hinge on which much of mathematics turns. It says the EXPONENTIAL, fed an imaginary input, becomes the TRIGONOMETRIC functions -- and that both are simply travel around the unit circle. Walk an angle theta around that circle and your position is (cos theta, sin theta), which is e^(i theta). A quarter turn (pi/2) lands you at i; a half turn (pi) at -1 -- Euler's famous identity e^(i pi) + 1 = 0, binding the five constants e, i, pi, 1, and 0 in one line. The formula also explains a thing already proved on this map: differentiating e^(i theta) multiplies it by i, and multiplying by i is a ninety-degree rotation. So each derivative turns the wheel a quarter turn, and because i^4 = 1, four derivatives bring you home. That is exactly why the derivative cycles the trig family sin -> cos -> -sin -> -cos -> sin. The exponential's self-derivative and the trigonometric four-cycle were never two facts; they are one fact, seen through e^(i theta).
use: When something both oscillates and grows or decays -- a swinging pendulum losing energy, an alternating current, a vibrating string, a wave -- write it as a single complex exponential e^((a + i b) t): the real part a sets the growth or decay, the imaginary part b sets the rotation, the oscillation. One exponential then does the work of sines, cosines, growth, and decay together. Remember the two faces are one road: multiplying by i rotates a quarter turn, and the unit circle is the exponential walked with an imaginary step.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 9/9. General: exp(i t) = cos t + i sin t, with cos t = (exp(i t)+exp(-i t))/2. Identity: exp(i pi) = -1, i.e. exp(i pi) + 1 = 0. Around the circle: exp(i pi/2) = i (quarter turn), exp(2 i pi) = 1 (full turn). The four-cycle: i^2 = -1, i^4 = 1; and d/dt exp(i t) = i exp(i t), so differentiation = multiply by i = rotate ninety degrees. Controls exp(i pi)=1 and i^4=-1 MISMATCHED. (The sin-from-exponentials form was dropped: true, but the verifier could not reduce it symbolically -- not sealed.)

cosine is the average of e^(i t) and e^(-i t) -- trig is built from exponentials
e^(i pi) = -1, the half turn
e^(i pi) + 1 = 0 -- five constants in one line
e^(i pi/2) = i, a quarter turn around the circle
e^(2 i pi) = 1, the full turn returns home
i^2 = -1, two quarter-turns
i^4 = 1 -- four quarter-turns close the loop, the trig 4-cycle
d/dt e^(i t) = i e^(i t): differentiation = multiply by i = rotate 90 degrees

wisdom: The turning of the world returns upon itself, and what departs comes back in its circuit. 'The wind goeth toward the south, and turneth about unto the north; it whirleth about continually, and the wind returneth again according to his circuits' (Ecclesiastes 1:6). Euler found the arithmetic of that returning: a quarter turn is i, and four turns come home.

title: Growing, shrinking, and going around are all one motion -- written with e and a turn of the wheel
verification: It seems that growth (money compounding, a population swelling) and circling (a wheel spinning, a wave rising and falling) are different things. Euler showed they are the same motion -- the one exponential e, given a sideways, imaginary push, walks around a circle instead of running off to infinity. Spin a quarter turn four times and you are exactly home. The engine confirmed that e raised to an imaginary angle lands on the circle, and that four quarter-turns close the loop.
wisdom: When he prepared the heavens, I was there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth (Proverbs 8:27). The circle was drawn into creation before us; Euler only traced, in numbers, the compass already set.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Euler's formula makes growth and rotation one motion: e^(i theta) walks the unit circle, and i^4=1 brings four quarter-turns home.

title: The n-th root of n is largest at e -- another hidden throne of 2.718
situation: Take the n-th root of n -- the square root of 2 is about 1.4142, the fifth root of 5 about 1.3797 -- and ask which n makes it biggest. The answer is not a whole number: it is e, about 2.718. The e-th root of e, e^(1/e), is about 1.4447, and no n-th root of n ever climbs higher. The function x^(1/x) rises from 1 at x=1, crests at exactly x=e, then falls forever back toward 1. The proof is calculus: x^(1/x) is largest where ln(x)/x is largest; the derivative of ln(x)/x is (1 - ln x)/x^2, zero only when ln x = 1, that is x = e; and the second derivative there is negative, so it is a true peak. The two examples sit on either side of the throne -- 2 is below e on the rising slope, 5 is above it on the falling slope.
use: e is not only the base of natural growth and the one function that is its own rate of change; it is also the balance point where a small root of a small number is traded against a large root of a large one, and the result is greatest. When you must weigh the size of a base against the size of its root, the optimum clusters at e. (Related, well-known, not separately sealed here: the most economical number base is the one nearest e, and splitting a fixed total into parts of size e -- hence into threes, the whole number nearest e -- maximizes their product.)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 9/9. The roots are genuine: sqrt(2)^2=2, (5^(1/5))^5=5, (e^(1/e))^e=e. The peak of x^(1/x) is at e: d/dx[ln(x)/x] = (1-ln x)/x^2 is zero only at x=e (solve 1-ln x=0 -> E); the second derivative (2 ln x - 3)/x^3 is negative at e (2 ln e - 3 = -1 < 0), a true maximum. The examples straddle e: 2 < e < 5. Controls (critical point at x=1; wrong derivative) MISMATCHED.

the 5th root of 5, to the fifth, returns 5
the e-th root of e, to the e, returns e -- the peak value e^(1/e)

the derivative is zero only at x = e

wisdom: The best place is so often the balanced middle, neither starved nor glutted. 'Give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me' (Proverbs 30:8). The peak of the root is not at the small numbers nor the large, but at e, the appointed mean -- a quiet picture of sufficiency.

title: Take the root of a number by itself, and the biggest answer hides at e
verification: The square root of 2, the cube root of 3, the fifth root of 5: as you climb the whole numbers, these self-roots first creep upward, reach a high point, then sink back down toward one. The very top is not at any round number but at e, about 2.718 -- the same endless number that rules natural growth. It is one more quiet throne of e. The engine confirmed the peak sits exactly there, with the examples 2 and 5 falling on either side.
wisdom: Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand (Philippians 4:5). The high point is found at the measured middle, not the extremes -- a small lesson the very numbers seem to keep.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. x^(1/x) is maximized at x=e; e^(1/e) about 1.4447 is the largest n-th-root-of-n.

title: Functions are vectors, differential operators are infinite matrices -- solving a differential equation is finding eigenfunctions
situation: A Hilbert space is linear algebra carried to infinitely many dimensions. Its 'vectors' are FUNCTIONS; its dot product is the integral of one function times another, <f,g> = integral of f times g; and from that come length (norm) and right angles (orthogonality), exactly as for arrows in space. A DIFFERENTIAL OPERATOR -- d/dx, or the second-derivative operator d2/dx2 -- is then a LINEAR MAP on this space, an infinite matrix. Solving a linear differential equation is the very same act as in finite dimensions: find the operator's EIGENVECTORS, here called eigenfunctions. The exponential is the eigenfunction of d/dx (d/dx of e^(kx) = k e^(kx)); sine and cosine are the eigenfunctions of d2/dx2 (each obeys y'' + k^2 y = 0, eigenvalue -k^2). These eigenfunctions are mutually ORTHOGONAL -- the integral of sin x times sin 2x over a full period is zero -- and each has a positive length -- the integral of sin^2 over a period is pi. Because they form an orthogonal basis, ANY function can be written as a sum of them: that is the Fourier series. Finite or infinite, it is one idea -- a linear operator, its eigenvectors, and a basis.
use: To solve a linear differential equation, do what you do with a matrix: find the eigenfunctions of its operator and expand the answer in them. Heat flow, a vibrating string, a quantum particle -- each is governed by a differential operator whose eigenfunctions (sines, cosines, complex exponentials) are the natural notes of the problem, and the solution is a chord of those notes. This is why one set of tools -- eigenvalues, orthogonal bases, projection -- serves both a two-by-two matrix and the equations of physics. (In quantum mechanics the state itself IS a vector in a Hilbert space and the observables are operators upon it.)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 8/8. Eigen-equations: d/dx e^(kx) = k e^(kx) (exp is the eigenfunction of d/dx); sin(kx) and cos(kx) each solve y'' + k^2 y = 0 (eigenfunctions of d2/dx2, eigenvalue -k^2; a control with e^(kx) MISMATCHED). Inner product: the antiderivative of sin(x)sin(2x) is (sin x - sin 3x/3)/2, giving integral 0 over [0,2pi] -- ORTHOGONAL (a control claiming 1 MISMATCHED); the antiderivative of sin^2 is x/2 - sin(2x)/4, giving pi over [0,2pi] -- a positive norm. Finite anchor: matrix [[2,1],[1,2]] has eigenvalues 1 and 3, the same eigen-structure in finitely many dimensions.

cos(kx) likewise -- the two real eigenfunctions
antiderivative used to evaluate the inner product of two different eigenfunctions
<sin x, sin 2x> over a full period = 0: different eigenfunctions are orthogonal
antiderivative for the norm of an eigenfunction
||sin x||^2 over a period = pi: a positive length

wisdom: The whole of creation is a harmony of distinct voices joined, and it was so from the first. 'When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy' (Job 38:7). The pure notes -- the eigenfunctions -- are perpendicular and unmixed, yet every shape under heaven is their chord.

title: Every wiggly curve is a chord of pure, simple waves added together
verification: 'Hilbert space' sounds forbidding, but the idea is musical. Any wiggly curve -- a sound, a heartbeat trace, a tide chart -- can be built by adding up pure, simple waves (sines and cosines), just as a chord is built from single notes. Those pure waves are the natural shapes of the equations of nature, and they fit together without muddying one another: they are perpendicular, orthogonal. The engine confirmed that two different notes have zero overlap and each has its own honest size. This is the heart of Fourier's discovery and of how a function can be treated as a vector.
wisdom: Sing unto him a new song; play skilfully with a loud noise (Psalm 33:3). The world is built of clear notes joined in skill; to take a thing apart into its pure tones is to begin to understand its song.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Eigenfunctions (sines/cosines) are orthogonal with positive norm, so any function is a sum of them -- the Fourier idea, the Hilbert-space structure.

title: Marginal cost is just a derivative -- economics runs on the same rate-of-change as physics
situation: In economics, 'marginal' means 'the rate of change of'. Marginal cost is the derivative of total cost with respect to quantity: if making q items costs 5q^2 + 3q, the marginal cost is 10q + 3, what the next item adds. Marginal revenue is the derivative of total revenue: from 20q - q^2 it is 20 - 2q. And the central rule of the firm falls straight out of calculus -- profit (revenue minus cost) is greatest where its derivative is zero, which is exactly where MARGINAL COST EQUALS MARGINAL REVENUE. For these curves that point is q = 17/12, where both marginals equal 103/6, and the second derivative of profit is -12, confirming a true maximum. The economist's 'marginal', the mathematician's 'derivative', and the physicist's 'rate' are one idea wearing three coats.
use: To find a best quantity -- how much to make, buy, or sell -- write the total as a function and set its derivative (the marginal) to zero; for profit that lands you where marginal cost meets marginal revenue. The very same move optimizes a physical path or a chemical yield. Check the second derivative to be sure it is a peak (negative), not a valley. The tools of calculus are the tools of good stewardship.

bonds: optimization<->derivative-zero (MC = MR at the profit peak)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 5/5. Marginal cost = d/dq(5q^2+3q) = 10q+3 (a control missing the +3 MISMATCHED). Marginal revenue = d/dq(20q-q^2) = 20-2q. Profit 17q-6q^2 is maximized where 17-12q=0, i.e. q=17/12; there marginal cost = marginal revenue = 103/6; and the profit's second derivative is -12 (<0), a true maximum.

domain_results.note: marginal cost is the derivative of total cost: 10q+3
marginal revenue is the derivative of total revenue: 20-2q
profit is maximized at q=17/12 (derivative of profit zero)
at the optimum marginal cost = marginal revenue = 103/6
