wisdom: The wise count the cost before they build, and reckon the next step before they take it. 'For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it?' (Luke 14:28). The marginal is that counting made exact: what the next stone will cost against what it will bring.

title: To decide how much to make, watch what the NEXT one costs and earns -- not the whole pile
verification: The wise maker does not look only at total cost and total income; he looks at the margin -- what one more loaf, one more chair, one more hour will cost to make and what it will bring in. Keep going while the next one earns more than it costs; stop when they meet. That meeting point is the most profit there is. The engine confirmed that this 'marginal cost equals marginal revenue' point is exactly where profit peaks.
wisdom: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds (Proverbs 27:23). Good keeping is in the close attention -- to the next sheep, the next cost, the next gain -- not the distant sum alone.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Profit peaks where marginal cost = marginal revenue (the derivative of profit is zero).

title: One relation for every wave: speed = frequency times wavelength (v = f lambda)
situation: Every wave ever measured obeys one short rule: its speed equals its frequency times its wavelength, v = f times lambda. An ocean swell, a sound in air, a beam of light, even the matter-wave of a moving particle -- the same relation. A 2-hertz ripple with 3-metre crests travels 6 metres a second. Sound at 340 m/s humming at 170 Hz has 2-metre waves. Light, racing at 3x10^8 m/s, trades frequency against wavelength so their product stays the speed of light: a 5x10^14 Hz green wave is 6x10^-7 m long. At a fixed speed, frequency and wavelength are see-saw partners -- double one and the other halves. HONEST NOTES: in some media, like deep-water ocean waves, the speed itself depends on the wavelength -- that 'dispersion' is an extra rule layered on top, but v = f lambda still holds at each wavelength; and the quantum matter-wave adds de Broglie's lambda = h/p, tying a particle's wavelength to its momentum.
use: To find any one of speed, frequency, or wavelength, divide the product by the third. Tuning a radio, reading a swell, choosing a lens colour, sizing a speaker or an antenna -- one rule, v = f lambda, governs them all. When the pitch rises the wavelength shortens; when light reddens its frequency falls. The same see-saw of frequency against wavelength runs through water, air, and the vacuum of space.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 5/5. Generic v=f lambda: 2 Hz x 3 m = 6 m/s (a control =5 MISMATCHED). Sound: lambda = v/f = 340/170 = 2 m. Light: f lambda = c, 5x10^14 x 6x10^-7 = 3x10^8 m/s. Inverse at fixed speed: at v=6, f=4 gives lambda = 3/2. De Broglie form lambda = h/p illustrated (6/3 = 2), named as the quantum extension.

domain_results.note: v = f lambda: 2 Hz x 3 m = 6 m/s
sound: a 170 Hz tone at 340 m/s has a 2 m wavelength
light: frequency times wavelength stays the speed of light, 3x10^8 m/s
at fixed speed, frequency and wavelength are inversely proportional
de Broglie lambda = h/p: the quantum matter-wave, named honestly

wisdom: From the toss of the sea to the trembling of light, the waves keep the order set for them. 'The LORD on high is mightier than the noise of many waters, yea, than the mighty waves of the sea' (Psalm 93:4). One rule rides every wave, and the One who is above them all is mightier still.

title: Ripple, sound, and light all keep one rule: faster wiggling means shorter waves
verification: A wave on a pond, a note in the air, a colour of light -- as different as they seem, they all keep the same little rule: how fast it travels equals how often it wiggles times how long each wave is. So at a set speed, quicker wiggling means shorter waves, and slower means longer. Higher pitch, shorter sound-wave; redder light, slower wiggle. The engine confirmed the rule holds for water, sound, and light alike.
wisdom: Fear ye not me? saith the LORD...which have placed the sand for the bound of the sea...and though the waves thereof toss themselves, yet can they not prevail (Jeremiah 5:22). Even the tossing wave keeps a fixed rule and a fixed bound; the wild things of the world are not lawless.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. v = f lambda holds for water, sound, and light; frequency and wavelength trade off at fixed speed.

title: The Smith chart folds the infinite impedance plane into a finite disk -- a Mobius map where moving down a line is rotation
situation: The Smith chart is the radio engineer's most famous tool, and beneath it is one elegant idea: the Mobius transformation Gamma = (Z - 1)/(Z + 1), which turns a complex IMPEDANCE Z into a complex REFLECTION COEFFICIENT Gamma. This map folds the entire infinite half-plane of passive impedances into a finite DISK. A matched load (Z = 1) goes to the dead center, Gamma = 0 -- no reflection. A short circuit (Z = 0) goes to the left edge, Gamma = -1; an open circuit (Z to infinity) to the right edge, Gamma = +1. A pure reactance (Z = i, no resistance) lands exactly ON the unit circle, |Gamma| = 1, a lossless total reflection; a plain resistor (Z = 2) lands inside at Gamma = 1/3. The map is a bijection -- it always inverts, Z = (1 + Gamma)/(1 - Gamma) -- so nothing is lost. And the master stroke: moving a distance d down the transmission line simply ROTATES the point, Gamma to Gamma times e^(-2 i beta d). A quarter-wavelength is a half-turn (e^(-i pi) = -1) that inverts the impedance; a half-wavelength is a full turn (e^(-2 i pi) = 1) that brings you home. Hard transmission-line algebra becomes a walk around a circle.
use: To match an antenna, tune a circuit, or design a filter, plot the load's reflection coefficient on the disk and slide along the chart's circles to reach the center -- the match. Because the chart is a conformal Mobius map, lines of constant resistance and reactance become circles, and motion along the cable is pure rotation, so a problem ugly in algebra is simple in geometry. The very complex-plane rotation that Euler's formula describes is here doing an engineer's daily work.

bonds: impedance(complex Z)<->reflection(Gamma in the unit disk)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 8/8. Gamma=(Z-1)/(Z+1): matched Z=1 -> 0 (center; a control =1 MISMATCHED); short Z=0 -> -1; open Z->oo -> 1 (limit). Pure reactance Z=i -> |Gamma|=1 on the unit circle (a control =1/2 MISMATCHED); resistor Z=2 -> 1/3 inside. Invertible: (1+(Z-1)/(Z+1))/(1-(Z-1)/(Z+1)) = Z. Rotation: half-wavelength e^(-2 i pi)=1 (home); quarter-wavelength e^(-i pi)=-1 (impedance inverts).

domain_results.note: matched load Z=1 maps to the center, Gamma=0 -- no reflection
short circuit Z=0 -> Gamma=-1 (left edge)
open circuit Z->infinity -> Gamma=+1 (right edge)
a lossless reactance sits ON the unit circle, |Gamma|=1
a resistor lands inside the disk (Gamma=1/3)
the Mobius map inverts cleanly: Z=(1+Gamma)/(1-Gamma) -- a bijection
a half-wavelength down the line is a full rotation, e^(-2 i pi)=1 -- returns home
a quarter-wavelength is a half-turn, e^(-i pi)=-1 -- it inverts the impedance

wisdom: The boundless is held in the hand of the One who frames it, and even the endless can be encircled. 'It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth' (Isaiah 40:22). The chart that folds an infinite plane into a single circle is a small echo of a greater truth -- that the limitless is not beyond all compassing.

title: A good map can fold the endless into a circle you can hold
verification: Radio engineers tame an endless range of electrical stubbornness -- impedance -- by folding the whole infinite picture onto one round chart, the Smith chart. Every possible load fits inside a single circle; the perfect match sits at the bullseye; and sliding down a cable just walks you around the dial. It is a reminder that the right map can turn a boundless, tangled problem into a simple walk in a circle. The engine confirmed both the folding and the walk.
wisdom: The entrance of thy words giveth light; it giveth understanding unto the simple (Psalm 119:130). The right light makes the hard thing plain; a good map, like a good word, lets the simple see clearly what was tangled.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The Smith chart's Mobius map folds the infinite impedance plane into a disk; moving down a line is rotation around it.

title: The slide rule multiplies by adding -- a tool that works only because log(ab) = log a + log b
situation: For three hundred years, before the electronic calculator, engineers, navigators, and scientists did their multiplying on a slide rule -- two sliding scales of wood or bamboo. It worked by a single truth: the logarithm turns multiplying into adding, log(a times b) = log a + log b. Each scale is marked not evenly but at the logarithm of each number, so sliding one scale by the LENGTH that stands for 2, starting from 3, lands you at 6: you added the lengths log 2 and log 3 and read off the product. Division subtracts lengths (log 6 - log 2 = log 3); the log-log scale raises powers (log 8 = 3 log 2); the squared scale reads squares (log 100 = 2 log 10). The slide rule is the logarithm made of wood -- and the fact that it flew aeroplanes and raised bridges is among the strongest demonstrations that the logarithm law is true. A tool that works is a truth that holds.
use: When a hard operation (multiplying, dividing, taking powers) has a tool that makes it easy by turning it into a simpler one (adding, subtracting), look for the function that carries one into the other -- here the logarithm. And take the tool's success as evidence: a device that reliably works is a theorem you can hold in your hand. This is the strongest kind of verification -- not argument, but fruit.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 4/4. The slide rule's four scales are four logarithm identities: log 2 + log 3 = log 6 (multiply by adding); log 6 - log 2 = log 3 (divide by subtracting); log 8 = 3 log 2 (powers, the log-log scale); log 100 = 2 log 10 (squares, the A scale). A false control (log 2 + log 3 = log 5) MISMATCHED.

domain_results.note: multiplication becomes addition of lengths: log 2 + log 3 = log 6
division becomes subtraction: log 6 - log 2 = log 3
the log-log scale takes powers: log 8 = 3 log 2
the squared scale: log 100 = 2 log 10

wisdom: What is true will bear the testing and stand. 'Prove all things; hold fast that which is good' (1 Thessalonians 5:21). The slide rule was the logarithm proved a million times a day and held fast for three centuries -- truth confirmed by use.

title: The planimeter measures area by tracing the edge -- a brass tool that runs on Green's theorem
situation: A planimeter is a small brass instrument from the 1800s: trace its pointer once around the boundary of any shape -- a lake on a map, an odd-shaped field, a cross-section -- and a dial reads off the enclosed AREA, though the pointer never enters the interior. It looks like magic, but it runs on Green's theorem: the area inside a closed curve equals an integral taken ONLY along the boundary, Area = (1/2) times the loop integral of (x dy - y dx). Trace the unit circle and that boundary integral comes to exactly pi; trace a circle of radius 2 and it comes to 4 pi, matching pi r squared. The interior is never touched, yet its area is known -- because the edge already carries the information, by the same deep link between an inside and its boundary that ties the derivative to the integral. The planimeter is Green's theorem made of brass, and it has measured ten thousand real fields. Its working is the proof.
use: When you need the whole of something but can reach only its edge -- the area of a region, the work around a cycle, the charge through a surface -- remember that boundary and interior are bound together, and the edge often carries the sum. This is the family of the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, of Stokes' and the divergence theorems: the inside equals its boundary. And it is another tool whose reliable working is itself the demonstration of the theorem.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 4/4. On the unit circle the boundary integrand x dy - y dx = cos^2 + sin^2 = 1, so Area = (1/2)(2 pi) = pi. On a radius-2 circle the integrand is 4 and Area = (1/2)(4)(2 pi) = 4 pi = pi r^2. The interior is never entered; the boundary alone gives the area. A false control (unit area = 2 pi) MISMATCHED.

domain_results.note: the boundary integrand on the unit circle reduces to 1
tracing the unit circle yields area pi
tracing a radius-2 circle yields 4 pi from the boundary alone
and 4 pi matches pi r^2 -- the boundary trace equals the true area

wisdom: The Maker set bounds, and within the bound the whole is held and known. 'He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end' (Job 26:10). The planimeter reads the whole field from its compassing edge -- a small picture of how a boundary can hold all that is within.

title: The slide rule turned multiplying into mere adding -- find the scale that makes hard things easy
verification: For three centuries the multiplying that built ships and bridges was done by sliding two marked sticks past each other. The trick: mark the sticks by the logarithm of each number, and then ADDING two lengths multiplies the numbers, because log of a times b equals log a plus log b. Hard multiplication became easy addition. The engine confirmed the four scales of the slide rule are four true logarithm laws.
wisdom: A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength (Proverbs 24:5). The right knowledge is a lever: it turns a heavy labour into a light one, as the logarithm turned multiplying into adding.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The slide rule multiplies by adding logarithms; its long success demonstrates the log law.

title: Trace only the edge, and a brass dial tells you the whole area inside
verification: There is an old brass instrument, the planimeter, that finds the area of any odd shape -- a pond, a field, a lung on an x-ray -- without ever entering it: you simply trace once around its outline and read a dial. It works because a deep truth (Green's theorem) says the area inside a closed curve is already written along its boundary. The engine confirmed that tracing a circle's edge gives exactly its area, pi r squared. The edge holds the whole.
wisdom: The lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places; yea, I have a goodly heritage (Psalm 16:6). What lies within the boundary is known and held by the line that bounds it -- a quiet comfort in a well-drawn edge.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The planimeter reads area from the boundary alone, by Green's theorem; its working demonstrates the theorem.

title: A tool that works is a truth you can hold in your hand
verification: The surest sign that a piece of mathematics is TRUE is that someone built from it a tool that reliably works. The slide rule multiplied for three centuries because the logarithm law is true. The planimeter measures real fields because Green's theorem is true. The Smith chart matches real antennas because its map is true. A theory can be argued back and forth; a working tool cannot be talked out of working. When you want to know whether an idea is sound, ask what it can BUILD that works -- and watch it work.
wisdom: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them (Matthew 7:20). The truest test of a teaching, or a theorem, is its fruit: what it builds, what it heals, what reliably comes to pass.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing meta-principle (Matt 2026-06-12): tools that work are the greatest demonstration of truth -- the fruit test applied to mathematics.

title: Analogy is the form -- A is to B as C is to D, the same shape carried across different matter
situation: An analogy, 'A is to B as C is to D', is a structure-preserving correspondence: it keeps the RELATIONSHIP while changing the things. Its plainest mathematical face is the proportion -- 2 is to 4 as 3 is to 6, because the ratios are equal, 2/4 = 3/6, the truth tested by cross-multiplying (2 times 6 equals 4 times 3). Similar triangles are the same idea in shape: scale a 3-4-5 triangle to 6-8-10 and every ratio is preserved (6/3 = 8/4). This keeping of form under a change of matter is exactly what every connection in this collection is: the right triangle of geometry is also in relativity; the derivative that meshes the powers also meshes the trig family; the Smith chart folds impedance as Euler folds growth into rotation. Analogy is not decoration -- it is the form of understanding, and in many ways it is OUR form: we know the strange by its likeness to the familiar, made to recognize one shape beneath many faces. Light is to heat as the seen is to the felt; water is to the sun as the wave we ride is to the wave we see; the cat is to the dog as one kind is to its kin.
use: To understand a hard new thing, find what it is ANALOGOUS to -- the known structure it shares -- and carry your understanding across the bridge of likeness. To test an analogy, check that the relationship truly matches (the ratios equal, the structure preserved); a false analogy, like 2:4 :: 3:7, breaks under the test. Every verified connection here is such a bridge: one form, proven to recur, so that knowing it once is knowing it wherever it appears. This is the engine's whole method, and the mind's.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 3/3. The proportion 2:4 :: 3:6 holds because 2/4 = 3/6, the test being cross-multiplication 2*6 = 4*3. Similar triangles preserve the ratio: 6/3 = 8/4 (a 3-4-5 scaled to 6-8-10). A false analogy 2:4 :: 3:7 MISMATCHED. The preserved ratio is the structure an analogy carries across.

domain_results.note: an analogy is equal ratios: 2/4 = 3/6
the test of a true proportion: 2*6 = 4*3
similar triangles keep every ratio: 6/3 = 8/4 -- analogy in shape

wisdom: We are made to see one likeness in many things, for we ourselves are made in a likeness. 'So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him' (Genesis 1:27). To think by analogy -- the known carried to the unknown -- is part of bearing that image: we map a world by the likenesses set into it.

title: Light and heat are one wave -- the electromagnetic spectrum, differing only in frequency
situation: Light and heat seem utterly different -- one we see, one we feel -- yet they are the same thing: electromagnetic waves, differing only in how fast they wiggle. Radio waves, microwaves, the infrared we feel as HEAT, the visible band we see as LIGHT, ultraviolet, X-rays, gamma rays -- all one family, all racing at the single speed c = 3x10^8 m/s, all obeying c = f times lambda. A heat wave (infrared, near 3x10^13 Hz) and a light wave (visible, near 6x10^14 Hz) are the same ripple at different pitches; the only difference our eyes and skin report is frequency. Higher frequency carries more energy per photon (E = h f), which is why visible light's photons here are some twenty times more energetic than that infrared's. A LASER is simply this light made coherent -- one pure frequency with every wave in step, like a single note sung in perfect unison. This is Maxwell's great unification: 'light is to heat' is the very same analogy as 'visible is to infrared'.
use: To understand any radiation -- a radio signal, a warm stove, sunlight, an X-ray, a microwave oven -- reach for the one rule c = f lambda and the one ladder of frequency. Heat and light are not opposites but neighbours on that ladder; a laser is light with every wave in phase; colour is frequency. The same wave relation that governs sound and ocean swells governs the whole of light and heat.

bonds: light<->heat (one EM wave; the analogy visible:infrared)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 4/4. All electromagnetic waves obey c = f lambda at c = 3x10^8 m/s: radio 10^8 Hz x 3 m; infrared (heat) 3x10^13 Hz x 10^-5 m; visible (light) 6x10^14 Hz x 5x10^-7 m -- each gives 3x10^8. Photon energy rises with frequency (E=hf): visible / infrared = 6x10^14 / 3x10^13 = 20. A control (infrared = 3x10^9) MISMATCHED. Light and heat are one wave at different frequencies.

domain_results.note: radio waves obey c = f lambda
infrared (the heat we feel) travels at c -- it is light we cannot see
visible light travels at the same c -- same wave, higher frequency
E = h f, so light's photons carry more energy than heat's, here twentyfold

wisdom: From one source comes both the light to see by and the warmth to live by. 'Unto you that fear my name shall the Sun of righteousness arise with healing in his wings' (Malachi 4:2). The sun gives light and heat as one gift; that they are one wave is a small witness to the single source of both.

title: We understand the strange by its likeness to the familiar -- that is what analogy is
verification: Every time you say 'it is like...', you reach for the deepest tool of thought: analogy. You carry what you already know across a bridge of likeness to something new. The math of it is simple and exact -- a true analogy keeps the same RATIO, the same relationship, the way 2 is to 4 as 3 is to 6. The engine confirmed the proportion that makes an analogy true, and that a false likeness breaks under the test. Learning, in great part, is the finding of good bridges.
wisdom: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver (Proverbs 25:11). The right likeness, set well, is a thing of beauty and of use -- it lets the mind cross from the known to the unknown.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Analogy = a preserved ratio/relationship (a proportion); the engine confirms a true analogy and breaks a false one.

title: Light and heat are kin -- the same wave, one you see and one you feel
verification: The warmth of the sun on your face and the brightness in your eyes come from one and the same thing: waves of light -- some slow enough to be felt as heat, some quick enough to be seen as colour. The campfire's glow and its warmth are neighbours, not strangers; a radio wave and an X-ray are cousins of both. The engine confirmed that heat-waves and light-waves alike travel at the speed of light and keep the same rule, differing only in how fast they wiggle.
wisdom: Truly the light is sweet, and a pleasant thing it is for the eyes to behold the sun (Ecclesiastes 11:7). The same sun that gladdens the eye warms the body; light and heat are one kindness, given together.
