title: A probability density is the shape of chance -- and the Rayleigh shape is the length of a two-dimensional random vector
situation: A probability density function (PDF) is the shape that says where a random quantity is likely to fall: the probability of landing in any range is the AREA under the curve over that range, and the total area is always 1 (the thing must land somewhere). The PDF is the slope (derivative) of the cumulative distribution, and the cumulative is the running area (integral) of the PDF -- the same rate-and-accumulation pair that runs all of calculus. The RAYLEIGH density, f(x) = (x/sigma^2) e^(-x^2/(2 sigma^2)) for x >= 0, is a beautiful case: it is the distribution of the LENGTH of a vector whose two components are independent bell-curve (Gaussian) randoms -- so it is the sum-of-squares (Pythagoras: r = sqrt(X^2 + Y^2)) married to the normal curve. Its area is exactly 1; its peak, the most likely value, sits at x = sigma; and it rises from zero, crests, and decays. This is why it governs WIND SPEED (combining the east-west and north-south gusts), OCEAN WAVE HEIGHTS (the significant-wave-height statistics mariners live by), the magnitude of random noise, and the scatter of shots around a target. Randomness has an architecture, and the PDF is its blueprint.
use: To reason about a random quantity, find its PDF: areas under it are probabilities (total 1), its peak is the most likely value, and its derivative-and-integral tie to the cumulative distribution is the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus. When a magnitude is the length of two independent Gaussian parts -- wind from two components, wave height, radial miss-distance -- expect the Rayleigh shape, peaking at sigma. HONEST NOTE: the verified facts here are the PDF/CDF derivative link, the unit total area, and the peak at sigma; the mean sigma*sqrt(pi/2) and the two-Gaussian origin are named standard results.

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 5/5 for the Rayleigh (sigma=1): pdf f=x*exp(-x^2/2), cdf F=1-exp(-x^2/2). The PDF is the derivative of the CDF (a control missing the x MISMATCHED). The antiderivative of the PDF is -exp(-x^2/2), so the total area is [0 at infinity] - [-1 at 0] = 1 (normalized). The PDF's own derivative is (1-x^2)exp(-x^2/2), zero at x=1, so the peak (mode) is at x=sigma.

domain_results.note: PDF = derivative of the cumulative distribution (FTC)
antiderivative of the Rayleigh PDF is -exp(-x^2/2)
the total area under the PDF is 1 -- it must land somewhere
the PDF's derivative is (1-x^2)exp(-x^2/2)
the peak sits at x = sigma (here 1)

wisdom: The same hand sets the shape of wind and wave together. 'For he commandeth, and raiseth the stormy wind, which lifteth up the waves thereof' (Psalm 107:25). The Rayleigh curve is the one shape behind both the gust and the swell -- chance given a form, and the form held in a steady hand.

title: Chance has a shape -- and the whole of it always adds to one
verification: A random thing is not formless -- it has a 'density', a curve showing where it is likely to land: tall where outcomes cluster, low where they are rare. The area under the whole curve is always exactly 1, because the thing must land somewhere. Wind speeds and ocean wave heights follow one such shape, the Rayleigh curve, which arises from combining two independent bell-curves at right angles -- the same square-root-of-the-sum-of-squares that measures any distance. The engine confirmed the curve's total area is 1 and its peak sits at the typical value.
wisdom: Thou compassest my path and my lying down, and art acquainted with all my ways (Psalm 139:3). Even the scatter of a life has a shape that is known and held; chance to us is a shape already seen by Him.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. A PDF is the shape of chance (total area 1); the Rayleigh shape is the length of a 2D Gaussian (wind, waves), peaking at sigma.

title: The Riemann Hypothesis -- an open question about where the zeta function vanishes, with the established facts sealed and the conjecture marked honestly
situation: The Riemann zeta function, zeta(s) = the sum of 1/k^s, is the deepest known bridge between the smooth world of analysis and the prime numbers. Several of its truths are firmly ESTABLISHED and sealed here: the Basel sum zeta(2) = pi^2/6, and zeta(4) = pi^4/90; the 'trivial zeros' at the negative even integers (zeta(-2) = 0); the special value zeta(0) = -1/2; and Euler's product, which writes zeta as a product over the PRIMES, each factor a geometric series in a single prime (1/(1 - 1/4) = 4/3 is the prime-2 factor at s=2) -- this product is exactly why zeta encodes the primes. The RIEMANN HYPOTHESIS is the still-OPEN conjecture about this function: that every NON-trivial zero (the zeros off the negative even integers -- the ones that govern how the primes are spread) has real part exactly 1/2, all lying on a single 'critical line'. If true, it would pin down the distribution of primes more tightly than anything now known. HONEST BOUNDARY: the Riemann Hypothesis is UNPROVEN. It has been checked numerically for many trillions of zeros, every one on the line, and it stands as one of the Clay Millennium Prize problems -- but numerical agreement is evidence, not proof, and no proof exists. What is sealed in this card are the surrounding established facts, NOT the Hypothesis itself. We map what is proven; we mark the open question as open.
use: When a problem joins two far-apart worlds -- here the smooth analysis of zeta and the discrete primes -- the bridge object often holds the key, and its zeros encode the hidden structure. Use the established zeta values and the Euler product freely; but treat the Riemann Hypothesis as a load-bearing CONJECTURE: a great many results are proved only 'assuming RH', and they stay conditional until it is settled. An honest map distinguishes the proven scaffolding from the unproven keystone -- and this card does, sealing the facts and naming the Hypothesis as open.

bonds: RIEMANN HYPOTHESIS = OPEN conjecture (nontrivial zeros on Re=1/2) -- NOT proven

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 6/6 -- the ESTABLISHED facts only, not the Hypothesis. zeta(2)=pi^2/6 (Basel), zeta(4)=pi^4/90, zeta(-2)=0 (a trivial zero), zeta(0)=-1/2, and the Euler product's prime-factor as a geometric series (sum (1/4)^k = 4/3 = 1/(1-1/4)). A control zeta(2)=pi^2/7 MISMATCHED. The Riemann Hypothesis itself (all nontrivial zeros on Re=1/2) is UNPROVEN and is explicitly NOT among the sealed claims.

domain_results.note: established: zeta(2) = pi^2/6 (the Basel problem)
established: zeta(4) = pi^4/90
a trivial zero: zeta(-2) = 0
each Euler factor is a geometric series in one prime: sum (1/4)^k = 4/3
that factor in closed form: 1/(1-1/4) = 4/3 -- the sieve that ties zeta to primes

pre_run.conjecture_boundary: The Riemann Hypothesis (nontrivial zeros all on Re(s)=1/2) is UNPROVEN; only the surrounding established facts are sealed.

wisdom: We see truly, but not yet wholly; the deepest things are known only in part until the day they are made plain. 'For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known' (1 Corinthians 13:12). The honest map keeps the proven and the merely-believed apart, and waits.

title: Some things look certainly true and still are not proven -- an honest record says which is which
verification: There is a famous riddle in mathematics, the Riemann Hypothesis, about how the prime numbers are spread. Computers have checked it in trillions of cases and it holds every single time -- yet no one has shown it must always hold, and a prize awaits whoever does. It is a good reminder: 'checked a trillion times' is strong evidence, but it is not the same as proof. An honest record keeps the two apart -- what is established (and several true facts about the zeta function are sealed) and what is still only believed. We map what is proven, and we mark the open questions open.
wisdom: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain unto it (Psalm 139:6). There is no shame in an unsolved thing rightly named; the honest mind holds its certainties and its hopes in separate hands.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The RH is unproven (open Millennium problem); only established zeta facts are sealed -- evidence is not proof.

title: The harmonic series: shrinking terms that still add to infinity -- and it grows like the logarithm
situation: Add up 1 + 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/4 + ... -- the harmonic series. Each term is smaller than the last, shrinking toward zero, so it seems the total should settle. It does NOT: the sum grows without bound, to infinity. The proof is a beautiful grouping: 1/3 + 1/4 is more than 1/2; 1/5 + 1/6 + 1/7 + 1/8 is more than 1/2; the next eight terms more than 1/2 again -- and there are endlessly many such blocks, each adding more than a half, so the sum climbs past every bound. This is the classic warning that terms going to zero is NECESSARY but not SUFFICIENT for convergence (the limit of 1/k is indeed 0, yet the sum diverges). It diverges only just barely, and LOGARITHMICALLY: the sum of the first n terms is about ln(n) plus a constant, so to reach a total of 20 you would need astronomically many terms. It is the exact BOUNDARY of the p-series, sum of 1/k^p: for any power p above 1 the sum converges (1/k^2 gives pi^2/6), but at p = 1, the harmonic, it just diverges. And its ALTERNATING form, 1 - 1/2 + 1/3 - 1/4 + ..., does converge -- to ln 2 exactly. The harmonic series sits on the knife-edge between adding up and running away, and the logarithm marks the edge.
use: When summing terms that shrink, do not assume the total is finite -- the harmonic series is the standard caution; what matters is the RATE of shrinkage (powers above 1 converge; 1/k and slower diverge). Its logarithmic growth is why 'diminishing but not fast enough' totals -- coupon-collector waits, certain random-walk and resistor-ladder sums, the expected longest run -- pile up slowly yet without limit. And alternating a divergent series can tame it: 1 - 1/2 + 1/3 - ... settles to ln 2.

bonds: p-series boundary at p=1 (the zeta line)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 5/5. The partial sum H_4 = 25/12 (exact). Terms vanish: lim 1/x -> 0 (necessary). Yet it diverges by blocks: 1/3+1/4 > 1/2 and 1/5+1/6+1/7+1/8 > 1/2 (a control claiming < 1/2 MISMATCHED) -- endlessly many half-units. The alternating harmonic sum (-1)^(k+1)/k converges to ln 2.

domain_results.note: exact partial sum H_4 = 25/12
the terms go to zero -- necessary but, as this shows, not sufficient
1/3+1/4 > 1/2: the first divergence block
1/5+...+1/8 > 1/2: endlessly many such blocks -> infinity
the alternating harmonic series converges to ln 2

wisdom: The slow and small, never ceasing, wears down even the great in time. 'The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth' (Job 14:19). The harmonic series is such a wearing -- each drop tinier than the last, yet the sum, in the end, surpasses all bounds.

title: Add ever-smaller steps and you can still travel infinitely far -- if they do not shrink fast enough
verification: Here is a surprise: add 1 + 1/2 + 1/3 + 1/4 + ... on forever, each piece smaller than the last, and the total does not settle -- it grows without limit, all the way to infinity. It is slow (to pass even 20 you would need more terms than there are stars you can see), creeping up like a logarithm, but it never stops. The lesson: shrinking is not enough; what matters is HOW FAST. Halving each time (1/2 + 1/4 + 1/8 ...) does settle, at 1 -- but the gentler harmonic shrink runs away. The engine confirmed it, block by block.
wisdom: If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed... nothing shall be impossible unto you (Matthew 17:20). The least things, added without ceasing, can outrun every bound; do not despise a small and steady beginning.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. The harmonic series diverges (slowly, like ln n) though its terms vanish; the alternating version converges to ln 2.

title: Inverse even powers fold into pi; inverse odd powers escape into mystery -- the two faces of the zeta function
situation: Sum the inverse powers of the whole numbers and a startling split appears. For every EVEN power, the sum is a rational number times a power of pi: zeta(2) = 1 + 1/4 + 1/9 + ... = pi^2/6 (the Basel sum), zeta(4) = pi^4/90, zeta(6) = pi^6/945, zeta(8) = pi^8/9450, and so on forever -- Euler proved that zeta of any even number is a clean rational multiple of pi raised to that even power. Pi, the child of the circle, hides inside sums that mention no circle at all. But the ODD powers are a different country. zeta(3) = 1 + 1/8 + 1/27 + ... (its partial sum after three terms is exactly 251/216) converges to a number, Apery's constant, about 1.202 -- and that number is PROVEN irrational (Apery, 1979), yet it has NO known closed form as a rational multiple of any power of pi. The deeper odd values zeta(5), zeta(7), and beyond are believed irrational but, for the most part, not even that is proven. So the zeta function wears two faces: at the even integers it is pi, transparent and solved; at the odd integers it is a set of strange constants, partly proven irrational, partly still open. We seal what Euler established, and mark the odd-power mysteries as open.
use: When summing inverse powers, expect a pi-form for even exponents (Euler's rational times pi^(2n)); for odd exponents expect an irrational constant with no simple closed form -- treat Apery's constant zeta(3) as its own named number (it shows up in physics, e.g. corrections to the electron's magnetic moment). The honest stance, kept in this card: zeta(even) = rational x pi-power is PROVEN; zeta(3) irrational is PROVEN; the irrationality of zeta(5), zeta(7), and the rest is largely OPEN.

bonds: odd-power zeta<->irrational, no pi-form (zeta(3)=Apery, proven irrational)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 6/6. Even powers are rational x a power of pi: zeta(2)=pi^2/6, zeta(4)=pi^4/90, zeta(6)=pi^6/945, zeta(8)=pi^8/9450, and zeta(2)/pi^2 = 1/6 (rational). A control zeta(4)=pi^4/100 MISMATCHED. Odd: zeta(3)'s partial sum 1+1/8+1/27 = 251/216 (exact); the full zeta(3) is Apery's constant, PROVEN irrational, with NO known pi closed form -- named, not sealed. zeta(5),zeta(7)... irrationality is OPEN.

zeta(4) = pi^4/90
zeta(2)/pi^2 = 1/6 -- even-power zeta is a rational multiple of a pi-power
zeta(8) = pi^8/9450 -- the pattern continues
zeta(3) partial sum 251/216; the limit (Apery's constant) is irrational with no pi-form

pre_run.conjecture_boundary: zeta(even)=rational*pi-power and zeta(3) irrational are PROVEN; irrationality of zeta(5),zeta(7),... is OPEN. Odd-power values have no known closed form in pi.

wisdom: Some things are given us to know, and some are kept; both are right. 'The secret things belong unto the LORD our God: but those things which are revealed belong unto us and to our children for ever' (Deuteronomy 29:29). The even powers are revealed -- pi, and plain; the odd powers are, for now, among the secret things, and we say so.

title: Add one-over-the-squares forever and out comes pi -- but the cubes keep their secret
verification: Here is a small marvel: add 1 + 1/4 + 1/9 + 1/16 + ... (one over the squares) on forever, and the total is pi-squared over six -- pi, the circle's own number, appearing where there is no circle in sight. The same happens for the fourth powers, the sixth, every even power: always pi to that power, times a simple fraction. But add the CUBES, 1 + 1/8 + 1/27 + ..., and the answer (about 1.202) is a strange number with no such tidy pi-form -- known to be irrational, yet otherwise still a bit of a mystery. The engine sealed the even-power pi-values and marked the odd ones honestly as open.
wisdom: Whither shall I go from thy spirit? or whither shall I flee from thy presence?...If I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there (Psalm 139:7-8). Pi turns up where no circle was drawn -- a small picture of a presence that fills even the places we did not think to look.

pre_run.summary: Human-facing. Inverse even powers sum to rational multiples of pi-powers (zeta(2)=pi^2/6); odd powers give irrational constants (zeta(3)) with no known pi-form.

title: Can we find an alternative for zeta(3)? Yes -- not a pi-form (none exists), but Apery's faster series, the one that proved it irrational
situation: Having found that the odd zeta values resist a clean pi closed form, the right question is asked: can we find an ALTERNATIVE? The answer is a careful yes. There is NO known way to write zeta(3) = 1 + 1/8 + 1/27 + ... as a rational multiple of pi-cubed (that pi-form alternative does not exist, as far as anyone knows). But there is a genuine alternative REPRESENTATION, and it is famous: Apery's series, zeta(3) = (5/2) times the sum over n of (-1)^(n-1) / (n^3 times C(2n,n)), where C(2n,n) is the central binomial coefficient. This series converges far faster than the plain one: its first two terms already give (5/2)(1/2 - 1/48) = 115/96, about 1.198, while the plain series needs many more terms to do as well (three terms of the plain series give only 251/216, about 1.162 -- and zeta(3) is about 1.202). This was no curiosity but a key: in 1978 Roger Apery used exactly such an accelerated representation to PROVE that zeta(3) is irrational, a result that had resisted everyone. The lesson is the engine's own creed: when one form is barred, do not stop at 'no closed form' -- seek the alternative that exists. A different shape of the same truth is often the one that unlocks it.
use: When a quantity has no closed form in the shape you wanted, look for an alternative REPRESENTATION -- a faster series, an integral, a continued fraction, a generating function -- that is exact and tractable. Apery's series is the model: the same number, a different and better-converging form, and the new form proved a theorem the old one could not. More broadly: a barred path is not a dead end; it is a prompt to change the form of the question.

bonds: no rational x pi^3 form (none known)

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 4/4. Apery's series term at n=1 is 1/2; its two-term partial sum is 23/48; so the estimate (5/2)(23/48) = 115/96 (about 1.198) -- already closer to zeta(3) (about 1.202) than the plain series' three-term sum 251/216 (about 1.162). A control (Apery 2-term = 1/2) MISMATCHED.

the plain series needs more: three terms give only 251/216, about 1.162

wisdom: Where the old road ends, a new one can be opened; the Maker delights to make a way where there was none. 'Behold, I will do a new thing...I will even make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert' (Isaiah 43:19). So the barred pi-form gave way to Apery's path, and the path became the proof.

title: When one road to the answer is barred, look for another -- a different form of the same truth
verification: Sometimes a number cannot be written in the neat shape you hoped for. The famous constant 1 + 1/8 + 1/27 + ... (about 1.202) has no tidy form in terms of pi, the way its even-power cousins do. A dead end? No. There is an ALTERNATIVE way to write it -- a cleverer, faster-adding series found by Apery -- and that very alternative was what let him prove the number is irrational, which no one had managed before. The lesson reaches past mathematics: when the obvious path is closed, the answer often waits down a different road. The engine confirmed that the alternative's terms add up faster.
wisdom: There hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will...with the temptation also make a way to escape, that ye may be able to bear it (1 Corinthians 10:13). When the way ahead seems shut, a way is provided; seek it, and do not call the matter hopeless too soon.

title: What maintains coherence when it could break apart -- groups, the Smith chart, DNA, words, peoples: closure is the form they share
situation: Some things hold together when entropy, or mere convenience, would let them dissolve -- and those are the ones that carry identity and meaning forward. The mathematical name for this holding-together is CLOSURE: a GROUP is a set whose elements, combined, never escape it. Add two hours on a clock and you land on an hour; the numbers mod 5 under addition are closed (3+4 = 2, still inside), with a do-nothing IDENTITY (4+0=4) and an UNDO for every element (3+2=0), the combination ASSOCIATIVE; the nonzero residues are likewise closed under multiplication (2x3=1). The SMITH CHART is the same form in disguise: its Mobius transformations form a group -- two slides down a transmission line compose by ADDING their angles (e^(-2 i b d1) times e^(-2 i b d2) = e^(-2 i b (d1+d2))), zero slide is the identity, and the map inverts cleanly. This abstract coherence is the deep layer beneath things that seem far apart. DNA holds its code coherent across a billion replications, base faithfully pairing with base, so the message is not lost. WORDS hold a cluster of sounds together as one unit of meaning when it would be easier to scatter into noise -- and the words that kept their coherence are the ones that survived to carry sense across the centuries. PEOPLES and persons keep an identity coherent against every pressure to assimilate or fall apart, and so endure. Even PHONICS, PHEROMONES, and PHENOTYPES share the shape: a hidden code held coherent and faithfully expressed across a gap -- letters to sounds, gene to signal, genotype to living form. And EUCLID's Elements is coherence as method: a whole world of geometry held together, deduced without a crack from a handful of axioms. Closure -- the refusal to dissolve under the operation -- is one form, and it is found wherever meaning is kept.
use: When you find something that persists -- a stable system, a conserved code, a lasting word, an enduring people -- look for the closure that holds it: the operation under which its parts combine and never escape, the identity that changes nothing, the inverse that lets it return. Build for coherence: a system closed under its own operations (a group, a well-formed grammar, a faithful copying mechanism) maintains itself; one that leaks does not. To last is to be closed under what you do.

bonds: phonics/pheromones/phenotypes<->coherent code expressed across a gap

pre_run.summary: Sealed HOLDS 9/9. Closure as coherence, across three groups: the additive group mod 5 (3+4=2 inside, identity 4+0=4, inverse 3+2=0, associative), the multiplicative group (2x3=1, 2x4=3 still inside), and the SMITH-CHART Mobius group (two displacements add: e^(-2ibd1)e^(-2ibd2)=e^(-2ib(d1+d2)); zero displacement = identity; the map inverts to Z). A control (displacements multiplying instead of adding) MISMATCHED. The same closure is the abstract form of DNA's conserved code, of words and peoples that keep their identity, and of a code faithfully expressed across a gap.

title: The things that hold together when they could fall apart are the ones that carry meaning
verification: Notice what lasts. A word stays a word when its sounds could scatter into noise; a living thing keeps its DNA's message faithfully across countless copies; a people keeps its identity when it would be easier to dissolve; even a clock's hours, added up, always land back among the hours. Mathematicians call this 'closure' -- combining the parts never breaks the whole apart. The things that hold together are exactly the things that carry meaning, life, and memory forward. The engine confirmed the holding-together of several such 'groups', including the very transformations of the Smith chart.
wisdom: For all flesh is as grass...The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away: but the word of the Lord endureth for ever (1 Peter 1:24-25). The coherent thing endures while the loose and scattered passes; hold fast, then, to what is built to hold.
